Much happier with this race than last weeks. Started out MUCH more conservatively, with more success. Hard to compare times from course to course, but I felt so much better and more in control. Took 5th on the team to complete the 1-5 sweep, which was awesome, but I must admit I much prefer being the upstart freshman than being beat by them. But it sure looks promising. Just need to find the confidence in the work I've put in and know I'm training for November. Splits: 6:35, 6:30, 7:05
half of each

strong run. 1' pickups every 8' or so, pretty hard pickups too, not just to shake it up a bit like usual. 7:53 was the cumulative pace. ate some dried apricots after lunch, which never sits well with me, and I was uncomfortable a lot of the way, but I managed to ignore it. just Karen and I today.

Forgot my watch, but it didn't matter since HBH had a time on everything. 5x1200 at Fenway, with 2x100m surges. Walking around during the day, my legs were shot and I knew today was going to be bad. Maybe it was the lifting, or the long run, or poor eating, I dunno. The afternoon lived up to my expectations, as I barely made my way through the workout. The last one I started half a lap late so that I could get a little more recovery, then added the half lap at the end. That actually helped a lot as I stuck with Karen and Julia for about 600m. 4:42, 4:42, 4:47, ?, ?

ran around campus, then hopped in the pool for some aquajogging. I think I need to focus a little more and pay attention to the details again. My eating habits have been slightly sporadic, although I am getting back to a normal sleep schedule. I don't know, I don't feel on track. Starting now though, it's only positive thoughts.

Such a blast! Woke up to absolutely pouring rain and arrived to a flooded course. Like state my junior year, but more so. And way better because I actually raced. Had a good start, got some position, but then when I let the field come back to me I might have eased up too much. Was really aggressive in the muddy and windy conditions, I'm proud of that. Got distracted by the tape coming off my shoe, then slipped sidelong into the mud around a turn just before the two mile mark, but that in total was probably only 3-4 seconds and a little loss of rhythm, maybe 2-3 places overall. I finished the race well, I think the last half of the race I only passed people, didn't get passed by more than one person. Coming off of the asphault HBH told me to "pick 5"; I got 12-15 people from that point. Maybe an indication that I was a little conservative in my start, but I didn't really have the feel for how hard the rain might make it. Maybe I could have hung with Karen and Kristen if I tried to from the start, but I let them get away early. I can't really decide if my season is starting off well or not, because different courses and variables have made time comparisons hard, and I can't tell really how I'm doing in relation to others. We'll see... Splits (aprox): 6:35, 7:00, 7:01 (6:26 pace)
drove to the Blue Hills Reservation. Hillier than Boston (in a good way) and slightly loose footing in parts, but it was nice. Cool today

counting the continuous warmup and 6x100m strides for the extra 1.5. Don't have a race for three weeks; I'm looking forward to pounding out some solid workouts. Just need to keep my confidence up; like Carolyn said, I did the summer work, and I'm fit-- if nothing else, I'm at least maintaining that.
tempo on the esplande. ran w/Jen & Liz and group. 7:03, 7:05, 6:44, plus whatever was left Didn't feel bad at all
recovery
5x(short loop, 2 min walk, 1 hill, jog down) at Boston Common. Actually felt pretty good. Managed to maintain/increase the effort throughout: 4:30, :30, :27, :27, :25. Pleased with today.

around Jamaica pond... I swear the dirt route must be longer, because I used to think that was 8, but the past couple of times it's take me around 70 min, so it can't be... anyway, just running in the AM since I'm not racing tomorrow; fasting for Yom Kippur
a lot of stops made the run feel sort of easy, but i'm sure it was a workout regardless. drove to the arboretum and ran around there and to franklin park.
sci museum-bu
rain pushed the workout to tomorrow, so we ran a bridge loop, and added on around campus (got kicked off briggs).

5 hills at Boston Common. New loop: from the corner of the baseball field, up, down, up to the monument, and down. Less than 800m each. The coaches decided to move the start back after the first two, so each one got longer but the last three were all the same. Felt awesome today! Could be just the confidence because there was no one in front of me, but I felt really strong on the hills, which I never have in the past. Either my legs are finally coming around from the summer workouts, or not having an elevator in the dorm is paying off. 2:56, 2:53, 2:55, 2:47, 2:57 #4 was a simulated "race start" with everyone going out hard, 5 was all by myself
Didn't really want to run less, but we both have to give a little
extras: 5x100m strides, aquajogged in the morning as per HBH's instructions. 40 min with drills.

comments: Hot damn! Finally, a good week of workouts. Today was huge, mentally if nothing else. Went to Franklin Park and did the course in 5x1000m segments, with 2:30 rest, plus Bear Cage Hill backwards once. Man, I really feel like I tore that one up. There were definitely those moments, but I didn't wimp out and conquered them. Can't wait until everyone else recovers and freshens up... oh so excited. Here are the times, but I don't think they were all 1000's. 4:00, 3:51, 4:05, 4:27, 2:58, (3:07). Adds up to a 19:26 5k Definitely though, I noticed that I need longer warmups
warmed up around campus, then to around Fresh Pond. Solo run, a little cold and not inclined to run too fast, but it was good. Followed by Dim Sum!

Everything according to plan. 5x1000 +2x400, 2 min rest at Fenway. My goal times were 3:50, 80, with special plans on each interval (pace, fast start, surge, fast finish, pace). With Kristen leading, we exceeded almost all of them: 3:45, 3:46, 3:49, 3:45, 3:52, 78, 81. The rests were around 10 seconds long but I don't think that made things significantly easier.
very stiff from yesterday. slowly to bunker hill w/jen, back for some strides and a 15 min aqua jog
10 min, drills and 8 strides, 10 more min

Effort and confidence-wise, it felt great. My first mile was faster than it had been all season, I was with Jen and Liz until Bear Cage Hill, and from there on pretty much picked off people for the entire last half of the race. I "backed off" appropriately on parts where I was weaker (uphills) but made sure I passed more people back where I was stronger (downhills). Didn't let myself get defeated by that stretch right between the 2mile and the wilderness, which usually kills me. Even managed to kick the last 100m and pass two more girls after barely holding my own during the last lap around the field. All in all, it was a strategically and mentally perfect race... just really slow. I just don't understand, 45 seconds off of last year's time... that's just not right. And the same time as the alumni meet, where I felt so much worse. And I was so set for today too. Well, Coach Thorne always used to say it take 21 days for a workout to get into your legs for racing, and two weeks ago I had some good workouts. So maybe that means next week... Splits (aprox): 6:15, ??

swam for 30+ min, including a few hard lap/2lap sets. kicked a bit for strength, then finished with some aquajogging. I think I might not do the half tomorrow; my left foot has been hurting for the past week and last night walking around Boston it hurt a lot more. 13 miles on pavement may not be a good idea.
to Concord and back on the Minuteman Trail. Beautiful day out
started at the Longwood T stop and ran out the Emerald Necklace w/dad, just showing him around. Went around Jamaica Pond and into the Arboretum. Got sick later in the day

still feeling not up to par, so I took it real slow w/Jen and Liz, who were recovering from a hard weekend. Went harvard/sci museum. My foot was in excruciating pain on the way back, about halfway through the loop. It was even sore last night going to bed, so I decided to finally head to the trainers. No conclusion there, except that there is some unknown lump (which I could have told them). Recommended I alternate days of cross-training and just lighten the load on my foot to see if anything changes (which I could have decided). The tuning fork test elicited no response, so it's not likely a stress fracture. I'm still a little scared.

swam for 45 min with some hard sets of laps in the middle. 45 min is a long time... took a bathroom break and when I came back the swim team was done w/practice so I switched over to the big pool for some aquajogging (and a little relief in temperature). Foot's still sore, but it's not getting worse. Last night I remembered that a 2x4 fell on my foot a few weeks back; maybe that put something out of whack that's just amplified with all the running. Who knows. I'm OK'd to run "light" tomorrow.

I have never ellipticalled before. That's some lame-o stuff. Ran a mile on the track, afterwards, striding the straights. Had the trainers put a donut on my foot to keep the pressure off the lump, and it felt good. Planning to race Saturday.
swam 10 min warmup, jogged 35 min, swam 5 min

Argh! There is no good reason that people should get slower from year to year. This blows. I almost hesistate to write something so negative for fear of planting bad thoughts into my head, but hell, they're already in there so what harm can it do? Today was crap. Every mile felt progressively worse. I hung with Kristen for 2 miles as opposed to wimping out early, which is more than I've been doing, but I should have been up there the whole time. 41 seconds slower than last year. Yeah, on the one hand you can say time is meaningless in cross country, because every race is different and you're out there to race people, but then again... last year I was 26th as opposed to 42 today. Kristen was 24. I should have been right fucking there. There are a lot of things I could say, excuses I could make. "If only I didn't have a cold for the first half of the week, yadda yadda..." that's no good. You don't win races by predicting what would happen under ideal conditions. You either perform, or you don't. At NEWMACs and regionals, if I don't perform, that's it, that's the result, no hypothetical situations. I have to take what I have that day, and just fucking race, and that's what the team has to do to. I know everyone has a bad season from time to time. I can't expect to improve eight years in a row. But I am simply not pulling. Geez, if I just had one sign to give me some confidence and tell me I can do this...
out the minuteman trail again, with Bryan
to Franklin Park and back

800 race pace, then 10 laps running the straights at a target pace that got progressively faster. Same as last spring . I don't remember the times, but they were around 23 for the first 4-5 laps, then faster for each set of two, coming down to 19-20. HBH's starting times were just way slow, but we hit the end times so I guess it worked out. Not a very taxing workout. *Went to the doctor today, got x-rays. There's no break; it's probably just my foot trying to heal but being aggravated. He said I can't make it any worse, it'll just take longer to get better, so it's ok to run within my own pain limits. The plan is to run workouts and races, and cross train on all the recovery days. Hope this freshens me up or something.
wu, drills, 10 min swim cooldown
aqua jogged in the AM, then hit the track for two laps of ins and outs, and an 800 (3:04) , just for my own confidence

went to the Armory Playground in Brookline, which ended up being only about a half-mile around... was actually not as boring as we anticipated. Julia went back early w/back problems and the rest formed their own group so it was just Kristen and I. Aside from Kristen rocking me on all the ones she led, it was not a bad workout. I relived my love-hate relationship with Mexican food after eating lunch only 2 hours before, but that only set me back a little. Foot is sore after running on it
foot was really sore after yesterday

Fenway track: 1x1600, 3x800, 1x300. Original workout called for 4x800, but I have a feeling HBH thinks we were working too hard because we went too fast. Anyway, the 800's were supposed to be tempo with the last 300 in a target of 63, I think. I ran: 6:13, 3:05 (65 300), 3:06 (63), 3:04 (62), and a 59. Times were what was set out for me, but I was really just going through the motions. Seems to be the theme of this season. :-(
swam 10' wu, aquajogged with drills. foot didn't hurt after yesterday, yay

200-300-400-500-400-300-200 on the track, plus 1600. 100m walk in between, but it ended up being close to 2 min. goal times: 41, 62-63, 83-85, 1:46-8, and back down, 6:24. actual: 39, 63, 86, 1:46, 82, 61, 39, 6:19. Pretty stoked about this one, a little tight after icing, but no pains really. Starting to be pretty stoked about Saturday
wanted to run to loosen up because I think aquajogging is making me sore. Tightened up a whole bunch last night.
still really tight. just a pre-meet thing

I'm pretty bewildered. I don't know what to think. All of my muscles were screaming, everything hurt and I wasn't in control of my body. I fell to 13th on the team, there's no justifying that. I just don't have the heart to race.
Talked to HBH about things. Afterwards he told me to go home and forget about practice today, which actually sounded like a really good idea. So I did.

Jamaica Pond, back via Mass Ave bridge, plus grassloops. HBH sent us out on our own in the AM to enjoy the daylight, so I left on my own but Julia happened to catch up with me. Good run, no pain, just a lot of sore muscles from goofing off. Happy to do a long run again.

~30 min of fartlek on the esplanade (12:01 to the head, 10:36 coming back), with the three-thirds format. instructions were to do shorter bursts (200-400m). Fell back on most of the ones that I didn't lead but I think it was a good workout, other than the dark.

A zippy science-river loop (Julia and Erica together are nuts) plus two laps around the turf to add strides. feels great to be running full workouts and not cross training. funny though, seems like the mileage assignments are higher than during the regular season

at fenway: 1x1600, 4x600. Goals: 6:15, 2:12. Recovery was walk the diagonals, which ended up being around 2:10-2:15. pretty windy today; HBH had us start in the back corner so we got to finish with the wind, but it was still pretty intense. 6:20, 2:14, 2:15, 2:15, 2:12. Feeling really good about my mental control over my body, I worked just a little past where I wanted to be working.
easy run down the river, since i took monday off
wow, the run felt sluggish and my legs were tight, but when I looked up the bridge loop (Elliot-Longfellow) I found out that I actually ran futher (and faster) than I thought. Nice...

same as last week, warm up to the Fielder head, 30' of fartlek. This week we were told to keep them between 200-300m. Was feeling awesome on this workout, not getting dropped, until the 3rd to last one, when my lower back/IT band tightened up. Stuck out that one but only did half of the next surge, then passed on the last. Fell back on the cool down. Don't think it was anything lasting, just not enough stretching on a cool day.

5x400, 1x1600 on the track, 100m walk recovery (~95 s). Goal times were 88 and 6:15, with instructions for the mile to be slow for the first 3 laps, and go on the last one. Workout was 88, 87, 87, 88, 87, 6:05, definitely going on the last lap. felt like i didn't work too hard.

Hey, well, it didn't suck. Satisfied with the finish. Still not hitting last year's times, but oh well. I put in a good effort. Last 1.5 mi a girl from Wellesley kept talking to me and encouraging me, which was absolutely awesome. Christine Grant... without her I might have packed it in toward the end. After I realized that she was saying good job to me, I tried to reciprocate the favor and we passed people together until the last 600 m where I had no more gas. Finished just over a second behind her. Good to have something pushing me in the race. And congratulations to Julia, 29th place for All-New England Region honors. Following last week's performance, I could not think of a better way to end a cross country career.
race_name: NCAA D3 NE Regionals, Cumberland, ME

comments: Went to Betsy's house in Poughkeepsie for Thanksgiving weekend. She took Jordan and me on a great run through the Vassar College farms. Nice wooded bike trail--we saw a couple of deer and wild turkeys, pretty crazy. Definitely feeling the effects of two weeks of not quite taking care of myself, but I'm not regretting it. Skipping indoor, so I'm going to begin my campaign for a crazy track season when I get home for winter break.
longfellow-BU. oof.
Happy Birthday Mom
extras: 4 short strides, lunges, 2 sets sp, lifting

comments: explored around Brookline; ended up running up Summit Ave
to Charlestown, down to the shipyard, wandernig
ran longfellow-harvard with Mike and his friend from HS

went to practice today, just for company. did 30+ min of continuous warmup, then a mile tempo, then cooldown to longfellow and back. everything but the cooldown was inside, and when we got outside, it was snowing! yay! I don't know if I ran faster on the cooldown or if i'm just out of shape, but I felt like the I got a bit overexcited by the snow and had a little extra bounce in my step.
to JFK bridge and back, starting from MacGregor... seems fast but I think it was, I was a little excited...
extras: lunges, 4 short strides, some core strengthening

comments: my toe is a little sore still, so I didn't really feel like running, and I figured there was no reason in forcing it. lifted at the z-center for about 50 min
perfect running weather. went along the emerald necklace

Another day of perfect running weather. Can't believe I'm wearing shorts and a t-shirt in December, in Boston. This was the longest run I've done in a while. Went exploring again--out Beacon to the Chestnut Hill Reservior. Just over 9 miles including going around the reservior, according to Microsoft Streets. Nice run, although I could do without running on the sidewalk all the way there. Haha, on the way back though, I passed by this path marked "Summit Path" and thought, "oh, hey there's a Summit Path at the top of Summit Ave, I wonder if this connects." And of course it did, and of course the other end of the path is at the top of a fatty hill, and of course I climbed a lot of stairs to get there. Oh well
extras: sp, included strides at the end

comments: I need new shoes. lifted for a bit to get some activity after my two finals

holy crap! 7:30 miles? That was definitely not on purpose... I think running with other people (who are still 'training') makes me feel like I'd better not drag them down... ran with Nancy again today (sci museum-bu) and while it felt like an effort, I was still able to chat. Also I think the cold inspires me to move a little faster to stay warm or get back faster or whatever-- temperatures dropped to below freezing, and a mild wind
just killing time before my last final...
held off until the evening so I could run w/detti and J and say goodbye... ran in the dark around the Harvard-footbridge loop. The run felt a little more taxing than I thought it should, but oh well
extras: Myriam showed us a bunch of distance-tailored lifts to do over break-- if I can do these w/o slacking off I should be pretty built by the end of IAP. She told me I could do them 4 days/wk instead of 2 since I'm not racing.

comments: SO EXCITED TO BE HOME! Despite waking up at 4:30 a.m. EST to catch my flight, and not quite making up the sleep on the plane, this was one of the happiest runs ever. Drove down to TP state beach w/my parents. Dad and I were both going north on the cliffs but I was so amped that I just took off right away and ran alone. Hit the gate at Powerhouse park in a breezy 14:05, and was to the 3 mile mark in around 23:30. Held up on the way back too, 24:54 out and 25:08 back. Man, I can't describe how wonderful San Diego makes me feel. Here's why my run was awesome: 1. Santa Ana winds made it a balmy 68° and I got to run in shorts and a sportsbra 2. People were driving by in convertibles with the tops down. 3. A train that went by was playing jingle bells with it's horn! The doppler effect made that pretty interesting... 4. The sun setting over the beach was flawless as usual.
Up Broken Hills, down including Guy Fleming. Didn't work especially hard, 12:30ish up the hill
at mammoth. not too bad feeling, no snow on the ground. Probably went a bit slow (esp. on the hills) but my heart working in the altitude
extras: don't have the equipment really to do myriam's lifts, so I picked and chose-- I'll start next week. Did hip abductions, supine hip lift, king dead lift, side hip lift, thin tummy, sp, lunges

comments: a little more acclimated then yesterday, but still a slow

last run in Mammoth before the drive down. Made it to the high school but running up here is still a fight. taking off yesterday cut this week down to a five day week but I'm in the offseason so it's no biggie, it just wasn't practical to run. but I had a crazy good day of boarding yesterday-- stuck a couple of new tricks

home again. still remnants of the santa ana so it was pretty warm and dry. bike path and up el camino, then up townsgate to check out the new playground by the new elementary school. Coolest playground ever!! Stopped for about 10min there. Pace was good though: 46:23 for the loop (ending at the top of graydon), and under 8 min for the measure mile on the bike path. After the loop I ran over to the school for extras
extras: played around with the hurdles for 20-30 min. gonna call coach and see if there's practice in Jan. Medium's "B" day-- I'll post the workouts later

comments: TP state beach, south up Park Rd, past UCSD. Ran into Julie on the way back, she turned around with me and we ran together. Probably picked up both of our paces a bit. Added North Fork-South Fork and Guy Fleming on the way down. Developed a few blisters in the new shoes which wasn't so fun.
TP state beach to Guy Fleming and back north to Powerhouse. Surprised myself with the pace, even with the park rd. hill
extras: "A" day, minus the squats and lat-pulldowns

comments: just around the neighborhood

In Park City visiting Kristina. I was trying to avoid ever taking off two days in a row this winter but flying and staying with other people made it impractical to get out the past couple of days. Woke up at 6 to drop Kristina off at work, came back and napped until it got light out, headed out around 7:45. No problems with the altitude, but it's lower here than Mammoth, maybe just under 7000 ft. It's been snowing since I got here but the roads were plowed.

AAHHH loving the crazy pow pow! Haha not that that has anything to do with running, but it's been snowing since I got here and the snowboarding has been insane! We didn't ride today though, Kristina was tired so I ran while she slept in. Used the snow-shoe-tread things that Dad got me for Hanukah, since it dumped a few more feet last night and the plows hadn't gotten out yet. They work really well, I didn't slip at all.

Last run in Park City, went around 4:30 after a full day of boarding. I'm wiped; the little neighborhood hills were eating me alive and I didn't even have the energy to do my strength work. Showered and ate dinner after my run and then crashed for a nap at 7:30 p.m. Probably could have slept until the next day but I had to get Kristina from work.

Around the neighborhood to get some hills in. I'm so sad that Black Mountain Rd. is dead. Not that it's a new thing; it's been inaccessible since just before I graduated, but today I tried to at least find another way in but it's actually physically gone... so sad.

a bit longer than planned, but oh well. Wandered through Carmel Valley again, ran two tempo miles on the bike path towards the end because I got bored, 6:54, 7:03 with just under 3 min of jogging in between.

felt pretty sluggish, but I knew I was going to because the last two days were pretty hard. hit my knee on the jersey rail at the end of the Dog Beach bridge (3 mi into the run) and felt it the whole rest of the run. It's still sore and stiff, hmm... Thoughts of the day: - Don't go for a long run with no sunscreen and wearing a sweattie while you're not already tan. - The Brown men's team does not require their California runners to come back early for track, because they train better out here. :D
extras: strides built in at end of the run

comments: continued to rain all day, but not really hard. Went north to 25th st. with Brenna, was pretty windy on the way back but a nice run overall

started a run at San Dieguito Park in the morning on the way back from errands, then remembered I told Brenna I'd run with her. Decided that a double wouldn't kill me an cut the run short. Worked out for the better, because the lagoon was flooded and I don't think I could have handled running more than maybe 40 minutes within the park itself

Deanna called while I was in the car on my way to run to see if I wanted to run with her, so we ended up going together. planning on going around an hour relaxed, up park rd, but deanna wanted to go around 70 and had never been up broken hills so I thought I'd take her. It was pretty muddy and slippery going up but we still managed to do it in around twevle and a half min. Turned around at scripps hospital and hit guy fleming on the way down. either pride or fear of seeming too slow for each other probably made both of us work a little harder, but it was good. Capped off the end of my first real week of mileage in a long time, which felt really awesome.
extras: 4 longer strides... skipped my second 'A' day of the week since we worked pretty hard on the run

comments: pretty sluggish. went down to the bike path for an easier run, because I realized I've had significant hills in every run since I've been home, except last week's long run. Got a shoe sucked off in the mud, but I managed to hop on the other foot long enough to keep my socks clean. On a normal week, I probably would have taken today off, but I'm planning on snowboarding thursday so I'll just keep today and tomorrow easy.
somehow I am sleeping 9 hours a night and am still tired. oh well, got plenty of time to rest up

Stopped raining last night and was bright and beautiful when I headed out. Good thing too, I thought I might grow mold or something. parked at Fletcher Cove and ran north to "Grandview St" or something like that, past Leucadia Blvd. Good run, out in 55 or so and back a few minutes faster, thanks to some pickups I threw in
Gone boarding...
Got dropped off at the bike path. To El Camino and back the other way to Carmel Valley Rd, then home
Broken Hills was closed, turned around and ran up Park Rd. to UCSD

back in Boston. Tights, woohoo.... it's like... cold here. oh well. a bit of snow in spots. Went around Jamaica Pond, to the Arboretum. Ran around there for a bit and ended up coming out a wrong entrance, so the run ended up being longer than I meant it, but it was pretty
science museum-footbridge. getting colder
dropped by practice today. science museum-BU with the team.

The outside of my foot is hurting; something I started feeling around Wednesday. Aquajogged then swam 10 lengths. Left my cap and goggles at home though. Kind of intended to go closer to an hour but I was bored out of my mind.

Fresh pond twice around. I ended up a bit underdressed somehow; it wasn't that cold out but my arms and hands were seriously cold, especially my defective left hand. Foot started hurting a bit about halfway through. *Switched tomorrow's long run to today because it's supposed to snow pretty crazy tonight.

My foot hurt after the run Saturday and still hurt on Sunday, so I decided to take the day off (and let the blizzard win its war, dang). It felt a little better this morning after the rest, but I figure I have plenty of time to make up the training so I'm going to keep off it for the next couple of days. Feels like a tendon type strain, maybe I slipped on the snow or ice or something. Warmed up and cooled down with swimming laps Julia showed up part way through, so at least I had company.

Woke up early to workout before class. It was nice to have everything done by 9, but man, that was early. Warmed up with swimming, jogged a bit, HBH's drills, some 'sprints', swam to cool down. Time went quickly today, yay. And the foot is feeling better with the rest. I'll go a couple more days to be sure.

the Z-center closed at noon today, along with the rest of MIT... for a snow day! Craziness! In any case, it meant that the pool was not an option, so I ellipticalled downstairs in the dorm's cardio room. Brought down the iPod and listened to Rancid while I did "weight loss 2" and "cross training 2" in succession. Somehow, it managed to be not boring. Meanwhile, since I wasn't going anywhere, I tried to figure out how close ellipticalling was to running. According to the machine, I ran around 15-16k strides in that hour. Now, running, they say you get close to 200 strides/min, which is just under 16000 strides in an hour. So that's close. But the HR doesn't get nearly as high; I was around 130 for most of it. I can get it closer to running if I crank up the resitance, but then I can't do it for very long. So I dunno. No conclusions really, just thinking. Can wait to start running again. I'll be patient though.
took the day off. with class 9-4 and a bunch of stuff to do in the evening, I just didn't have the energy to squeeze a workout in

worked out before class again. something about getting out when it's still dark really makes you feel like a badass. anyway, ellipticalled for 20 min then went down to the pool and aquajogged, with a few hard intervals mixed in. didn't have time to do strength though, with 2.670 going all day. but my engine is so pretty!
alternated swimming/aquajogging. gonna run tomorrow regardless, i'm bored as heck

that felt great. stretches of unplowed snow slowed me down, but who cares. my plan to go sci musuem-river were thwarted when the path past the harvard bridge was entirely unrunnable, but i ran into boston toward the fens and it was all good. felt the foot a little, but no pain, so i'm going to keep running

headed towards the reservior thinking that would be a mostly plowed route. everything was clear except the reservior itself. tried to run around it for a bit, but it got too snowy just over halfway around, so I hopped into the street and ran around Boston College. Went up Summit Ave on the way back; I feel so much stronger than before break

I guess last week was a good week (if there ever are any) to be cross training--third day in a row that it was above freezing when I ran, yay. out toward bunker hill, then followed the freedom trail to boston common. ran around the commons for a bit, then back on the esplanade. thinking of racing the home meet in two weeks
extras: hip abductions, U supine hip lift, side hip lift, thin tummy (from 'A')

comments: Wanted to see how fit I was, to see if I would embarass myself by racing next weekend, so I made up my own workout: 3x1200, 400, 300 on the indoor track, with just under 3 min recovery. Was shooting for 94's on the 1200's, but if I fell off pace I would just stop. Made it through all three: 4:43, 43, 41. 86, 63 for the 4 and 3. Pretty pleased with how it went. Wasn't working out under ideal conditions; it was a little close to lunch and my legs were a bit tired on the warmup.
extras: lat pulldowns, incline dumbell for yesterday (wu/cd split 20'/20')

comments: arboretum
woke up early to run before class and leaving for NYC. Super sluggish, maybe because I wasn't awake or fed. Shooting for 7.5 or so, but after I got to Elliot bridge I realized I was going nowhere

got sick over the weekend visiting the cousins. No fever, and I had already promised to go to practice, so after an afternoon nap and some Tylenol cold I decided that I could manage a short run. Wasn't too bad, still would like to race saturday.
taking it easy still. too bad continuous warmup killed me

legs still feeling shot from yesterday. almost didn't want to run, but after doing 8 slow warmup minutes inside, i felt good enough to go out for a longfellow loop. giving up on the mileage this week though
some warmup inside, continuous warmup, and a longfellow loop again.
took the prefrosh down Comm. Ave to Boston Common and back on the esplanade

My first collegiate victory...hot damn! Granted, it was a slow race without much competition, but seriously, today was exactly what I needed... I'm ready to roll and so excited for outdoor! Everything about today was great--I raced unattached in the old TP jersey which made me feel all goofy and relaxed, and then when I put on my spikes... oh man that just felt great! Strategically, the race played out perfectly too. I shared the fastest seed time with another girl, so I knew I was in the mix, and without any time goals, I just figured I'd chill until the last three laps and see what I had. I relaxed into 4th early on, but by the end of the first mile I had moved into second without any effort. A lap later, I started moving into the lead, not really on purpose. I hesitated to make a move so early, but HBH yelled "If you're going to go, make it decisive!" and that did it. I decided that if I was going to pass her I had to make her feel it, so I pulled away a few more steps and just repeated that phrase in my head for the next few laps. With three to go I picked it up a bit more and finished with the win.
10xs pavement at boston common. ran the first 4 a bit behind kristen and erica, who were doing only 4. ran the next 3 at a harder effort, then harder on the last 3.

river-sci museum loop. 20 min tempo from BU to halfway across the sci museum bridge, i'm guessing around 3 miles which puts me at a good 6:40 pace. had to run alone since everyone's tapering for the end of indoor, and it was a little hard to push myself that hard for that long, but I was running faster than it felt. good run *Edit: that stretch is actually just over 2.8 mi, so I was running just over 7 min pace. That's more like what it felt
arboretum, w/1min pickups every 10. to the gate in 38 min.

no school today; ran in the AM with erika. It was snowing when we left and there was already a couple inches on the ground. went out on mass ave and the footing was ok, but coming back along the river was super tiring. I had the yaktraks though, which actually helped a lot
same as last week, but much better. really worked the last 3
decided i'm going to trust in HBH's schedule more, so today was pool. warmed up, drills, harder intervals of 1:30, 2:00, 3:00, 2:00 w/30 seconds rest, then swam for 10 min.

sci-footbridge. reverse direction from last week's tempo, but did the same stretch of the esplanade. started w/kristen, but 5 min or so into the tempo her stomach started hurting and she told me to go on. Apparently she felt better soon after and spent the rest of the run trying to catch me, but I thought I was alone from there on, so I just tried to push myself and go after all the joggers. Felt really good, well focused today and I was able to keep up the turnover. Went farther than last week, maybe just shy of 3 miles, but I think the wind was at my back.

started off slow, a little tired from hurdle drills, but then got moving. Not running tomorrow because it's not on the schedule, which I'm going to stick too as much as I can from now on. Over 40 miles in only 5 actual running days though is pretty good.
extras: warmed up four indoor laps then did hurdle drills before the run, about 20 min worth. all the stretches plus step overs and trail leg. lifted after

comments: Harvard-N.Beacon bridge loop with 1' pickups every 10', plus a warmup around campus. 90:13 for the bridge loop, just under 8:10 pace
warmed up 5' before doing steeple drills. ran to jamaica pond w/julia, but not around. pretty good pace

same as last two weeks. had betsy for company on #2-7. Had a little trouble making my last set of three a harder effort, but I think I worked the first 7 more than usual since I had someone to run with. all in all a good day, I'm pretty beat now. going to bed early tonight, haven't had quite as much sleep as I would like since I've been back at MIT. averaging 7 hours a night, when races get underway I'm really going to make an effort to get more.
Forgot to write down on Monday that HBH and I were discussing steeple goals and he thinks I can run 11:20 this year. NCAA provisional is 11:21. I think I can do that too. I'm going for it.
extras: continuous warmup. not as draining as usual.

comments: esplanade was too icy, so we planned on going out and back on the cambridge side of the river. erika was supposed to do 15 minutes tempo, so we went out 17 and turned around. she dropped back early on though, I guess locked up in the cold, so I was on my own again. After I got back to Mass Ave I turned across the bridge and ran toward Fenway track. Made it to halfway around the park area in about 8 min. Hard to judge the effort, I think parts of it were really good but overall maybe not my best. substantial headwind coming along the river though.
extras: half mile wu then steeple drills (just stretches) before the run, lifting after

comments: aquajogged (including drills) for the first 50 min, then swam some laps to finish up.
extras: half mile wu then steeple drills, including walkovers. at HBH's suggestion, I also did some runs stepping on the barrier onto the high jump mats. Definitely need to work on getting my left leg over in both cases.

comments: out beacon towards BC, plus summit hill on the way back. despite the run being almost all sidewalk I really like it for some reason.

3x1600 w/400 recovery. Goals-- 6:30, 6:20, 6:10. Hit 6:25, 6:17, 6:10. Rest ended up being about 3 min. Nancy led the first, me second, then we split the third. Had a few others in the mix but we were pacing. First two Swere on solid, but I took the third out too hard (3:01) and really felt it hard in my legs when Nancy took over and dropped back a few seconds

did continuous warmup and steeple drills before running, wasn't done until 6:30. Everyone that had come in at that point said it was hailing pretty badly and pretty painful so I conceded and did laps around the indoor track. I'm ok with letting days like today be low mileage since we did so much other stuff.
extras: cwu, steeple drills (incl. stepovers and barriers. should probably try hurdling soon)

comments: the workout (hills at Boston Common) got switched to an indoor one on account of ice and high winds. While it probably would have been ok to run outside, I agree that a workout would have been difficult. 2x(100m fast, 100m recovery for 10 laps, with pairs of laps being at 24, 22, 21, 20, 19). Jen, Nancy and I hit pretty much everything: 24, 24, 22, 23, 21, 21, 20, 20, 19, 19 22, 22, 21, 21, 20, 21, 19, 20, 19, 18 8:29 total for the first 10, 8:42 for the second (slower jogs, I guess). Good to have training partners again
extras: 21:23 wu, 18:37 cd, new A day abs

comments: aerobically, probably a worthless workout. aquajogged for 45 min with jen and nancy, mostly just talking the whole time. at least it woke me up a little, i've been really tired for most of the last two weeks. can't manage to wake up feeling rested, even though I mostly get 7.5 hrs. it hasn't been affecting my workouts at all, as far as I can tell, but it makes my days really suck when I have work to do and just want to sleep. oh well, spring break is coming soon...
extras: started to lift and then scrapped it, i need to rest

comments: ran early. first normal, aerobic run all week. I was a little restless
extras: joined the team for half of cwu, then did steeple drills, went over a few hurdles

comments: Trudged through sci museum-elliot loop in the snrain.... man, it was gross out. It was just above freezing and snowing, meaning the snow melted every time it hit something. so i got soaked and cold. on the way back, the stretch from river to BU was way flooded, and my feet got uber cold and it was miserable. scrapped my plan to go to the track and hurdle a bit
extras: new "B" abs....going to actually learn a new set next week. oh well

comments: Today was gorgeous. Real glad that I ran YESTERDAY....
extras: warmed up, did drills, and hurdled, leading only with my left leg. I can get it over now, but it's far from pretty.

comments: 5x1000 in 3:51, 1 lap jog recovery, 1 lap walk after the third. 3:50, 3:50, 3:49, 3:47, 3:49 Can't believe I managed the workout. Was feeling exhausted after waking up at 6:45 to drive to Attleboro for my TI interview, and not getting back to rest at all before practice. I basically got back to Cambridge at 4, tossed my bike in the rental car, drove to Harvard Square to return it, and hauled ass back to practice in 15 min. My legs were still feeling the biking on the first interval, and a bit on the second. Finally I warmed up though, and by letting Gwen and Nancy lead and just focusing on sticking to them, managed finish the workout pretty strong.

lots of extra stuff today. I think with the time I need to put in for steepling, the training this season is going to be a lot of specific work, with not so many miles on the recovery days. only a 5.5 mile run, but I'm counting the warmup, continuous warmup, and other drills also. Just going to try to keep the weekend and long runs up, and put in a couple extra miles when I have time and am not tired, but not force it otherwise.
extras: steeple drills, 1/2 cwu. did the form drills first, then stepovers and other practice after the run. Sluggo came by and helped out toward the end; made some real progress on my trail leg. Hopefully I'll be able to catch him when he's free more, because I have a lot of issues to work on.

comments: finally had company for this workout! did the same 4-3-3 pattern, with a walk down after the 3. Julia did the first six with Gwen and I, and Gwen did all but #9 with me. I was a bit back on the first half but then felt stronger toward the end. good stuff
Wasn't supposed to run, but it's been so nice out, and I was just feeling good. Felt great during the run, was just moving smoothly and it was great

extras: steeple drills (incl step overs, trail leg), Spring "C" day

comments: arboretum. nice day out, was in a T-shirt and shorts. going home tomorrow w/Nancy and Julia! rounded out a solid week today, I'm feeling good, even though I ran two days that I wasn't supposed to.
TP state beach, north to Dahlia drive. Sun was out today, up in the 60's.... home is great.
3xMile on the bike path. We didn't get going until around 10, so it was pretty warm (70's). The window was 6:10-6:20 w/3' rest, but with the rolling hills that was tough. 6:16, 6:21, 6:18

extras: hiked up Coweles Mtn., 3 miles, ~1 hour

comments: (12+1)*200m hill. Workout said 10-12x250, but the hill was short. Julia, Nancy and I did 12 together (walking halfway down after 6) but toward the end they wanted to do two more to make up for the shortened hill, so I jogged around for one and joined them on their 14th. Overall pretty good workout, I'm feeling really fit.

up Broken Hills, out to the golf course, then South Fork/North Fork and Guy Fleming on the way down. It was high tide and we had to pick our way through the rocks a bit, but once we got up the trail it was nice. Another beautiful day, after some sprinkles yesterday. Going up Broken Hills felt great, I couldn't even believe. I clicked the watch going up the hill just to see, although I didn't push the pace. Made it up in a solid 12:23, without even feeling beat at the top. Can't wait to race

TP state beach north to Ki's. we put off the run until the afternoon because we were pretty tired, but then we all ended up being a quite full and a little uncomfortable the whole way... but things still went pretty smoothly. Capped off a solid week of training with Nancy and Julia--definitely no junk miles in there. Feeling great and I can't wait for next weekend.
sci museum-BU with Julia and Kinnaman. Light rain, but warm enough for shorts. Moving the whole way but the heart and lungs didn't feel it at all.
extras: steeple drills, walk overs, lead leg/trail leg running drills, then a few times over two hurdles. Feeling a bit more confident than before. Still much to be done. Spring "A" day

comments: 4x1200 with the first lap over 4 barriers, 400m jog recovery. Goal pace before the workout was 93/lap for a 4:40, but that wasn't really happening. I was a little tired from lifting and steeple drills yesterday, but put in a good effort. Would have liked it to feel a little better, just as a confidence boost, because I was way sloppy over the hurdles and tired on the flat laps. On the plus side, I went over with my left leg a couple of times when I had to, so I know I can do it. 4:45 (93 1st lap), 4:44 (94), 4:46 (95), 4:42 (94). Recovery was about 2:50 for all
feeling pretty tight/sore today, so I let Julia and Nancy take off and ran with Jen and Kinnaman (still at a good pace). Kept the run short to have time to do hurdles, which I did a lot of.
extras: steeple drills, walk overs, lead leg/trail leg running drills. Worked for probably 45 min on just my lead leg with Sluggo. Need to get the leg straight up and over, without kicking to the side. Started to get the hang of it towards the end, but it was 7:20 already so I had to go. I think I might have done a bit better if I wasn't so tight.

comments: 2 sets of 3x400, 1st set over hurdles, plus 1600 tempo pace. 200m recovery jog between the 4's and a 200m walk between the sets, although it was too cold to stop moving. Goal times were something like 88-92 for the hurdle laps, and 82-86 for the flat, and an ambiguous 6:40 or so for the mile. The surprise cool weather (I warmed up in pants but didn't have any tights for the workout) locked me up a bit, I think, I never really felt like I was in control or smooth. I've also been tight (from lifting or drills?) for the whole week. I actually hit the second hurdle on the first interval, taking both it and myself down. First time I've taken myself out hurdling--I guess there's nothing to be embarassed about, ha. Didn't hurt though and I haven't bruised yet, so yeah. Despite that I managed: 95, 90, 91; 84, 85, 86; 6:35

warmed up with a lap on the outdoor track and drills, then hit the pool for aquajogging. it's supposed to pour tomorrow, although it was beautiful today, so I'm preparing for the worst. still crazy tight in the hamstrings, I hope it's not cold

meet got rained out, which was a disappointment, although I guess I would have been discouraged if I raced in crappy conditions and ran a bad time. The substitute workout was a team-wide relay race planned by HBH so the teams would be balanced. We ran a "DMR" and a "SMR", which were actually 1200-800-1000-1600 and 600-800-1000-400. I ran the 1600 and 1000 legs with Betsy, Jackie, and Liz on my team. It was actually pretty fun, the teams were pretty fair for the first race; I got the baton a bit in front of Julia and managed to hold her off for a lap, and tried to hang on again while Kristen gained on me toward the end--but she eventually got me. Probably hurting a bit because my first lap was a little hard, but it wasn't as bad as I thought it might be, and it was a good experience because I'd probably be too wussy to go out like that in a race. Ran a 5:41 to Julia's 38 and Kristen's 32. The 1000 was about 20 minutes later, which was a little close, but I managed to get my team unlapped (by the sprinters no less! but they were fresh...) and worked myself pretty hard, but I was feeling the 16 and came in at 3:35. Overall a fun day, despite the letdown of the cancelled meet. Wrapped up an unusally abusive week of training, especially considering the low mileage (no long run this week, pool on Friday). Don't know if it was the lifting or the extra steeple drills or what, but I was beat by Saturday.

Between forgetting to set my alarm and forgetting the time change, I missed my morning meeting with Kinnaman so I opted to run at 5pm w/Julia. Despite being a little full (BC Iron Chef tasting at 3), and the run being pretty windy we had a great run at a good pace to Jamaica Pond, around and back, plus a lap around Briggs (in shorts and a tshirt!). What a contrast to yesterday (and all of last week). It felt great and I felt like I could go forever, but I decided not to add on and save the energy for the week.

2x800 over hurdles (3:12-3:16), 2x800 flat (3:00), 1600 tempo. One lap jog in between everything, walked around a bit before the 16. Felt great today. This was the confidence and control I wanted to feel last week before my first race, but I guess it worked out. Still very sloppy on the hurdles, but that's just time and practice. 3:07, 3:09; 3:04, 2:58; 6:27

62 degrees today! Yay! Did steeple drills outside for 45 minutes or so before hitting the pool for aquajogging. Just as I was intending to finish, Coach Terri started to comment on my form a bit, so I worked with him and Sluggo for another 20 min. Picked up a few more things to work on (getting the trail leg around, not jumping so high), and actually started to get it a little bit right today. Apparently I do everything right indvidually and in drills, just have to put it together. Sore/tight from lifting yesterday, but only mildly, not nearly as bad as last week.

Another solid, confidence building workout. Short in terms of mileage, but fast: 1200 tempo, 4x400 over hurdles (90-92?), 4x400 (81-82), plus water barrier practice. 100 walk between all, although we didn't quite take that every time (ended up <1:50 rest). 4:45; 88, 89, 90, 87; 80, 79, 79, 79 Stoked especially about the flat 4's, I haven't run that fast in a long time. Before the workout, I saw the goal times, and thought, "man, that's fast," and just got really pumped and decided that I was going to kill those today. Ran them a second back of Julia, just staring between her shoulders and trying to hang on.
extras: "B" lifting, a little bit of water jump practice (though I cut it short b/c my heel was tight)

comments: Julia wanted to do a longer run in anticipation of not being able to do one Sunday after the steeple/5k double, so I went with her. Started off feeling a little fast, I guess because I'm slow to warm up, but I stuck with it because I didn't want to leave Julia and I didn't want to run alone either. Settled into the pace a few miles in and it felt really good by the end.
3 laps around Briggs, drills, strides. super pumped for tomorrow

Can't complain about that double. Pulling out a 19:44 2 hours after running the steeple was not something I expected to be able to do. Best of all, it felt good . Counted the laps backwards and with 6 to go I acted like I was running repeat 800's. With 5 laps left, I just started moving , not intentionally or anything I was just suddenly going faster, and it felt really fluid. Unfortunately, my left shoe came untied with around 700 to go--entirely my fault, since it happened before in cross and I know that I need to triple knot my XC spikes (the other spikes were wet from the steeple). That cut into my kick a bit, especially since I had so much left it seemed. But now I know that I can run a solid time if I have to do the double at NEWMACs, and that's all that matters, it wasn't like I was going to PR anyway. The steeple was a little slower than I thought I was capable of, but most of it was a focus issue (30 second gaps either side of mide), and also not quite remembering how effort levels/times/fatigue perception correlate in that race (or any race). Slower than my first opener last year, but I also didn't steeple until two weeks later last year. The water jumps were solid, and the hurdles got better around the middle laps, but I still need to just put in the time practicing those. Fired up to go after the ECAC time (11:55.24) next weekend.
extras: 32:33 total wu/cd, about 11" before the steeple and 6" before the 5k. Kept all the extra mileage low as per Julia's suggestion

comments: elliot-harvard w/Julia. sore and tight from yesterday, and a little heavy, but not dead
aquajog incl drills to breakup the boredom

3xmile in the SNOW!! Yes, it snowed, on APRIL F---ING TWELFTH! But we were not to be deterred, and insisted that we do the workout outside, which was well worth it but took it's toll. We adjusted the workout to be entirely flat vs. 4th lap over hurdles, but the times ended being close to the original times (including the hurdles). 6:28, 6:26, 6:31, with 1 lap jog (~3") in between. Good effort, couldn't do anything about the conditions. A good mental conditioning workout too, trying to keep my head in the game on the last one
extras: "A" day, a little heavier than the last couple of weeks

comments: to Bunker Hill then the Charlestown docks with Gwen and Julia. Nice day out again, not like sunday though. Had an improving hurdle session, the right lead leg is coming along well, I still kick the left one out to the side. Found two things that help though: 1) imagining Sluggo's hand holding the hurdle and him saying "don't kick my hand" 2) thinking about pulling up the bent knee rather than thinking about going over without swinging--I think this gives me a more direct physical means to accomplish what I want.

Instructions were a "crisp" 30-36 minute run, followed by 6x200 (100 walk rest) at mile pace: 41,40,39,38,37,37. Ran Longfellow-BU with Julia for about 5 mi, about 7:15 pace, dropped back a few feet at the end but feeling good. Also felt nice to throw in some 2's finally, the last 2 felt rough but I hit all the times: 40, 40, 38, 36, 36, 36, about 1:20 rest each time

extras: drills, streched out on the hurdles

comments: Sunny skies, calm winds and a balmy high of 64° put me in a great mood before my race and set today up for some good running. Today's goal was to qualify for ECAC's (11:55), and I did! Mentally, today were some of the two strongest races of my life. There were more people around in the steeple so things went out a bit faster, but it was good because I hung on and realized that the start didn't really tire me out much. Technically speaking, I think it was one of my sloppier races, at least in the water pit. Fell into the first one, actually, it would be more accurate to say I "crumbled," because I landed on one foot in the right position and all, I just sort of continued moving toward the ground until I was on my hands and knees. There was second water jump later on where I stumbled a bit too, can't remember which one. The actual hurdles, as far as I can remember, were improving I think, I had some better than other and I was probably still swinging my lead leg around but I think it's getting smoother and less of a disruption to my running. The 4x800 went well also; I was on the A team w/Julia, Alisa, and Kristen, in that order plus me as 3rd leg. Great team race-- everyone put in their effort and did their job, and we came in second. Haven't run a relay in a while since I skipped indoor; it was a good feeling. Colby's 'A' team pretty much took the lead from the gun, but we battled their 'B' team the whole way. Alisa pulled ahead of their girl by a meter or so just before the handoff, so when I took the baton we were practically even. Thankfully Colby didn't take the first 100 out way hard (which usually happens in relays), and I think that helped me have more control over my body at the end. Tucked in right on her shoulder, a bit to the outside so I could keep contact, and hung on for the first 300. Started to get gapped at the end of lap one, but people were yelling at me and I kept telling myself that it was for the team and I had to do it. Down the first homestretch I stared Colby straight between the shoulders and suddenly I was right back on her. Sat again for the next 200, tucked in on the final turn, and with 150 to go I realized how strong I felt and made my move to pull away. Handed off to Kristen in second by maybe 4-5 meters, and she held our place. Awesome day, I feel like I'm stronger than I've ever been. Steeple splits: 86, 98, 96, 96, 99, 99, 99, 96, 43 *First lap--2 less barriers, 2nd lap I stumbled. no laps over 100 for the first time ever, and a nice close.
extras: 48:55 total wu/cd, about 15/14 for the steeple, 11/7 for the 800
race_name: State of Maine Meet, Colby College

comments: beautiful day out, yesterday's maine weather must have blown down here. around the BC reservior and up Summit

Wow, what a workout. Best part is, I finished it! The assignment: 2x800, 1st lap over hurdles (3:04); 4x400 (80, 79, 78, 77); 1600 tempo; 200 walk rest between the 8's, full recovery for everything else. The outcome: 3:04, 3:01, 77, 81, 78, 77, 6:26. The 400's were really the meat of the workout; I ran the first one too hard, keying off Julia whose goal times were faster than I realized. That made #2 hurt, but I recovered the rest, although #3 was way painful. Everyone was beat after today.

crashed early last night but I still had trouble waking up and was tired all day. Was tight starting the run, and struggling to keep up with Jen and Julia by the end, but apparently we were still moving pretty decently.
extras: steeple drills, lead leg/trail leg, strides over hurdles. My form is still not the greatest, but I am improving. I think I should practice the left leg more, because it's good enough but I'm still not confident enough to use it.

comments: 20:07 tempo, 200's in 38, 39, 38, 38, 37, 36 (Goal 40 39 38 37 36 35) Didn't really have anything in the legs today, even on the tempo and especially on the 200's. Tuesday's workout must have been intense.
extras: BU loop for the cooldown, B day

comments: Didn't really have my head in either race, so I don't regret the poor performances. Guess that's what happens when you develop a favorite event (and don't run it). The meet was non-scoring and I wasn't in any relays, I had a lot on my mind other than the race, plus it was raining, so basically I didn't care. All in all it added up to me not being very motivated. The 1500 was good in that I dug down a little deeper and eked out a kick over the last lap, moving up to 3rd. The 800 was really crappy, if my time didn't indicate that already. I warmed up way too early and my warmups were wet so I wasn't wearing pants, which made my legs lock up hideously on lap two. For part of it I just felt my left heel (the one that's been tight) freeze up and I was scared that I was going to incur some lasting damage, so I just slowed up and finished it off in poor form. Then I rushed in a 5 min cooldown and showered up for a Passover dinner that I was supposed to be at less than an hour after my event, hence the lack of mileage for the day. Dinner was good though, and it was nice to have a mental break for a week. Getting pumped for my badass double next week.

took it easy

2 miles "in's and out's" (straights fast, jog curves) with two laps each at 24, 22,20,19. Then 4x400 over hurdles, 86, 85, 84, 83 Ran 23, 22, 23, 22; 21, 22, 21, 22; 21, 20, 21, 19; 19, 18, 19, 18 Every odd one was into a rough headwind. 400's were: 91, 85, 82, 86 Totally erratic and not in control of my legs today. They're still super heavy and unmanageable from Saturday, I think. Going to hit the pool tomorrow. But at least I can run an 82 over hurdles...
Really beat up from the past couple of days and last week, so I decided to take today off. Was considering the pool but didn't sleep much last night, so I just hopped in the ice bath for 10 min

1x1200 (4:30), 3x400 (85), 1x1200 (4:55). HBH shortened my workout on account of my tired legs--the other steeplers did 1600, 4x400, 1600. Fine with me, the original workout seemed a little long for a tuneup anyway. I guess I'm not really supposed to be tapering yet, but I at least want to be fresh for NEWMACs. Times were 4:27, 82, 85, 86, 4:52, with full recovery in between, ~3min. Feeling much better than Monday. Just gotta remember how strong I've felt in all my races so far, and be confident that it's in me for Saturday.
Going well, feeling fresh again. Super pumped for NEWMACs, my new steeple spikes came today!
extras: a couple of water jumps, strides

comments: did my run in the morning between classes. Just a relaxed easy one along the river. did a very abbreviated version of hurdle drills, then over a couple times each leg to test out the new spikes.... Ohhh man they're hot!

Take 2 of the insane double and I killed it. PR in the steeple, outdoor PR in the 5k, only 5 seconds off my all-time best. It was raining slightly but that was really not a big factor; the temperature was perfect. I was seeded 2nd in the steeple, 4th in the 5k, and managed to get 3rd and 4th. A little bummed about not getting the 2nd place, but I don't think I could have done it today without killing myself for later on. Whalen of Wheaton got away from me early on... after she came by and elbowed me a lot for no reason, she really just took it out way hard-- we came through the 800 in 2:57 and she started to go after Julia and I realized that I had to ease up a bit if I didn't want to blow up later on. She wasn't gaining on me any after lap 5 or so, but when I ran by HBH he told me to either really go for it or just relax and save it for the 5k. I started to go but she had about 15 meters on me, and I started to get really tired and sloppy. She beat me by about 10 seconds, and my goal time actually was around 11:40, but like I said...I had three more miles to race after that. The 5k went really well. Didn't want to do any work until the last mile because of my previous race, so after getting quickly off the line I settled and let the #3 and 5 seeds catch up to me, and slowed it up until one of them got frustrated enough to take the lead. Went through the laps very evenly, the whole race I kept hearing other coaches yell 94, 95, 92, 94... about 6 laps in, the Smith girl (who was leading) dropped back and I was forced to go out in front again, with Coast Guard sitting on me. With about a mile to go she took off and left me in 4th, but I don't think that my leading affected the outcome much because she ended up closing in on Julia (who won both the steeple and 5k!) and finishing in under 19, which I don't think was going to happen at that point in the day. All in all, a great day--took a cumulative 40 seconds or so off of the same double from the first meet of the season, and even though I didn't get the Wheaton girl for the 4 point swing, I still had some great races and the team outcome (2nd place by 14 points or so) was still incredible. I'm really proud of a lot of my teammates: Nancy and Gwen in the 10k, Jen for her continuing streak of steeple PR's, Julia for the double conference champ, Leanne and Erika for super gutsy finishes, Meri for doing like a bajillion events... great day.

ran really slow down the river, came back and aquajogged. Not hurting too badly.

Jamaica Pond, around plus a little on the roads past the far end. started intending to run with everyone else, but I ran off as soon as we hit the river. Wasn't trying to run hard or fast or anything, but I just had a lot of restlesness in me and wanted to feel like I was moving. Julia was at some dinner so I just went on my own. It was good, thought to myself a bit about this whole thing with HBH and Sluggo leaving, which I don't care to elaborate on, and I thought about my races last weekend and next. Spent some time daydreaming about Div 3's, envisioning myself getting back the Wheaton girl and also qualifying for All-NE's. Pace probably picked up there for a while, but I eventually settled down and just moved very comfortably. I decided that I'm probably in the best shape of my entire life right now, and I can't let myself miss this opportunity to go after something I really want and is truly within reach. I'm just going to push the first mile and then start going after whoever's in front of me, regardless of how fast I started or how I feel. Plus, I found out today that my parents are going to be in town for the weekend of All-NE's, so I have to qualify so they can watch me steeple!
extras: 'B', going to stop doing 'A' for the rest of the season and lift only 2xs/wk

comments: sci museum-harvard and back to the track. instructions were to run "crisp" today; hung with Julia until about 3/4 of the way between longfellow and harvard. got to the base of the bridge in a little over 26 min, it's about 29 on a normal run.
extras: hurdle drills, including separate lead leg/trail leg

comments: the assignment was a ladder: 400, 800, 1000, 800, 400 with the 8 over hurdles--goals of 86, 2:56-3:00, and 89 sec pace, will walking full recovery. Worked a little too hard yesterday and was feeling pretty heavy, and nothing really went as planned. Ran 87, 3:02, 3:52, 3:04 until HBH cut it short, which is fine because the 400 was probably not going to be fast or useful anyway. Gotta freshen up for saturday

What a CRAZY meet! Drove up the night before and woke up in Lewiston to rain and temps just over 40. Then the race was delayed over 45 minutes while they tried to fix the timing system. Finally we were called back out, by then we were all wet and hungry and my feet had started to get really cold. But everyone was in the same position. The race started and I settled next to a girl about 10 ft. back from Julia and USM. We can through the first lap really slowly (90), everyone must have been cold. The only other split I heard was the mile in 6:10, which surprised me in those conditions. I had resigned to race for place (3rd, hopefully) considering the weather, but when I came through that point and heard the time and saw Julia and USM still not more than 20ft ahead, I started to think maybe I'd run a good time. The next few laps were me and the Williams girl battling it out; kind of fun to have someone there the whole time. With 600 to go the two of us raced up to Julia and USM. I felt a lot of momentum right there, and just after the backstretch hurdle we both passed the two of them, and I took the lead. Technically, I was having an excellent day on the hurdles... the water jump was not so hot this week but I was probably subconciously dreading the cold. I led the race for just over a lap--had the gun shot for me, which is mildly rare--until with 120 to go, the three of them passed me back: Williams, USM, Julia, and I finished in 4th. Not good enough for a T-shirt, but I think it was one of the best races in my life. In retrospect, I had a definitive lack of confidence in leading the race. No one could ever say if I could have placed higher at all; there is always the chance that the girls who beat me were simply faster than me, but if I were to do it over, there are two things I would have done differently: 1) When I decided to take the lead, I had to take it. My memory of the race is a little fuzzy because I was so out of it, but I do remember sort of panicking for a second. If I'm in front, I have to act like I belong in front. 2) I should have said something to Julia when I went by. I mean, in the end, she passed me back, but getting passed is usually a discouraging thing and when you're running for qualifying times you can't afford to wait like that. I think saying something might have made her go faster which would have made me (and the race) go faster. On that note--I am not going to let my emotions or perceived "place" on the team affect my competitiveness. Obviously, I'm never going to "let" someone beat me, and Julia outkicked me fair and square, but I'm not going to say that I was not surprised or incredulous during the race. But that's not going to happen again. If I'm ahead of someone, I'm ahead of them. That goes back to (1).
race_name: NE Division 3 Championships, Bates College, ME

comments: active recovery. Happy Mother's Day

20' wu, 20' tempo, 20' cooldown. I guess it was a combined long run/workout. Ran with Julia, we're the only ones left steepling. Although I don't know if I'm racing on Saturday or not. Bates is messing with my head. They posted an 11:29.24 as "official" earlier in the day, and then changed it to 11:35.24 in the afternoon. That's a load of crap. I deserve to run at All-New Englands. One of the top most disappointing events in my running career, just behind the whole CIF fiasco my senior year of XC. Both were situations where I ran my best but something external kept my best from being good enough. We'll see. Sluggo submitted entries today before they changed it back. Keeping my fingers crossed, but trying not to hope much. Anyway, today's workout was really good. Warmed up BU loop, then did tempo back and forth on the Esplanade to stay on the grass. Ran a 6:40 first mile, then dropped to about 6:15-17, then finished out the 20 with another mile+. Feels really good to know I can finish some workouts with Julia. I think I have an 11:20 in me; I'd love to have my name on the national list. Gotta start prepping myself for what that race might feel like, whether it's this week or ECAC's.
extras: a little bit of 'B': 3 sets each of modified pullups and incline press, plus lat. crunches and jacknife

comments: Ok, I'm going to assume that this 11:35 is finally the right time for last Saturday. The first posted time was in the 3 hr range, which was wacked. Finally, on Monday they put up 11:29.24 officially, which is an All-NE auto time. Then, Monday night, they changed all of the results from the steeplechase, listing me at this 11:35, which is NOT an All-NE time. I was pissed as all hell. (See yesterday's log entry.) However, entries were due during the day, and Sluggo submitted me at 11:29, not aware that they had changed. We got a confirmation email today saying I'm entered, but I'll believe it when I see seed sheets.
extras: steeple drills, including lead leg/trail leg separate run-overs. doing much better on those, although I have a lot to learn

comments: Seed sheets are up! And I'm in, for real. And Wheaton is in the race... Another great workout today. 1x1200 tempo (to get us tired), 2x600 over 5 hurdles (2:10), 2x400 flat (78-80, 73-77), with full recovering in between. Ended up running 4:35, 2:07, 2:08, 76, 74, right on. Getting pumped for Saturday, but trying not to obsess over it.

grass loops with Julia, nothing exciting. Round 1 of the 2.007 contest was tonight; I made it to day two even though I didn't score anything, because they liked my robot. Heh, looks like I'm getting a lot of lucky breaks this week
extras: Guess I'm not going to squeeze in a second day of lifting

comments: ready to roll
extras: 2 sets of walkovers just for feel

comments: Put way too much pressure on myself today and I cracked. I guess with all the stuff going on last week, I just really wanted to prove I belonged, I really wanted to not embarass Sluggo, I really wanted to get a legitimate PR, I really wanted to beat certain girls, and I really wanted to do well for my parents. That, combined with a crazy week at school and two nights of 2.007 contests, just drained me. I didn't sleep well last night, was dozing in the car on the way up and passed out as soon as I got in the car on the way back. I'm not entirely sure why, when I have bad races, I have such bad races, but oh well. I was more nervous going into this meet than I can ever remember being and I was still shaking a little even after I finished. I tried to calm myself down after a pretty sloppy first water jump but I never got into a rhythm and wasted all my energy being worried. I either never had a chance or never gave myself a chance.
race_name: All New England, University of New Hampshire

comments: Did the workout on my own so that I wouldn't get competitive and overdo it. Just back and forth on the dirt/grass between BU and science museum.

extras: hurdle drills, lead leg/trail leg, a couple of strides over. Forgot to do barriers

comments: 15 min, strides and a couple of barriers, one time over hurdles with each leg, 5 more min.

Felt great mentally and emotionally this morning, but I think MIT had already taken its toll. I've been really restless and sleeping less lately. Conditions were perfect for me to go after a solid PR--the first sunny meet since Colby, and I was way less nervous than last week. The race went out fast again, which I wasn't quite expecting. I tried to sit and run my own race, hanging just off the pack in last, trying not to go out hard. Came through the 800 in 2:57 again anyway, although it felt much easier than last week. The 1600 was I think about 6:04, 2 seconds faster than I wanted, but I was already tiring. Didn't have the energy or control to make any moves in the last three laps, even though I kept trying. Got passed just around that point and finished the race in last for the 2nd time (9th), one place out of getting a medal. In any case, I ran faster than last week, and it's a little comforting to know that a race that felt so slow could still be under 11:50, when three weeks ago I was working as hard as I could to run that.
ahh.... summer

weather's perfect. ran down the Comm. Ave median in Newton since that's where I parked to meet my carpool for work. This summer's going to be a tight squeeze with a full time job and 2.5 hrs of driving every day, but I'll make it

woke up early to run before work. I really have to find a schedule that works for me; I think I'll try lunch time next week. down to JFK and back then ran by killian court to watch commencement get started. I have the beginning of summer soreness all over
drove out to Newton to bike some hills with Bryan. Just thought I'd give my legs a rest since they're a little surprised from all the running
extras: 2 sets sp, some lifting and core stuff

comments: Boston summer has finally arrived...headed out around 9am and it was already 75. Not a bad run though, today's the first day that I'm counting in my "summer mileage"
Took the day off after moving all day yesterday, ugh I'm sore

meet up w/people to run at lunch today. it's a bunch of guys, some young and some more middle-aged types. They're about my pace, so it was good. Today, though, they were getting ready for the Texas Instruments/Dana Farber 5k, so we warmed up a mile, ran a mile hard, ran another mile or so, then picked it up again. I wasn't timing but someone behind me said I ran one of the hard miles in 7:20, certainly didn't feel that bad even in the noontime heat. I think I'll try running at lunch on Mon/Wed
ridiculously hot
to Boston Commons before work, still too hot for a shirt at 6:30am
some thunderstorms this afternoon cooled the area off quite a bit, it was down to a nice cool 58 by the time I got going. quads were dying from lifting yesterday (leg press)
wandered around attleboro during lunch; couldn't meet up with the guys because i had a meeting at 1:00 and had to start early.
got a quick one in before work

Was going to only run 5, but Gwen was going long and I figured trading Saturday's and Sunday's runs wasn't a big deal. Actually, I'm probably going to total my mileage in 14 day spans rather than weeks, to give me more flexibility. Anyway, Gwen had looked up a route to Castle Park, so we went on an adventure. Not too bad of a run, although we didn't make it all the way to Castle. Including the trip from WILG, Streets and Trips says it's 10.5, maybe I'll figure a little under that.
ran around the Comm. Ave median in Newton again, since it was on the way back from Hali's fencing tournament, which she won because she's a stud

ha, got my ass kicked by a bunch of old guys at lunch today... ok, actually, they weren't old, most of them were in their mid 30's (some were probably in the late 40's early 50's range though), so I suppose they were justifiably faster than me. We were going to go for an "easy 7" and I knew from the start that it was going to be fast but managable, which it was, but I was definitely pretty wiped out the rest of the afternoon. good times though, I'll take tomorrow to myself and go with them again Wednesday
ran in the morning when i got to work, to beat the heat and start my day off right. saw a deer on my run, actually on the TI campus

same loop as monday, with the same guys, 40 seconds faster. it felt fast again, but I was more motivated this time when I got into a discussion with one of the guys about La Jolla and Del Mar... sigh. Measured the loop on streets and it's closer to 7.5 even though they call it 7; I find it hard to believe I was going that fast though. Going to settle for 7.25. Anyway, held up better and wasn't beat when I went back to work--running with these guys is either going to kill me or make for one heck of a season; we'll find out in November
extras: pushups (the better ones), a little bit of ab stuff and leg press

comments: biked to campus and ran Sci-museum/harvard from the Z-center. just felt like i didn't want to pound on the road any more
extras: a quick set of stability ball stuff

comments: nice and easy again today

Sci museum-footbridge from WILG. Woke up at 10, which in retrospect was way too late to start running, but oh well. Got up above 90, but the time I was out there, and I started to feel pretty dehydrated about halfway through. Didn't drink enough yesterday at the beach... started feeling a little funny coming back from the footbridge, but I couldn't cut the run short at that point, so I just slowed up until I felt comfortable. Got water twice and doused myself with the hose at the tennis courts to make the last half-mile in much more comfortable. gonna wake up early to run this week as long as this keeps up.
woke up before work to avoid yesterday's mistake. much better.
extras: lifting, core, hurdle drills just to entertain myself. Worked on lead leg today, maybe I'll do trail leg next week.

comments: morning run again
ran up and down hills at boston commons, not workout or repeat style, just keep trying to go up a lot
No work today or tomorrow; cost-saving shut down. Slept in and did my long run in the morning. decided to go sans watch
to fenway and around with gwen
Was going to do the long run tomorrow to rest a bit more, but erika wanted someone to run with so I met her at BC and we ran to Jamaica Pond. Got the extra miles by adding on going from and to WILG
ran over to Government Center via Sci Museum to see the 4th of July festivities, but I guess i got there too early, nothing was going on yet. built in some strides at the end
ran easy over to summit ave, up once and then in and out of the streets a bit, added on around campus

tried to go with the guys at work again, but I was running late (actually working, ha) so I missed them. ran on my own and rolled my ankle a bit, but I was able to make it back ok. It didn't swell up but is sore now; I was taking tomorrow off anyway so it'll be ok
still unsure about the ankle, so I aquajogged and did HBH's drills
biking w/bryan out the minuteman trail. nice day out, still trying to give the ankle a rest. it should be fine tomorrow, got a good workout in today
great run out at Walden with Erika. shady, soft dirt/fallen leaves trails the whole way. stopped for water at 80 min when erika finished up her run, then hit the last 20 on my own. great day
extras: swimming at walden! haha, or, floating rather...
