comments: warmed up 9 min on my own because i was expecting to run fast with the guys at work, but then a few of the mid-level guys (they have a self proclaimed "A" group and "C" group but no "B", but there are a couple of guys who consider themselves "B+") went out so i went with them easy. Picked it up the last mile or so, because I felt like i had it in me.
extras: pushups, core, a couple of strides

comments: warmed up a mile, did hurdle stuff, ran another mile while waiting for bryan, missed bryan some how, ran by myself. Just an easy day
extras: hurdle drills. today's self-chosen focus was being able to takeoff on either leg, unplanned. went pretty well. lunges, some situps

comments: almost missed my run with the guys at work; the "A" team headed out before I realized that they were going, but I only left less than a minute behind them and caught up before they left the TI campus. Had a good run today, they were only going 6 which probably helped a lot, but even so I didn't really feel like I was struggling to hang on. hopefully I'll be able to stay with them for the rest of the summer, I really like these runs

went to the Fresh Pond race for the first time. Got there, warmed up about a mile and a half, then sat around and stretched until the race started. Started off with one of the only other women in the race; we were both going twice around (5 mi) and shooting for around 7min pace. Hit the first mile in 6:45 or so, which seemed to scare her a bit since she was just coming off knee surgery. She had dropped back by the time we finished the first (of two) loops, and I was on my own from there. Afterwards, chatted with her and another guy for a bit and cooled down with both of them. Fun times, a good crowd. Exactly even splits too, 17:06 for both loops

started at uncle ira's house, ran back toward the reservior, twice around, back past the start to Auburn St. then back again. Pretty good sized hills in there too. luckily the cloud cover stayed the whole time, since I didn't finish up until almost noon.

warmed up three quarters of a mile slow , I figured it might help my run with the guys at work. either it did, or I'm getting in better shape... about 50 seconds faster on the same loop, and I finished up front with the first guy in. :D Weather is getting hot and gross, as one guy put it, "I feel like I just took a hot bath... except that I'm not clean."

National Weather Service warned that the heat index would push 100 day, so I decided to wake up early and get the run out of the way. Definitely glad I did, it was already 77 when I got out the door at 5:30. longfellow-western, from wilg
another hot one, so I woke up early. bummed that i didn't get to run with people at lunch, but it wasn't really worth bearing 90+ temps. summit ave, including the last 1.5/2 miles a little faster
took myself on a tour of boston; hit science museum, north end, financial district, the harbor, etc, all the way around to the commons.
out Rt. 9N from Halcyon in Lake George. rolling hills the whole way
from Mass Ave to the last bridge. was a bit sore from waterskiing yesterday, but I tried to stay concious of the pace and not let it slip from "easy" to "lazy"

extras: core (sb), pushups, 20+ min aqua jog in the afternoon, just to loosen up

comments: 35:32 for the 5 mile loop with the guys at work, plus a mile before hand and a mile after on my own. decided I could suck it up and deal with the heat because I really wanted the company, but it was roasting: 93, "feels like" 105

phew! what a difference 25 degrees makes! the weather broke and I ran at 6pm after work, while it was about 65-70 out. had a great run. I was trying the same idea of running my fastest "easy" pace as possible, where the only strain was mental, and the results was 10.25 mi at 7:40 or so pace. (I'm writing it at 10 though, because I think a run earlier in the week was a hair short) I'm starting to getting really pumped about this season, now that the coaching thing is official and we're getting down to business.
great run with Gwen and Nancy. Weather was cool and cloudy and we were moving pretty well.

warmed up a mile, ran 42:55 for the 6 mi loop with the guys, then straight into one more mile. feeling good, i just need to start dedicating 20 min everyday to stretching, massaging, taking care of myself, etc. can't wait to go home
probably would have prefered run a little slower on my own, but I emailed out for company and gwen and erika came along, and good times were had by all
extras: bench press, leg press, core (took a break at work)

comments: same deal as last week: warm up a mile, stretch, run 5 with the guys, then straight into another mile. This time did the 5mi loop in 35:13, not bad. My IT band is way tight and was a bit sore all day, I think the leg press machine at work is a little funky. good thing I'm only there two more days! anyway, it was only a dull ache for some parts of the run so i figured it was ok, gonna stretch and ice and stuff

wanted to make sure to take it easy so i left the watch at home. science museum-footbridge from WILG. while i was running past the longfellow bridge, some girl yelled out of the car, "hey speeeeedy!" it was really cute and made my day, I laughed out loud while running
postponed yesterday's run due to lightning, but running today worked just as well. went with Nancy for the first part, then tacked on one more bridge

blech. a pretty pathetic run, just logging miles. met up with gwen with the intent of running to the reservoir, twice around, but we both felt like crap for some reason, so we went around once instead. we slogged our way back and called it a day. i'll go long once i'm home and rested

One of the best parts about coming home is that first run setting out from TP state beach. North just past via de la valle, then south to guy fleming. My morning pulse was still myseteriously a bit high (44, although it was 50 on Sunday, usually 40-42) but since it was going back down I figured as long as I went easy it was ok. Got a good night's sleep in, plus a 90 minute nap midmorning. Going to spend the next couple of days resting a lot, catching up on my sleep, and watching the World Championships on wcsn.com . $4.95 for 8 days of coverage, including archives. It's awesome!!

I went to the track with the intent of just doing hurdle drills, but then I bumped into Deanna who was doing a workout, and begged me to join her. I agreed to do the second set of what was essentially long strides, and here's how it turned out: 2x200, 4x400 equal dist. rest: 41, 39, 87, 89
extras: hurdle drills, stability ball stuff. feeling a bit more confident with the left leg

comments: a bit slow, been spoiled by the flats of Boston

deanna convinced me to drive over to PQ, we started from canyonside park. she took me on a loop (55:51) that included a pretty solid hill that kind of had my heart racing, then I grabbed some water back at the car and continued on another 50 min out and back in the canyon.
extras: another nap, hah. Third day in a row that I've slept over nine hours and taken a daytime nap. It feels gooood

comments: ran up broken hills to genesee (across from UCSD) and back, hitting north/south fork and guy fleming on the way down. I think it was closer to nine, but i'll write 8.5 to give me a rounding cushion. 12:44 up the hill, not the greatest but it felt good. this makes the week top 58 miles in 6 days and on singles, which i'm glad to note hasn't given me any trouble. in other news, USA men's 4x1 dropped the baton! again! tragic, but almost funny to hear the British annoucer say, "They've blown it so early in the race!"

North from TP state park. Thought I started pretty sluggish, but I got to the gate in under 15 min. Ran to Cliff st. in Solana Beach, which according to MS Streets is 4.59 mi. Ran a lap around the parking lot when I came back because I thought I was short of 9, but it turns out I was a bit over. Out in 36:30, back in 34:33

So I think I've been sick for the last week or so. Over the weekend, I thought it was the Clarinex that was making me sleepy and making my heart race, but this morning I was still feeling funny and I didn't take any Clarinex. So I've decided I probably have a cold, and I put off the run until the afternoon while I rested. ran out and back down the lagoon trail then back into the park and around our home course. I was surprised to find that I was holding an 8 min pace or just under for the whole trail section.
extras: 4 strides, a couple of runs over hurdles

comments: decided to finally break it up into doubles to increase the quality of the runs, and partly so I wouldn't get bored. Probably should have done the easier run in the morning, since it seems that morning isn't my best hour, but oh well
TP state beach, south into the park and up and down the trails a bit, to get some hills

ugh. Still feeling sick a bit, mostly right after I wake up I'm kind of shaky and my heart is beating like I had too much caffiene. Breakfast and a little bit of rest seems to make me feel better, so I decided to postpone this week's long run and wait until later in the day to go. Headed out around 11:30, down to the bike path and out Green Monkey Loop. Didn't do the whole loop because I didn't want to get lost, so I turned around at just over halfway and made it out and back.

delayed the run again until I felt solid; TP state beach to Lomas Santa Fe and back around noon. The sun was out but the ocean kept things cool, so it was no big deal. I like to leave my afternoons free to go to the beach... Anyway, solid run, I'm feeling much better, I'll be 100% before I go back to school. Out in 32:30, so back in 30:30, but coming back was a little longer since I stayed on the streets until 11th St to avoid the rocks on the cliffs.

did the loop down to carmel valley rd and up through the other side of TP state park, coming back Del Mar Heights. It was longer than I remembered and I was roasting in the sun (~3pm), so the run ended up being quite unpleasant. oh well, good thing I was going short

last run at home, bummer. Finally feeling better and I was able to run in the morning without trouble. Left from TP state beach again, south to Guy Fleming and then North to Frogs. Nothing special, going kind of slow at the start, I think the mileage is getting to my legs for the first time. Tomorrow's a day off, though, for flying, so no big deal. All in all I had a great two weeks of running in San Diego and I'm pumped and fit for the season.

Another talk before we set out this afternoon. A big group starting out on the Harvard-Elliot loop but by the end it was me, Gwen and the two frosh Liz's booking along at a strong sub-8 pace. The loop is 7.17, plus the distance from the Z-center to the road and back, plus a quick trip backwards to snag one of the frosh, so I'm guessing 7.5. Plus the strides.
extras: 7x100m strides, as a "cool down": 3 accelerating, then 4 slowing down. Chin ups/pushups, back stuff on sb, sideways plank. Wow, what a day. I am definitely beat, and going to be sore tomorrow.

comments: First day of practice, started out with a talk and then went to the pool for a quick workout and form review

Grass loops at Coach Taylor's suggestion. Turns out it's 0.89 mi around including the turf, did 9 loops with Erika, Gwen, Vicky, and Finn. If that mileage is accurate, we were moving at 7:26 pace, which surprised me. Even if it's a little off, we weren't going much slower than 7:35. Granted, it's flat, but still, that's pretty nice. Here are the paces per loop from Coach Brooks: Loop 2: 7:27 mile pace Loop 3: 7:23 Loop 4: 7:19 Loop 5: 8:07 (switched directions) Loop 6: 7:18 Loop 7: 7:14 Loop 8: 7:20 Loop 9?
alarm snafu and I was late to practice... argh I feel lame. Anyway, made it in time for the run, although I was a little out of it having just woken up in such a panic.
still moving at good paces. Did a gait analysis today at noon between workouts, running on the treadmill (weird sensation). Anyway, we'll see what comes of that later.
extras: Full Speed 400 - 78. Basically, hit the start line just as you come in from a run and do an all out four. It felt rough but I think it's a good place for me to be.

comments: grass loops again, only 7 this time. Ran w/Vicky, Gwen, Erika today, but was kind of dragging. The whole thing felt like it was too much effort--I led lap 3 but all the rest it was Erika and/or Vicky up front and I was just trying to prevent a gap. After looking at the splits though, I figured out why: Loop 1: 6:31 (7:19 pace) Loop 2: 6:30 Loop 3: 6:32 Loop 4: 6:24 (7:11) Loop 5: 6:25 Loop 6: 6:23 Loop 7: 6:18 (7:05) I did the last one on my own, surprised I could pick it up like that, but all of them were pretty damn fast, and faster than Tuesday... although apparently that's just about the perscription for a sub 19:30 5k runner. This is going to take some getting used to, I think.
longfellow +extra, started easy with Jordan and finally loosened up a mile and a half in.
extras: chinups, pushups, abs and sb, same as monday. lunges

comments: BU loop nice a slow, plus a pickup on the way in to simulate a race warmup.
probably wasn't focusing enough on my form, got to talking and all

wasn't the best day, not bad either, just meh. Not dwelling on it too much. 6:08, 6:24; went out fast because I thought I had low 12's in me. But really it was just a hard tempo with no extra gears in my legs. Took 5th on the team.
arboretum and around there for a bit. 37:40 to the gate, I can't remember if that's slow or fast or what.
drove out to Franklin Park and ran the course, then wandered, then parts of it backwards. Didn't really get into any groove at all running, but I got through it
focused a little more on my form

drove to the arboretum, 2 mi warmup, then fartlek of 600-800m "whenever someone was ready," keeping the pace in between pretty upbeat. Things were rolling along nicely for the first couple, I was with Gwen and Kristen. By the 3rd, I was dropping back from Kristen a bit but still feeling pretty good, but then on the 4th, I rolled the same ankle as I did earlier in the summer. It hurt really badly this time, and I had to stop a bit. I was able to run in though, another 10-12 minutes, until I could feel my opposite leg get tired, and then I knew I was compensating and that I had to stop. I'm not really concerned, though, some standard RICE should be fine.
didn't want to prolong the injury, especially something that's so easily healed.
extras: ankle strengthening stuff in the trainers'

comments: Footbridge/harvard with Gwen and Martha, with tape on the ankle. Didn't like the tape because I think it made me run a little funny, but if I focused I could do things normally. Could have maybe run a little faster but I was worried about Gwen's foot.

Oof... started out about where I should, 6:19, 13:04?, then bombed the last mile. Don't know if it's fitness or mental or what, but I'm trying not to let it get in my head for the future. The course was slow for the men and women today, and actually a bit physically long due to some concert. My slowest start of all the seasons, but I haven't done any speedwork except that unfinished fartlek on Tuesday. It's ok, I'm still fitter than ever before.
Fresh Pond. Feeling pretty heavy and junky.

Franklin Park. Groups were rearranged based on Saturday, so there were more of us running together today, which was nice. My legs also felt much better than yesterday, and I handled the pace no problem. A little slower than last week, I think, but I'd say under 8 min pace. I was super tired today though... fell asleep before 9 last night and woke up at 9 this morning, then took a nap in the afternoon. (!)
extras: strides, lunges, body weight lifting (2 chinups today!)

comments: Oh man! What a great workout! 7x800 with 200 jog in between, which had everyone seriously scared. The currently 8-woman B group consisting of Gwen, Kristen, Jessie, Labuz, Karen C, Steph, Vicky and myself had a window of 3:02-3:08, which we hit with utmost consistency. Taking turns leading so that pretty much everyone was in front once, we fired off 3:03, 3:00, 3:03, 3:00, 3:02, 2:59, 2:58, with the first three rests being 90s and the last three delaying only to 1:40+. Man, I can't explain how great today was, with so many people taking turns doing the pacework, I think confidence was really up, and it certainly made the workout more doable.
extras: 3mi wu, 25:36, 5 min cooldown, strides AM Elliptical (20 min)

comments: to Jamaica Pond (not around) and back via the Harvard Bridge to add on. I think the run was about 7.75 again, but we did the full speed 400 today, so I'll call it 8 and make sure to round down other days. My hamstring was way tight again and didn't loosen up until halfway back from the pond, but after practice Coach stretched it out a bit and it felt a little better.
extras: Full speed 400 (74s), strides AM Aquajog (20 min)

comments: grass loops, a bit easier this week: 6:56, 6:44, 6:53, 7:19, 6:50, 7:07, 6:58
extras: lunges, chinups, pushups, abs, missed sb ball because I had to get to class (AM 20 min elliptical)

comments: Great day for the team, as far as getting the pack running business down. Took 23rd thru 29th as a team, which was cool. I think it helped a lot, and made this week much more comfortable than last week, even though it was run in the same time. Of course, we did go out in like 6:50 or something ridiculously slow like that-- I guess it was slow because I was trying to wait for our group to form, and some people got stuck at the start. But oh well, learning to do it right is more important than the time/position right now. Personally, I'm still not happy with where I am race-wise. I think I should be running a bit faster right now, and definitely 25th place is not exactly what I should be at if I'm trying to get top 10 at NEWMACs, since this invite ended up being almost all NEWMAC teams anyway.
race_name: Engineer's Cup and Smith Invite, Smith

comments: out beacon st. to the reservior and around. meant to go up summit ave on the way back, but I was dogging it so much that I didn't want to violate my 8th amendment rights.

I have lab on Mondays, so I'm going to miss the drive out every week, but it looks like a chunk of my group is in the same siituation, so no big deal. Instructions were to run "moderate pace" but we started out a little faster than that. After Vicky and Gwen turned at BU I was on my own and probably slowed it down a bit.
extras: lunges, chinups, pushups, abs, sb, ice bath. missed the AM pool because I was waiting around for my car to get fixed.

comments: drove to the Arboretum and good some good hills in with Finn and Hana. A little bit sluggish but got through it.
grass loops, all pretty much in the 7:0something range. muddy day, nothing exciting
extras: full speed 400, 75, alone today and it felt a lot easier than last week's 74. lunges and the usual core circuit, AM elliptical

comments: Yup, another one. Next week is just going to be about racing for place (it's a 6k anyway) and I'm not going to think about it until then. Just trying to remember that the ability is in me and that it'll all come around in October.

easy run over at franklin. took yesterday off, my first run-less day since I've gotten to MIT. much needed, I think, and this week is supposed to be lower mileage, so maybe I'lll feel better. Starting to get things sorted out about my approach to racing and this season, and I felt a lot better after tonight's team meeting and my goal meeting.

The workout was our choice of (2-3)x1600, (0-2)x800, and 1x200 on the track. The restriction to that was that it had to total 5000m in 19:20, and the total time of the workout was supposed to be 27:30. From there we got to pick our repeats, order, time and rest, although it ended up not being quite so open-ended. In any case, my group (C group this week, based on last week's performance), decided on a ladder: 800 (3:05), 1600 (6:15), 1600, 800, 200 (40), with 2:30 after the 1600's and something less after the others, adding up to 8 min rest. We ended up cranking it off beautifully, with everyone in the group strong and tightly in the pack. 3:01 (2:20 rest), 6:09 (3:08), 6:14 (2:01), 3:00 (66), 38, for a total of 19:02/27:39. As usual I got smoked on the 200, I really need to develop legspeed, but all in all a good, comfortable workout.
extras: icebath, 2 mi wu/2 mi cd, AM ellipitcal (15 min)
FS400 in 74... again I think my legs had it faster this week, but I ate just before practice and wasn't quite feeling up for running fast. AM aquajog

comments: all just around 8 min pace. really tired all over today, legs, arms, everything

I'm back! Well, on my way, at least. A 6k PR, not bad, 4th on the team and 42nd overall. We've never run this course before, but I really liked it, as did everyone on the team. It was hilly, but in a nicely challenging way, not something brutal. Went out pretty reasonably; hanging back with my "C" group (well, Hana and Adrienne were the only ones I was aware of). After the first mile I tried to move up a bit, because there was a downhill stretch. I picked off a good number of people until I was caught up with Steph, Liz and Jessie, then just made the rest of the race an attempt to stick with them. I was on and feeling good until a short steep hill in the 3rd mile--I don't know, those just really kill me. After that my stomach was churning and I was tired--admittedly, there were probably a few points in that next mile or so where thought it would be "ok" to slow down, but I just focused on trying to get to the 3 mi mark. Once we were into the woody loop, things were better because I had things to concentrate on, although I had lost Jessie and Steph by then. They were still in my sights, though, and I kept after it. I didn't quite know the kicking points, but the Wellesley coach called out "500 to go" to one of his runners and I tried to go there. Passed the Wellesley runner and attempted to pass at Bentley runner, but I didn't do it very convincingly. In the final stretch, though, I found that last gear and passed the Bentley runner again, along with two others. Splits: 6:25, (6:36) 13:00, (6:50) 19:51, 6:07 pace for the last .72
race_name: Tufts Invite, Tufts Farm Course, Grafton, MA

comments: up Summit Ave. via Beacon, determined to be better at hills by the time NEWMACs rolls around. Felt pretty good today, surprisingly.

extras: new lifting: weighted lunges on a pad, one-leg hip-ups on SB, chin ups and SB incline press, calf raises

comments: warmup 2mi, 4 mi fartlek, cd 1 mi. Same thing as in August , only the race pace was in 400-600m segments. For some reason today, though, the group fell apart pretty quickly, and for the first couple the pace wasn't quite staying up in between segments. I led the first, convinced Steph and Adrienne to lead a couple, then led a few more. Once I started to try to keep the pace up in between, people really started dropping off, and by the last two or three, it was Jessie, Adrienne and I only. Towards the end Jessie started to burn me and I finished each of the last three successively further back from her, but I think I had the right pursuit mentally. All in all, I think it was a solid workout, not quite the intensity that the coaches had in mind, but good nonetheless.
To the Commons and back via Sci. Museum with a big group, plus some chunk added on at the end. Nothing exciting, I think we were moving pretty well.
extras: FS400 in 73, the fastest yet. Sprint drills, AM pool

comments: 8 loops, right around or under 7" each.

drove out to Blue Hills Reservation with Jordan-- a long drive due to route choice and traffic, but worth it for variety's sake. We parked at Houghton's Pond, ran around there one, then crossed over to Ponkapoag Pond, where we wandered through the bog for a bit before making our way all the way around. A nice steady run. The left hamstring is a bit tight again, I'll ask Coach Brooks to take care of it Monday.
supposed to be in the pool for an hour, but I really just felt like running 3 miles. So I did, nice and slow, then aquajogged for 20 min.
Rolled my right ankle again, about 1.5 mi from school (on the way back). Oh well, just icing and stuff, it's not swelling much, but man, not something I really needed right now
extras: missed the lifting, did one set of pushups, AM pool

comments: stayed on campus to do the workout on the track, instead of going to Franklin, to keep tabs on my ankle. Got a lighter tape job on it, but on the warmup it was making me lopsided and making my left hamstring tired, so I pulled it off before the workout. 1600, 2x800, 1600 with 3:00 after the 16 and 1:45 after each 8. Windows were 6:00-6:03 and 2:56-58. Did a poor job of focusing on the long intervals though. Schoolwork and stuff on my mind kept keeping into my head during the workout; I need to really leave it at home. Wound up falling back from Gwen on both 1600's, although the 8's went ok: 6:06, 2:56, 2:58, 6:18. Tomorrow I'll be back in action.
extras: 2 mi wu, 1 mi cd, AM 3mi (24:54), lunges

comments: down to Charlestown and back through downtown Boston, just for variety. Moving at a good pace by the end
extras: FS400, 72, another "PR" These things are starting to instill fear in me... AM 3mi (24:04)

comments: supposed to do grass loops today, but I had a test in the afternoon and I was running on my own, so I ran to the commons, around there for a bit, and back. Since I ran early, it was a little close to my AM run and I was tired, but this is the last AM run for the week.
extras: 3mi AM, lifting on my own in BC

comments: things are starting to settle down... getting pumped for a muddy, "this is XC" race

Hm... I felt like I went according to plan. Was in position at mile 1 and was moving up the whole way through. But somehow, my second and third miles dropped to 6:50, which was just no good. Yeah, it was a rainy, muddy day, but still... I guess I'm *generally* satistfied with the mental effort I put it, but disappointed that it didn't amount to much. On a positive note, I think I had a solid last 600 meters or so, and picked off about 6 people in the last straightaway.
extras: 2 mi wu, 3 mi cd, forgot to do strides and i'm hurting
race_name: New England Championships, Franklin Park , MA

comments: Cooled off a lot this weekend. Missed the AM run because I didn't realize it was Monday already (no school), but I'll make it up throughout the week.

2 mi wu, 4 mi of 600m fartlek, 2 mi cd at the arboretum. This one went much better than the last, at least from my perspective. We kept it up pretty well in between, and everyone was pretty much sticking together, leading different pickups. lots of downhill on the roads, though, which pounded my legs
Off for Yom Kippur

Not a bad day. Finally getting back into things, this race is up there with Tufts. Due to construction, the course was apparently 172m short, or something like that. It rained all least week though (although the sun was out for our race!), and the course was ridiculously mucky and wet, especially at the start and finish, which were across fields of long grass. Everything that wasn't muddy was pavement, and I actually wore my XC flats from high school. Coach Taylor claims that the shortness is probably is equivalent to the bad conditions (i.e., it's "worth" a 19:34 5k), but I try to go by what I've run is what I've run, so yeah. Anyway, I think today was a good day mentally. Starting the second mile I just kept telling myself to only concentrate on that one mile, and not worry about the rest of the race, and it actually worked for the first third to half of the mile. Going up the long hill I lost Hana, but instead of giving up right away, I really gave it a good shot. I think next time I'll have the confidence to be more aggressive for even longer... next time being NEWMACs!
extras: 15:53 mi wu, 24:42 cd, strides. I tried the extra 400 or so thing before strides today... I don't know if it helped or if I just had a better day, but I don't think it can hurt. I'll consider making it a habit

comments: wasn't supposed to run, but the sun was out and I didn't feel like going to the pool
a solid paced run down to Fresh Pond and back via Brattle St. My legs are feeling a little beat
fartlek at Arboretum, same deal as before but with 400 meter stretches. A solid workout with a lot of hills included. Didn't have time to ice bath
Ugh. My legs are still dead as ever. Struggled to keep up with the pack; really glad it was a 200 today.
lost the timing for each loop, but they were all under 6 min and most in the 6:54 range. Legs still feeling a bit junky, Coach H reminded me to try my Stick.

Pizza relay 1--5xs wilderness loop at FP. We were paired off so that one person's rest was her partner's running time, and the starts were staggered so that the fastest people started last and theoretically, all the teams would finish at the same time (or at least close). Steph and I started 3rd of 4. Finn and Jessie passed us by the 2nd repeat, but we made up ground on Liz L and Betsy on the fourth, finishing 3rd again. Definitely an intense workout, my gut was wrenching on the 4th as I tried to catch Liz, and the fifth was pretty brutal: 3:02, 3:08, 3:08, 3:05, 3:06. Legs starting to loosen up, although my quads were still kind of tired. I think it's the lack of AM workouts

drove out to Wellesley to watch part of the team run. Fun times with body paint, although the temp. dropped into the mid-40's. Jogged around putting up signs, then watched the race, then did a loop around the course, took a break for awards, and another loop. Kind of a silly run but whatever.
cooling off a bit now, which made my joints a little achy
even cooler today. started about 5:30 after a nice team meeting, and it was dark and cooling off a lot by the time we finished

The workout started with the Pizza Relay, a 3x1600 race (each runner doing one 1600). 10 minutes after the last finisher, we had to do the rest of our workout, 2x800 and 2x400 with 2:30 rest after the 8's and 75s rest between the 4's. I ran second leg, getting the baton a bit back from Gwen. I knew I had a faster 1600 in me than her, and set out on a controlled chase. I was making up ground each lap (although not as quickly as I had hoped), and by the back turn on the last lap I was pulling up. Went by her going around the turn and brought it in a few seconds ahead, good for a 5:44. Finished the rest of the workout well until the wheels fell off a bit on the last 400. Times were still where they were supposed to be: 3:05, 3:00, 81, 83.
loops in the sub 7:10 range, today. Nothing exciting, although nice and short to rest up for NEWMACs. I think I'm ready to roll
extras: FS 200, 34. My continual 'one of the slowest in the full speed stuff' performance inspired the coaches to ask me what I thought about being a 5k/10k runner. Hmm...

comments: We get free internet here at the hotel and I have my 2.671 laptop with me, so looks like I get to update tonight. Drove out to Smith in the early afternoon and ran the course. Looks pretty good, muddy in some spots and covered in leaves, but overall pretty good. Plus it's supposed to drop to just under freezing tonight, and with the women racing first, hopefully that will solidify the course a bit. Might snow in the morning too, which is kind of exciting. I'm trying not to overthink tomorrow, but I feel pretty good. I just have to trust in the training and hope it comes together.

A decent effort on a tough course. I mostly executed the plan to go out smartly and move up, gaining 10 or so places over the last two miles. Really though, I should have gone out a little harder and moved up another 3-5 places, but I feel good about how the second and third miles went. It kills me, though, how bad I am on hills. Teamwise, we took fourth, which was kind of a bummer, especially with Smith in 3rd being only 1 point ahead. But that's just luck.

daylight savings time ended and now it's dark when we start running, which sucks. Went Harvard-Footbridge and got moving pretty well even though I was feeling pretty tired from classes and stuff. I guess the tired showed through, though, since I ended up getting sloppy and rolling my ankle while crossing the footbridge. I think the ligaments are so loose, by now, though, that no damage was done. I just really have to remember to keep the exercises up.

3x800, 4x400 with 2:00 jog after the 8's and 1:00 after the 4's. Window was 3:00-3:02, 85-87, and we managed 3:00, 2:59, 3:00, 85, 83, 83, 81. My legs were not moving well at all on the first one, but the second seemed significantly easier. Most of the time it was Adrienne leading, since she felt good and was doing well pacing, but I took a 400 and also tried to stay up in front and help out. A good workout in that we were beating the windows, but I still don't have the pop in my legs that I'm waiting for in the taper.

Supposed to run a more substantial run, but 4 miles was all I could fit in before my cousin's Bat Mitzvah, since I wasn't willing to sacrifice a scarcely encountered full night's sleep. At this point in the season, though, I'm not too concerened, I got something in so that I wouldn't lose fitness, which is good enough.

A short, light version of last week's workout: 2x800, 3x400, with 2:30 after the 8's and 1:15 after the 4's, with windows of 2:57-3:00 and 85-87. 2:55, 2:57, 85, 83, 86. Still waiting for that end of season "pop." I think this school year is really dragging on me.
practice was short and sweet today, nothing like a good 4 grass loops.

I had a bit of a sore throat yesterday morning but I didn't think much of it, but after dinner yesterday, I started to get a headache and bodyaches, which was not good. Took some Tylenol Cold and slept for a good 11 hours or so last night, and I feeling at least mobile by the afternoon when we left for Springfield. Today I jogged around the course for a bit until I was feeling too worn out, then I stopped to walk the rest. I'm really hoping I can run tomorrow, but if I'm not 100% I should probably give the spot to Adrienne. We'll see

There's never any point in dwelling on the last meet of the season, but I'll just jot down a few quick reminders to myself for next year. I felt better when I woke up this morning and decided to race. I think from that moment on, I should have focused on removing any doubts on account of having been sick, because those doubts stayed with me and caused me to race more conservatively, something that I had been doing too much of this season to begin with. The start was really tight, 40-something teams with two people on the line. I got out what I thought was ok, hitting the mile in 6:10 or so, but in reality, it wasn't enough, because I spent almost the entirety of the race moving up, only to wind up in 115. Which means that if I went out 30 places further up, and maybe say I could pass 5 less people due to being tired, I'd have been much better off. But that's ok, that's just a lesson for larger, fast races that I forgot about this year. The same thing happened at NEWMACs. The positive side of the race was being able to work with Vicky and drag myself through the middle miles, I think it was a big push for both of us.
race_name: NCAA DIII New England Regionals, Springfield

comments: Just a quick one to make me feel normal again. Did a short loop, swinging by Pearl on the way back to pick up something

First day of indoor. Headed through Charlestown and back via the Comm. Ave with Steph, before "testing", where we ran a 55 and a 400. I was definitely no longer warmed up by the time I ran the 4, and it hurt a lot.
extras: 55 (9.75), 400 (76.36) - both two of the slowest times on the team, heh

comments: I'm home! Oh, it's so good... slept a good 9.5 hrs last night, in my own bed, then headed over to TP state beach, where I did a standard Guy Fleming loop + dog beach and back. I was hurting a lot from Monday's testing, as expected, but somehow managed to put in a pretty good pace, although I wasn't trying to push it at all. Gotta love running in shorts and a sportabra in November.
extras: Short strides in the parking lot (PM) Another 2.5 miles on my way over to the school to do chinups and pushups

comments: slept in and woke up hungry, so I ate and then had to wait awhile to run. Got going around noon, at which point it was wonderfully sunny and warm. I drove down to the bike path and attempted to run Green Monkey Loop plus the trail for 8 miles, but there was construction on the loop, and my legs were feeling fried so I cut the trail part short and went 6. The dead legs feeling was probably a result of spending a bit of time this morning moving our 200 lb TV... my parents were both feeling it too. No big deal, just taking this week to get back into it.

went to Potato Shack this morning and I was pretty full so I put off my run until around 1pm, although I was still feeling it at that point. wasn't too uncomfortable though. Just around the neighborhood, wandering a bit
extras: lunges, crunches, some chinups and pushups

comments: TP state beach, up broken hills (12:34) over to USCD for a lap around, then down including Guy Fleming. The run took longer than I expected, but I've been dogging it the whole time I've been home. I feel like crap today too, lots of allergies, hopefully it's just that and not a cold. Some lady ran up next to me today and I thought she was going to pass me, but instead she just ran up next to me. I was feeling uncomfortable until she actually started talking to me, which I was actually ok with and enjoyed for a bit, since my run was just kind of not happening very well anyway. we chatted for a bit until I turned off to USCD
got sick at home on Saturday, and was way too worn out to do a tempo run like we were supposed to, so I hit the pool
forgot my watch, but we ran Charlestown backwards
ate too close to the workout, plus I just feel out of shape. got dropped by #3

extras: new lifting routine. A little more intense: weighted lunges, Roman dead lift, calf raises, chin ups, supine pullups, bench, stability ball incline press (1 and 1/4)

comments: Snow today!

A dark, icy 5x5 min tempo. The rest between the first two went long trying to avoid the ice, but each of the intervals was about 10 s long too. Pretty poor conditions for a workout... for the first time in a while I felt pretty good and fresh, but I could never really open up because of the road conditions

It's crazy, but I actually feel pretty good in the legs right now. I was able to keep up with the Finn/Lauren tandem today, which I haven't been capable of since indoor. Plus I just found out that this is the last week of practice for the term, which in all honesty, is kind of nice. Starting to feel excited and get competitive about the intrasquad meet.
Harvard-Elliot in 800m repeats, plus some add on (the loop took about 54 min). Felt good today, but it's way cold out

XC Reunion run! Ha, not really, but we've spent so much time running in our own groups that today we decided to "break the rules" and all run together. The distance group went Harvard-River St., and the milers turned off at BU. The pace slowed down a bit at that point; apparently Hana likes to stay a few steps ahead ;). Anyway, a good run all in all, good pace and the temp. rose above freezing for part of the day, so there was less ice.

Wow, surprised myself on that one. I was optimisically hoping for 11:20-11:25, since that's what I ran in that February meet last year (since I took indoor off). We warmed up a little early (about an hour ahead) so I did another 400 about 20 min. before the race, picking it up on the last 200. I had entertained the idea of just trying to stick with Finn from the gun, since it wouldn't have mattered too much if I dropped back, but when we went out, I could tell she was way out of my reach and that would have been stupid, so I dropped back. Hit 44 for the first lap, then pretty much stayed at 90 second quarters from there on. It was a great race, style-wise, if you want to call it that. Looking back, I was a well-paced race that exhibited a lot of focus on my part. I felt really strong and in control, too.There were also a couple decisions that, had I not made them, probably would have had me end up a good 20 seconds back. One was just after the third lap, Christa was still just behind me, and as I hit the fourth lap I decided to make a move, to avoid getting drafted. That worked out well, keeping me on 6:00 pace and opening up a gap. Next, coming through the mile, I told myself that I need to throw in another surge, both to make sure I had opened the gap and also since it takes an increased effort to run even splits. Again, a lot of times this is something I'd be afraid to do, but it worked out well, kept me on pace and didn't tire me out. A well timed kick with 600 to go and a strong last lap (40) brought me in under 11:20. Four seconds faster than the last time I ran on Dec. 10, freshman year, and a month ahead of the last time I hit 11:16. Splits: 89, 3:01, 4:30, 6:01, 7:32, 9:06, 10:36, 11:16.85

Bored of the usual routes, so I decided to head down Mass. Ave. (toward Boston) just to see what was out there, since I figured Sunday would be less crowded. Not much out there, but I did swing down Tremont and by the Reggie Lewis Center on the way back

extras: lifting, minus the supine pullups, because I couldn't move the squat rack. I'm at 3 chinups on the first set and 2x6 at 85 lbs on the bench, hurrah.

comments: I wore my warm pants today, it was definitely quite cold out. Down to Fresh Pond along the river, than back via Concord Ave. and Harvard Sq.
Another cold one. Ran down Beacon almost until the reservior

wasn't really looking forward to this workout, just because of all the other things going on (finals, etc), but it turned out to be pretty decent; warmer than the last two days. Finn was with Christa, Jennifer and I today but took off once we started the fast stuff, leaving me to lead our group. The first two were a bit inconsistent while we roamed around the Commons trying to find a bathroom, but the rest of the workout was solid. I was afraid of possibly not being able to hang on once things got rolling, but I ended up being strong on all of them.

yeah, not quite sure why it didn't occur to me not to wear a rain jacket, since I own one now. The ice was all melted and it was warm enough to wear shorts, but 3 miles in I was absolutely drenched. Went down Beacon in order to go up Summit on the way back.
Really slow today, lots of ice after yesterday's rain
extras: 15 min on the elliptical at night, blowing off some steam from studying. also sprint drills and abs

comments: First "long run" that I've done in a while... it was good. It actually went by really quickly, even though I went solo. Ran out down E. Berkeley all the way to the the beach or Castle Park or whatever it's called, like I did over the summer with Gwen. No ice today, so I was able to get moving.

Harvard-Elliot with 4x5 min tempo. Ran on my own in the morning before my afternoon final, and since I was running alone it was hard to get a gauge on the quality/pacing. I think I worked it pretty hard though; when it's cold out I always feel like I'm fighting extra hard.

TP state beach to Lomas and back, with a few detours. warmed up with my dad to Powerhouse, then ran the workout to Lomas, and back to 11th street. I tried to keep pushing as much as I could alone; I think it ended up being a pretty solid workout.

San Eligo lagoon plus a couple miles in San Dieguito Park. My legs started off pretty tired from yesterday but I was able to keep up a decent pace. I'm headed off to Vietnam with my family at midnight tonight, for almost two weeks. The running is going to be secondary but hopefully regular, even if I have to suck it up and use a treadmill (safety, culture, whatnot). We'll see if I can have some adventures.
extras: Weighted lunges/roman lift, calf raises, SB incline and some ab work. Limited resources at home.

comments: I lost a day crossing the international date line, so this is really one day off, not two. I ran on a treadmill in Hanoi, Vietnam, since the air quality and roads are really bad. Bumped around from 7.6-7.8 mph, so faster than usual.

We've travelled up to Sa Pa, a mountain town at about 1900m elevation. There's a small lake near the hotel that's about 1000m around, so I warmed up two laps, then did some pickups that were each one lap around.
extras: 1 set of lunges, half sprint drills, pushups

comments: around the lake again

Now we are in Central Vietnam, in Hue, the rainiest city in Vietnam, where is happens to be the rainy season. Which means that I was ridiculously muddy while running, which earned me a lot of strange looks, in addition to the strange looks that I got simply because I was running. I started off with my dad this time, but he turned off early with a stomach ache. I myself wasn't feeling my best, being a strange combination of full, tired (~5 hrs of sleep last night), and caffinated (they have strong stuff here), but aerobically, I felt ok with the longer run.
extras: weight lunges, romanian lift, calf raises (although their heaviest weight was 5kg), pushups

comments: After a disasterous bout with some kind of bug, I wasn't able to keep food down for a day and was feeling pretty bad for the next couple days after that. Not the greatest 2006 kickoff. This run in Hoi An (we're in the South now) was the first in a while, but I was still a but stomach achy. At least here no one yells strange (ok, strange from my perspective) things at you
Woke up early get a run in in Cambodia, before our 26-hour endeavour home, although I'm still having some stomach problems
extras: weighted lunges, romanian lift, SB incline on San Diego's Jan. 7

comments: TP state beach south just past UCSD. I was unsure about my abilities after the trip and the flight, so I decided to go up Park Rd, plus it was high tide anyway. Turned out that I was fine and feeling pretty strong, much better than I felt in the morning
extras: AM 3 mi (which were a bit difficult, after travelling) with some chinups and reverse pullups mixed in... I lost a little bit of strength there

comments: Ugh, kind of an ugly workout today. Surprisingly enough, though, I think the limiting factor was my legs, not my fitness, on account of yesterday's romp up Torrey Pines. I'm not sure why I like to work myself like that. The tempo run has been moved from 4x5:00 to 5x4:00, so I ran down to the bike path for my warmup (plus some add on) and did the whole thing along there. The first three pickups were ok--I was struggling but moving, but the 4th was absolutely disastrous, crawling close to 8:00 pace. I jogged the recovery over to the 800m mark we used in HS and did my 5th interval going back past the start, so that it was a bit downhill, so my legs had a break. That seemed to help, and the last one went better, although the cooldown up the hill back home was pretty ugly. I forgot how much it sucks to cool down that way.
Well it's back to Boston until the summer. I came in too late to make practice, but I stopped by before running on my own, so I ran around the Commons since it was already dark.

Sci Museum-Western. I was concerned that I was going to get dropped today, since I didn't run much in Vietnam, but it actually turned out ok. I guess I was feeling fresh--hopefully it'll last. Steph and I worked the first half of them together, then she turned back early (injury) and I was working on my own, with Christa trailing. A pretty solid workout, I guess I didn't lose much.

out the Emerald Necklace via Fenway. My knee was hurting a bit. It hurt for a day while I was home and then yesterday a bit, but I didn't feel it during the fartlek, so I didn't think much of it. I think it's from doing lunges funny while I was home, I'll just ice and take Motrin, usually things like this go away pretty quickly

I was shooting for 11:15, hoping that I hadn't lost any fitness over the break, but I think I did. Mentally, I think I stayed competitive the whole race, which is an improvement, and held off moves when I could, but in the last third of the race I dropped off pace and got passed by two people. I also tried my best to stay on the front of my foot and approach the race more as a long mile than a short 5k, which Coach Taylor has been mentioning in practice, but it was a bit tiring and I didn't have the guts or the focus to quite get all the way through the race like that.

I really wasn't looking forward to having a workout today, after getting into a car accident this weekend in New Hampshire (with two teammates in the car) and dealing with all the aftermath. No one was seriously hurt but I was definitely shaken and doubtful of my ability to focus enough for a speed workout, but it turns out a run was just what I needed. We had 5x4:00 again, so we went Western-Sci Museum and then I added on some extra cooldown. I was working well with Gwen on the first three, although Lauren gapped the both of us early on. For the last two, Gwen's hamstring was acting up and she dropped back, but I think I was still able to push on my own. We covered the 7.5 in around 54 min, so I think we were at a good pace for the tempo parts.

We went pretty slowly because my knee still hurt this afternoon, so I decided to finally get to the trainers. They said it was an early stage of tendonitis in my IT band, but still in a very managable state. I just have to heat and foam roll before runs, and then foam roll and ice after, and I don't need to necessarily change the workouts unless it hurts too much to run. Good thing it's IAP and I have plenty of time to manage things
extras: new lifts today: pushups and chinups, forward and backward weighted lunges, hip extensions on the SB and "windshield wipers" on the SB

comments: trying to get the mileage up to decent levels. Didn't wake up until 11 but I headed out soon after. My knee was sore again, maybe I just need to make sure to warm it up and stretch it out well.

Did the heat and foam roll before the workout, and the knee felt ok. A dull ache, but definitely not at all like yesterday. Today was a super windy day to be running a workout, and in traditional New England fashion, we manage to be running against the wind almost everywhere, despite going in a loop. It went pretty well though, and I think we managed to work pretty hard. We were doing 600m intervals again, and even though we strung out by the second one, I had Finn and Lauren in my sight for half the workout, and Jennifer on my tail for the last half, both of which kept me going. Like Monday's workout, I tried to prevent my slowing by getting up on my toes, which will hopefully help come saturday.

I used a heat pad and the Stick and tried to run in the morning again, but it was too painful, and so after a mile and a half I finished up with 20 min on the elliptical. In the afternoon, though, after a heat and foam roll session, I had no problem running to Jamaica Pond with everyone else, minus a couple of sporadic incidents of pain.
extras: starts, handoffs, and... steeple drills! I can't wait for outdoor!

comments: A good race, effortwise and mentally, but the fact is that I'm out of racing shape, on account of my trip. Not too much I can do about that, except keep running and hope I catch up in time to run a qualifier, although in all likelihood, my best chance is in the 5k.

After going to Killington for the day, I did a late-night elliptical session to hopefully loosen up and prevent some soreness... although it was pretty icy and I think a little bruising is unavoidable
Yuck, this morning's fluffy snow turned to gross slush and we were pretty much wet and gross for the whole run. My knee was a little sore in the colder weather but it was ok

Woke up to big flakes of snow falling and decided to try another morning run. This time, I heated, used the stick, then did a few minutes on the elliptical to make sure I was warmed up, which worked well, my knee didn't hurt at all. I ran out past River St. and then took a small detour to Trader Joe's, and ran the last mile back carrying a small amount of groceries.

Everything about today was bad from the start. We were supposed to do 4x800 then 6x400, with 200 rest between the 8's, 400 between sets, and 100 between the 4's, with the times being sub-2:50 and sub 82, which seemed hopelessly optimistic to all of us, but I thought I could do it, even though I was still a bit sore from Sunday. We warmed up with a BU loop, and I started to feel the IT band a bit toward the end, but it seemed to go away with stretching an strides so I started the workout. It was a mess from the get-go, we were never in the window. 2:54, 2:59, 3:09, 3:08. Everyone else was slow too, I was behind Lauren on the first two and then got passed by Karen and Christa. After the rest, I regrouped a bit and managed to get back on Lauren's heels, running 85 then 84, before falling apart again and running an 89. 120 meters through the 4th one, though, my IT band flared up and I dropped out. I could barely walk on it, and stretched out some more before cooling down 12 minutes on the elliptical. I went to the trainers and rolled and ice-cupped afterwards, hopefully it was just a factor of me having not warmed up enough beforehand. Ugh, pretty frustrating.

I made it to River St. before my knee really started hurting again. I turned back early, but soon had to start walking. I went to the pool when I got back, but even aquajogging hurt a bit when I bent up. I think I'm gonna take it easy for the rest of the week

I had planned on going to the pool yesterday already, but I still went to the trainers and ahead of time to chat. This time I did ultrasound and some massage in addition to the heat and foam rolling, then went to the pool. I foam-rolled and iced after the workout as usual, and will cross train tomorrow also, so I can race thursday.
extras: steeple drills. They're going well and I feel more flexible, although I lost my form on the skipping drills pretty badly

comments: Went to the trainers for heat/ultrasound/massage/foam roll again before going to the pool. It was a little sore today, but possibly from the massage more than the tendon itself.

What a crazy day. I was pretty keyed up and feeling good about today, and was in the same mood when I started the race. The IT band hurt a little bit at the tail end of the warmup, but felt ok during strides. It hurt again at the beginning of the race, but went away within 3 or 4 laps. I settled into a good pace early, and after a Colby girl passed me about 1000m in, I was able to tuck in right behind her and just not think for a while, while she pulled me along at pretty consisent 47's (hey, I hate when people do it to me, but she made the move). Just over a mile into it, Coach convinced me that I could catch Lauren, so I picked it up a bit, passing Colby and throwing in a surge or two over the course of a lap in order to shake her. And then... The fire alarm went off. They tried to keep the race going while evacuating the rest of the field house, but just over two miles into the race, we were pulled off the track. Long story short, the race ended then and there, they gave us points based on the places we were in at that instant, and of course, no times. It was as fair as they could do and I was given 4th place. There are a lot of woulda/couldas and what if's that I could play out from today. I felt really good today; we were closing in on the Tufts girl and maybe the places would have changed, and I was on pace for something in the 19:40's, maybe even a D3 qualifier. But hey, maybe I would have crashed or aggravated my ITB in that last mile. All in all, what happens happens, and if a squirrel chews through the wires in a transformer and starts an electrical file, that's the way the cookie crumbles. I had no more events after the 5k, and when the meet resumed 2 hours later, I had a good time cheering for everyone and watching one of the most incredible team performances I've seen in a long time. There were PR's and big pull-throughs all around, and it was a good meet.
30 minutes of lap swimming and 30 minutes aquajogging.

Warmed up with heat, ultrasound and foam roll today, then decided to run grass loops in case any problems came up, but none did! No pain at all today, during the run or during strides. I'm going to do tomorrow's workout and then crosstrain Wednesday., keeping the mileage low for the rest of this season, and then building back up for outdoor.

It was definitely wrong of me not to be afraid of this workout beforehand. We had 2x800, 6x400 and 6x200, with 400 rest between sets, 200 rest between the 8's, and 100 rest between everything else (it ended up being about equal time for the 4's). The high end of the window was 2:50, 84, and 38, respectively. The reason I didn't think it was going to be that bad was because two of the 800's had "turned into" 200's, and, as a distance runner, I always assume I can't run fast enough to make a 200 hurt, and see it as a long stride. But by the time I got to them, I was already hurting pretty badly (from the workout, not my ITB), and they were far from pleasant. Some of it might have been the fact that we lifted yesterday, but with all the target times faster than I've run ANYTHING this season, I haven't felt that dead after a workout in a long time. It went well though, and we mostly hit the times. Starting with the 400's, Karen and I traded leads (Lauren and Jennifer pulled ahead to form their own group, and Christa dropped back), which, as always, helped immensely.I think I did a pretty good job about staying aggressive and keeping my form even when it became not easy, and not falling off when Karen led. Times: 2:49, 2:55; 82, 84, 83, 83, 85, 83; 38, 38, 38, 39, 38, 38
25 min swim, 35 min aquajogging, although the jogging wasn't my hardest effort

woohoo, 5 days without any pain! Today and yesterday I skipped the ultrasound and did only heat and foam roll, and it was ok anyway. Longfellow-Western with a big bunch, including the 800 runners, Betsy and Andrea. I'm really glad I was running today, because it was ridiculously nice out, above 40 degrees
extras: steeple drills, lifting. today was a very good day on the steeple drills--somehow things just worked out well. I've been stretching a lot, which is good I guess, but I was just much more on today, even on the skips I had decent (although far from perfect) form, keeping my knee up and such

comments: everyone's doing sorority stuff tonight, so I ran on my own around noon. It was raining but warm today (53), so i decided to run in shorts, figuring it wasn't cold enough to bug the ITB. Turned out to be fine, again no pain.

Phew, well I hit the Div 3 qualifier, although not in the most efficient manner. The race went out way faster than I expected, and even though I sort of wanted to drop back a bit and save myself some anguish toward the end, I couldn't see the field behind me and didn't want to risk being stuck in no man's land, although by the middle of the race Karen and I were sort of floating there anyway. It was the right move, though, I think, because after us two, there was Christa 7 seconds back, then no one for almost a minute. Anyway, as a result, my splits were a bit in the ugly range: 6:03, 6:20, 6:30, but it did the job, and hopefully I can manage something better in two weeks. Karen and I worked together for most of the race; I led her the first mile, not knowing where she was, then she took 2 laps, I took 2, she took 2, but by the end of the second mile she was pulling away a bit and I wasn't able to get back up and help her. After another 2 laps though, the gap never widened, so maybe I could have stuck there, my attempts to get up weren't happening. As a result of my fast start, I didn't have much when I tried to pick it up for the last 4 laps, but I did manage a 39-second last lap when I ran by the clock at 18:51 and panicked that I wouldn't make it. A good day overall, I think there's more in me with a smarter race though. Splits: 1:30, 3:02, 4:31, 6:03 7:37, 9:10, 10:46, 12:23 14:00, 15:37, 17:16, 18:51 19:30.67

beautiful day for a run, low 50's and sun. Just ran down Mass Ave into the South End, then turned right on Columbus to hop on the walking path that goes past Northeaster and the Reggie Lewis center. Windy on the way out, which slowed me down a bit. Still no issues with the ITB though
another great day out. we left at 3:30 and went out the emerald necklace, finishing on the way back at BU, to watch the men's meet.
extras: 25 min swimming in the morning... it seemed to really help me feel awake

comments: We did the workout at BU today to give our legs a break and run a strong workout. Jennifer and I both had class until 5 (first day of school) so we didn't get going until around 5:15, and consequently had a bit of a short/rushed warmup. That, combined with some stomach discomfort made the workout kind of unpleasant generally, and even by the time it was over I didn't ever really feel good or fresh. Same workout as last week, but with 2 less 400's, which made the whole thing a lot more pleasant, even though I was running alone this week, feeling junky, and hitting pretty much the same times 2:51, 2:56; 82, 84, 83, 83; 6x37
meh, kind of a just messing around day... my ITB was tight, so I figured it'd be good to cross train, but I didn't work very hard in the pool

We were trying to avoid crossing any brindges, so karen took me on a loop from Kendall Sq, to Broadway, to Harvard, and then back along the river via DeWolfe St. The GMaps Pedometer gives it as 5.75 . It was not a bad loop.
extras: 20 min swimming AM, steeple drills, which seem to be improving every week.

comments: went in the morning so I could watch some of the team run at BU in the afternoon. It was pretty chilly and windy but just a relaxed premeet so no matter. Later on, I got to see Finn run a nice 5k in the 17:50 range, along with Jen and Lauren running 10:34 and 10:50-something in the 3k, and Hana at 5:18 in the mile. Nice showings, still more to come

I'm happy with today, it was kind of just for fun and for me, rather than trying for a time or qualifier or anything. Ended up finishing third behind Wellesley and Karen Condon; I didn't realize I was that close up front. I don't know if I would have tried harder or not though had I known. I felt like I was working things pretty hard but it's deceptive to drop down in distance that much. I felt really strong and in control the whole race, but as soon as I crossed the line I realized that despite the fact that I picked 3-4 people off in the last half of the race, I hadn't gone my absolute hardest. Karen and I did a long cooldown after that and I was recovered pretty quickly. In any case, being only 3 seconds off my PR gives me confidence that I am as fit as I ever was, and I'm pumped for next week. 83, 2:46, 4:12, 5:37
extras: 20:29 wu, 17:02 cd... extend both a bit, especially the warmup, since the event was so short

comments: woke up to a blizzard this morning, and on account of my being extremely lazy, I didn't get out the door until 11:15. The news report just before then said that there were already 9 inches, but they were plowing a lot and cars were driving so I figured it would be ok. turns out it was a good choice, better than being inside, and other than being slowed down by some of the deeper drifts, it was a pretty good run. I wore the yaktraks, which helped a lot, and my ski goggles for the wind, and had no problem at all. it made me happy to be outside, a tow truck driver even yelled "that's devotion" to me, which made me smile. Lots of people on XC skis out though, would have liked to mess around on a pair of those.

6x400 (77-81) w/100m rest, 6x600 (36-38) w/100m rest. 10:00 full recovery between the two. My legs were really tired, even on my warmup strides, maybe from running the snow the last two days. I don't know, the whole workout was just really forced, although I mostly hit the times. During the workout, for some reason I thought that we only had 4x400, and so I was a bit off mentally on #5, but I got it back around. 79, 81, 79, 81, 83, 81; 36, 37, 38, 39, 37, 37

footbridge-harvard. My legs were feeling beat so I dropped back from Lauren and Karen and took it easy on the way back. I didn't really feel like working hard today, especially at this point in the season, and my HR was up a little this morning
wanted to pool today, but there was a swim meet and i was too lazy to go to the alumni pool, so I'll do it tomorrow as a pre-meet
I'll get to the race review later... but long story short, I bombed pretty badly, maybe due to being sick, I don't know. DFL
Considering how low my mileage has been, I decided that yesterday would be the extent of my between season break. I ran with Kevin over to the aquarium to check out the seals, which was exciting.

Fresh Pond, back through Harvard Sq. with Kevin and one of our classmates, Bob. We were moving pretty well for me, although I'm sure they were doing fine. It was fun to have different running partners though, and I felt good.

Finished class at 3 today and headed out toward the arboretum. Nice a sunny, a great day to get back into the long run habit, even if this one wasn't that long. A little sluggish from going faster yesterday, but no matter

extras: steeple drills, adding in lead leg/trail leg drills in. My right lead leg is good, the left needs work, and both trail legs are a little off

comments: ran two miles over to BU to watch Adrienne tear it up in the 1000m at All-NE's (6th place, 3:00.6x), then took a long route back to make it 5

I thought for some reason that Harvard-No. Beacon was more like 11.75, but I'm not gonna split hairs over it. It was windier than I expected, and I spent about 2/3 of the run with the warmth blowing right out of me and the wind slowing me down. Plus there was a padding of snow from yesterday on the ground, and I don't think I was going much faster than 8:30 pace at all, but no big deal

another super cold day, gah. Started practice again for outdoor, so I ran with Lauren and Karen to Jamaica Pond. I forgot my watch but according to Karen's it was just under 8 min pace the whole way, although it felt a bit faster

second day in a row with major stomach discomfort... I think it's the gouda cheese that I've been putting in my sandwich, even though that's 5 hours before practice. ugh, definitely skipping that tomorrow
extras: lifting: chinups, shoulder press, lunges, romanian dead lift, calf raises-- I'm gonna be sore tomorrow

comments: ran on my own earlier in the day because I had a meeting with Coach about website stuff at 5, after which I did my steeple drills.
extras: steeple drills-- gotta get that trail leg squared away

comments: I'm switching tomorrow with today, since I plan to go to snowboarding tomorrow and won't be able to put in a long run. It makes this week pretty hefty mileage-wise, but I'm really only going to run like 3-4 mi tomorrow, so I'll have the rest I need. Anyway, ran to the Arboretum again so that I could stay on the dirt whenver I could, and intended to wander around in there for 20 min or so. I ended up with closer to 28 minutes in the park itself, making the run about a mile longer than I intended. The nice thing about long runs in the cold, though, is that you don't feel the same dragging on feeling like you do in hot weather. In fact, today was the best weather in about a week or so, low-mid 30's, sun, and relatively little wind.

after a GREAT day of snowboarding, came back and hopped on the treadmill for a quick 3.5 mi, since I didn't feel like getting all dressed warmly to go that short. Started off around 8:30 pace but moved down to just over 7:30 by the end.

river-sci museum, 2mi warmup, 4 mi fartlek (800m segments), 1 cd. Adrienne has joined me in the steeple group, but I lost focus on the second repeat and dropped back from her and Lauren. I kept them in sight though, until the very last one or so. Pretty good workout, just tried to get up on my toes and such
extras: steeple drills-- the trail leg is *slowly* improving, I just have to focus on keeping the knee up, which feels much higher than it actually is

comments: ran to the aquarium with Steph and Karen... our group seems to disintegrate a lot. From there we wandered through Boston into Chinatown and back via the Commons and the Esplanade. It was a bit sluggish and the whole thing was probably just shy of 8, but yesterday's run was a bit long, so it's good
extras: lifting--I'm up to 4 chinups on the first set! Followed by 3, then 3 with help.

comments: Tuesday is my long lunch day, so I put in an easy run at 11am, after my first class

fresh pond, back via harvard sq. Looks like the gmaps pedometer can save routes now, which is pretty cool. Here's where we went . Lauren, Karen, Adrienne and I were moving pretty well, but it felt good. I'm a bit sore from yesterday though

3x5:00 tempo. despite being a little sleepy in class before the workout, I felt pretty strong today. Lauren and Finn went on their own, so I was with Adrienne, Karen and Steph. I got to the front on the first one and pretty much maintained the lead for the whole workout (although Adrienne got a bit confused at a traffic light and I lost her completely on the last one). Of course, 3 repeats is a pleasant change from the 4x5:00 or 5x4:00 that I was doing in indoor, too

everyone was beat by the end of this week, myself included. We ran to Bunker Hill then took a 10-15 min stretch break while Betsy and Finn climbed the monument, ran over to the Consitution then back the same way, pretty relaxed
extras: lifting... the chinup count dropped a bit but I was still able to to 4 on the first set (plus a 5th with help)

comments: dropped off a pset and swung by LaVerde's in the midst of an easy 3

another beautiful day... today and yesterday were both shorts and t-shirt weather, and I am loving it. Today's run totally had a spring/outdoor track feel to it. I was really happy and it rounded out a solid week

drove out to Newton with Finn, starting our run near Uncle Ira's house. We ran to/around the Chestnut Hill reservior, then back out to the fire station. A nice cool day, but warm enough for shorts/t-shirt still, perfect long run weather. I felt like we were going pretty relaxed, but measuring the route we were about 7:50 pace the whole way, which is comforting

This week is going to be an intense week of workouts; a lot of volume and some hard runs. Today was a fartlek, 800m repeats like last Monday, but the fartlek part was supposed to be 5-6 mi long, as opposed to our normal 3-4 or 4-5. We headed out toward and around Jamaica Pond, running the dirt way which is a little longer. I was on my own pretty much from the second pickup on. I'm not sure why I'm feeling so good right now. Of course, on the other hand, once you're running by yourself, it's hard to tell if you're still going fast or not. Anyway, I total up 41:28 of the fartlek part, so between 5 and 5.5.
extras: "10-12" drills. Basically, jumping off the barrier into the grass, the objective being your first step is 10ft away and the second is 12, which is a little difficult jumping from the lower women's barrier.

comments: longfellow-JFK plus some around campus to try to make it 8, but we were maybe a little shy. sunday's run was longer than 11.5 though, so I'll call it even. I'm still having a bit of a cramp in my right hamstring, it started Sunday at the end of the run but it's subsiding now
wound through charlestown then boston with Adrienne; we ended up wandering a little further/longer than I intended. The run was probably closer to the 9 end
a 7 mile run, then a mile over hurdles on the track. I wish I had learned hurdling correctly when I started...

8x800, 200 rest, window 2:55-3:10. I was on my own for this one, Steph is cross training and Adrienne does 1500 workouts. But the 5k/10k group was doing 5x1600 at the same pace, essentially, so it worked out that I was able to start every other interval with them, and thus have someone to run with or chase half the time. The workout went well: 3:02, 3:06, 3:03, 3:04, 3:01, 3:04, 2:58, 2:58. The rests were all about 72-76. It was a pretty windy day and a little cool, I was in tights and a long sleeve and gloves. I'm pleased with how it went, especially considering that my legs didn't feel the greatest after standing up for a couple hours today in the machine shop. I was definitely pretty beat afterwards though, and wasn't quite myself until I sat down for a while and got some food and drink in me.

I'm switching Saturday and Sunday again this week, because Saturday was a more convenient day for a long run. I could count my weeks Monday to Sunday to avoid this, but once racing season starts, this works a lot better, plus I get to get my big mileage out of the way early. Anyway, drove to Walden Pond with Finn and Karen. The three of us were pretty beat from yesterday (they did 5xmile), so we took it easy, which is why I think we were short of 10 miles. It was pretty cold out today again, but a nice (and pretty) run nonetheless
a very easy, flat 3 mi. part of "last week's" mileage

ugh, cold and windy today. The mileage is supposed to drop a little this week, so we only had 4-5 mi of fartlek again, we pretty much did 4. I got out with Lauren and Finn today, so I wasn't totally on my own. I was able to hang for a while, dropping back only slightly from Lauren on the 3rd, with Finn right on my heels. On the very last one I couldn't stick with the two of them anymore, but overall a good workout

Much nicer weather than yesterday, definitely could have gone in shorts. THe sun was out and it was a beautiful morning. Plus, special guest star Ms. Julia Espel came along with me today, which was fun

8x800 again, this time the window dropped to 2:55-3:00. Amazingly, though, I crushed the workout, even though the timing this week made it such that I only started about 3 intervals with someone else. I don't know what it was that made me feel so great--I guess it was warmer (T-shirt and shorts) and a lot less windy than last week, plus I think having already done it once, it wasn't so daunting and I knew I could handle it. The guy steeplers that did the same workout had a similar report. 2:56, 2:54, 2:57, 2:55, 2:56, 2:56, 2:55, 2:52.

ugh. sometime between this morning and this afternoon the weather got colder and my legs got more and more tired. I barely made it through 7 miles to Jamaica Pond and back, dragging ass the whole way, wondering how I was going to make it through mile over hurdles AND lifting. Once I got back, I dragged ass a little more and it took me 20 minutes or more to get outside and get the hurdles set up. I did the mile ok, some good ones, some bad ones, then came back inside for some half-hearted lifting. last night was a long night
a bit stiff from going straight to a test after last night's workout and sitting for two hours, but I felt much better toward the end so hopefully that will help in the afternooon
didn't really need to run much today, as far as the mileage total was concerned, but I'm going to take tomorrow entirely off in preparation for the training trip (!) in Florida. Yay spring break!

Our first day in Clermont, FL for our spring break training trip. We started the workout about 10:30 am, about 80 degrees out. It was hot but I was definitely up for having the sun on my back. 1x1600, 400 rest, 4x800 w/200 rest and 5:00 after, 4x400. The workout was originally 5x800, 5x400, but we modified it so that both Lauren and I would have someone to run with. Today, though, she wasn't feeling well and I was on my own after the 2nd 800 anyway. I felt good and even though the workout was hard and I was dragging the cooldowns out a bit, I felt strong and in control of my body, as in my legs were doing what I wanted them to. Good to know last week wasn't a fluke. The wind picked up a bit around the time of my 2nd 800. 5:54; 2:56, 2:56, 2:59, 2:59; 80, 83, 88, 81.
extras: lifting...the NTC didn't really have the facilities Coach Brooks wanted, so we did a circuit of all the weight machines

comments: ran on the bike path that starts at the NTC. Unfortunately, all of our distance runs are going to happen here, and it's kind of a boring path. Just a paved road that pretty hilly and in the sun.
extras: hurdle drills and 10-12's... didn't have my spikes for the latter so they went only ok. I am improving on the hurdle drills though.

comments: This meet ended up being pretty small, with mostly just Colby and USM in the distance races. It was also pretty poorly run; the water pit was not even full by the time the race was supposed to start, so it was delayed about 45 minutes. No matter though, it was warm so I just put my legs up and waited. This track also had an outside water barrier, not that it really makes difference, but it was new, I guess. There were only 3 women in the steeple and even though I didn't want to be trying to lead from the gun, there was a pretty big gap in abilities so I just went out and tried to push myself. I did pretty well coming through the first mile, but as I got tired I was apparently looking down (especially at the barriers a lot). I tried my best to stay aggressive though, and ended up with a pretty pleasing season opener, only 6 seconds off my PR on a solo effort. Splits: 88, 2:57 (89), 4:27 (90), 5:58 (91), 7:35 (91), 9:14 (97), 10:51 (96), 11:41.38
race_name: Spring Fling Meet, NTC, Clermont, FL

comments: another run on the bike path, followed by 8x200. The entire 8x200 was supposed to happen in 8:20, including rest, but there was no way that was going to pull. did the track work on my own again and ran: 35, 39, 36, 38, 38, 38, 37, 37 for a total time of about 15:00. Mostly my legs are just pretty shot from an intense first half of the week, but my body feels ok. Was going to do an afternoon run but Coach Taylor said I didn't need to

standard meet warmup. the schedule said 6-7 miles, but again we had the option of cutting short and freshening up, which I took. The mileage is going to be a bit short this week since I skipped Sunday's run, but we've worked so hard that it's no big deal.

A nice way to end a good week. The meet was a non-scoring event between mostly D1 Florida teams, and in a lot of events we were closer to the back of the field, but Marzouk from USM was in the race and I was hoping to at least hang with her and avoid another last place. Again the meet was delayed and I warmed up a little early, but that was my fault. The gun went off and after 100m I was settled at the back of the field, but we were moving pretty quickly so I just kind of kept in there. By the time we came around the 400 I was about 3rd from last and comfortable with the pack I was in. I decided to just keep cruising and not worry about if my splits were fast, because it was a lot easier to just tuck in and follow (especially after doing so many workouts alone). I started to get a little nervous after we came through the first mile in 5:48, but I just kept following Coach Taylor's instructions, one lap at a time, to either stay put or move up. Somewhere just after that point, I thought to myself, "I'm doing 60 mpw and working just as hard as most of these girls, there's no reason I can't run with them." Just around then, the pack I was in broke up, with Marzouk going with a couple of girls up ahead.
