Taylor's instruction this time was to stay with the pack I was in (so that I wouldn't crash later, which was fine with me) although after a lap I moved to the lead of that pack and then surged a bit to keep them from following me. Coming up on the 2 mile mark I was still feeling pretty fluid and I still had people to run with, but soon after that I was not only alone but getting tired. My splits pretty much reflect that, but I kept telling myself that I didn't want to get passed (or last), and tried to stay in it. With two laps to go I really tried my best to pick it up a bit, but I was just hoping it would be over soon. I guess there was some semblance of a kick, looking at the splits. I missed D3 qualifying by 0.50 seconds, which is a bummer, but I think with this experience I can manage sub-19 next time I get in a good race. One thing I'm still having trouble with is getting really flat-footed when I get tired, hopefully that will eliminate my 96+ second laps. But I'm not upset with how the race went at all, and I'm glad I can finally record a PR again. Splits: 84, 2:52 (88), 4:20 (90), 5:48 (88), 7:20 (92), 8:52 (92), 10:25 (93), 12:01 (96), 13:37 (96), 15:15 (1:42, yuck), 16:52 (97), 18:24 (92), 19:04.71 (40)
race_name: Diet Pepsi Invitational, University of Florida

comments: What a crappy day for a workout... it was super windy for most of each lap (Boston is magic like that, a loop can be more than 50% windy), especially down the backstretch. It even snowed during one of my intervals. As a result, everyone's times were slow and it was hard to tell how well I was doing, but Coach seemed to approve. I mostly just tried to focus on looking up and staying on my toes, and shortening my stride into the wind. I don't really like workouts like this because you work really hard but you never feel fast. Anyway, I ran 5x800 (200 rest), 5x400 (100 rest) with 5:00 in between: 2:55, 2:58, 2:59, 2:59, 2:59; 86, 88, 90, 87, 89

River St/Science Museum easy with Becky; everyone on the team seems to run so fast on recovery days. Oh well, my pace seems to be working for me so... Geez, it frickin snowed this morning! I wish I was still in Florida
ran by the Green Building to see the cannon that we apparently stole from Caltech, then through Cambridge
extras: mile over hurdles. they get a lot better when I don't look down. Lifting

comments: A really cold day out--it never really topped 40 the whole time we were at the meet. Made for some awesome steepling, right... actually the steeple went pretty well. I was really solid on the water jumps and managed to get only my left foot/leg wet the whole way through, which made things a bit more bearable. But I could tell that the cold water was tightening me up, because anytime I tried to hurdle with my left leg, things were not happening. I think my hurdle form was generally improved, though, at least I didn't stutter as much and tried to keep my head up. Unfortunately, on the last water jump, I took the first steeple spill of my career. I can't remember really how it happened but I was all the way in the water, which cost me maybe 8-10 seconds between the getting up and the reduced speed post-wetness. I'll look at the splits to see where I was with 200 to go. It was bound to happen eventually, and now I'm glad to not have the inevitability hanging over my head. Adrienne, on the other hand, must have negative inevitability after managing to go into the water 4 times... but she still busted a 12:15. I think I might have had to fear for the win if she managed to stay on her feet. The 800 was mostly a workout, to develop my speed (which I don't have). The cold got to me again here a little bit, although in this case it was just muscle tightness rather than actually numbness due to having 35 degree water on me. I tried to glue onto the tail end of our MIT pack, and did ok, running just a few seconds back of Hana. Steeple splits: 88, 3:00 (92), 4:32 (92), 6:07 (95), 7:48 (91), 9:27 (99), 11:05 (98), 11:56 (51) 800: 75, 83

reservior +summit, plus some adding on
mm...warming up...yay....
grass loops, i was pretty stiff/slow this morning

4x800, 6x400, rest 200, 8:00, 100, window 2:52-2:56, 82, 86. I still don't understand why I don't do the same workouts as the 5k/10k group, because my windows are the same, I usually just have about 800m to a mile less of workout. anyway, I'm not complaining. So the warming trend is sticking so far, and the lack of wind made today's workout much more pleasant than last. I also had company on all the intervals, and today went really well overall. I had no trouble hitting mostly the bottoms of the windows, and wasn't really straining much. I also threw down a random 76 for my first 400 after the break.... it was kind of an accident and I wasn't looking at my watch and I thought I had to work that hard. The next 400 after that was a little rough but after that no problem. Traded leads with Karen most of the time. 2:51, 2:55, 2:52, 2:54; 76, 84, 84, 83, 83, 81
Legs were a little tired/heavy... yesterday's workout didn't feel to bad doing it, but oof. Maybe I didn't have a sufficient recovery meal last night
extras: hurdle drills-- did them early because of Passover, so I didn't have anyone watching, but I think got my left lead leg a lot better, by taking off a little earlier.

comments: I was way out of it before the run, but I felt a lot better once we got going. My legs were not as bad today
extras: Mile over hurdles (about half of them what'd I'd consider good) and lifting

comments: Now the legs are tired from lifting yesterday... just taking today easy and I should be good tomorrow.

Woo! What a day. A tie for a steeple PR, followed by my first 800m PR since...9th grade! I had been a little concerned all week since school's in full swing now and I've been tired a lot lately, but today was really a confidence booster. I had my choice between steeple and 5k today and opted for the steeple, hoping that at least one of the fast steeplers from Colby or Bates would be in it, but both already have NCAA qualifying times, so they didn't really need to, and as I sort of expected, they didn't. In any case, the same steepler from Colby who I raced over spring break was there, and unlike then, she was right on my shoulder today. My plan, though, was to race through the first mile on pace, since that has been relatively managable the last two times, and then 200m later, coming around with 3 laps to go, I wanted to really dig in. So I went for it today, throwing in a surge with 1200m to go, and managed to gap Colby a bit. I had a feeling she wasn't expecting a move, so I really went for it, and managed to keep the lead through to the finish. Today was an near-excellent day technique-wise; I was really attacking the hurdles (teammates even said they noticed), and barely stuttered at all. I had one little slip on the first water jump, nearly two-footing it, but didn't go down and caught right back up on the next hurdle. Next week will probably sans barriers, but I'm really pumped about going after a NEWMAC win and an All-New England qualifier in a couple of weeks. I don't have much to say about the 800, since it's not like I was in it to place anyway. I went out in the back of the race but moved up a place or two and came through in 74 (I think), trying to stay up on the front of my feet like I've been working on. around the 500m mark I picked it up and passed a few more people, but got passed back by some later. A 2:34 though, that's not bad for someone like me. Splits: Steeple - 89, 3:02 (93), 4:36 (94), 6:08 (92), 7:10 (92) , 9:15 (95),10:51 (96), 11:35 800 - 74, 79

out to Jamaica Pond, plus some wandering around the trails just below the pond. Didn't know there was so much down there, I didn't even see half of it. Beautiful day out, just cruising

No school for Patriot's Day, so we started in Kenmore Sq. where we were watching the Boston Marathon, then ran back (away from the finish) along Beacon St. for a bit, before wandering back to campus for strides and whatnot. It was pretty riduculous...this is the third year in a row I've watched the marathon but I've never left Kenmore...it's not until you get out further from MIT kids that you realize the extent of the public drunken-ness throughout Boston. I mean, people everywhere were drunk, open bottles and red cups in the street, happy and enjoying their purposeless Monday off of work and school. And of course, since we were running, we got a lot of high-fives and hurrahs, and half a dozen different groups yelled at us "You're going the wrong way!" It was pretty fun
extras: 10-12's... I tried going over with the left leg a couple of times, to avoid needing to stutter on the water jump. It could happen. Lifting too.

comments: 3x800, 4x400, 4x200, rest was 200, 8:00, 100, 8:00, 100. The windows were 2:48-2:50; 80-82, and 35-37... I didn't realize until the warmup just how daunting 2:48-2:50 was, that's 4 seconds faster than last week's window, but I guess the workout was a lot shorter. Anyway, Karen and I pretty much smashed the workout... it wasn't as smooth and "easy" as last week's, but we got the job done in a convincing manner. The 400's were getting pretty tough and required a lot of focus, and the 200's were a bit brutal on the hamstrings/glutes, but we hit the window, and mostly the bottom ends: 2:50, 2:48, 2:47; 79, 80, 80, 81; 36, 37, 36, 36
extras: 3mi wu, 1mi cd, ice bath. Also, I hopped on the scale right after the bath, I was down to a post-workout 123 lb. I'm guessing that's maybe 125-26 normally, but that's still a good 4-5 lb lighter than I've been in about 3 years. I don't know what that means, but it was a surprise.

comments: grass loops. we were all pretty beat up from yesterday, so loops sounded good.

extras: mile over hurdles. They're getting there, as long as I really make the effort to accelerate 15m out. The new thing to fix is having the lead leg come up bent.

comments: ran at 11:00, after my first class. I'm slighty more awake at this hour...

harvard-footbridge with a pre-frosh from CA... everyone else was getting ready for a sorority event and I'm technically my own training group anyway, so we went on our own. It was cooling off a bit today, we'll see how tomorrow is

Coach thought I could run 18:40, but my goal was just to hit 90 second quarters all the way through for an 18:45. The seeds indicated that the race might be a little thin, but I tucked in behind a girl for the first three laps, which we went through around 4:20, so under pace. The next lap dropped to a 91 or so, though, so I took off from her and pushed the pace a bit. Coming through the mile, Coach told me that the Wheaton girl ahead of me (I was in third) was coming back, and I looked up at her and decided that I'd catch her in the next mile. So for the next four laps, I just kept assessing the gap and telling myself "I only have x amount of laps left to catch her," until I did get her just before the two mile, at which point I started chasing the girl in first.I knew there was a chance that I would lose focus or motivation or whatnot after I caught the first girl, so I really made an effort to maintain the focus that I had for the first two miles. Admittedly, though, I didn't stay quite on top of things; I was looking down a little more and doing a little more "maintaining" than racing, but I held onto pace pretty decently. With 400 to go the girl I had passed earlier came up and started kicking, and I couldn't match it. I responded a half-second late, and once I got into my kick I gain a little bit back, but I think she had more finishing speed than me regardless. If I were to beat her, it would have to be by putting a bigger gap on her earlier. I put together a decent final 200 though, because the Stonehill girl I had been drafting off of earlier seemed to be right behind me and I didn't want to lose another place to her. Splits: 87, 2:55 (88), 4:24 (89), 5:52 (88); 7:21 (89), 8:51 (90), 10:20 (89), 11:49 (89); 13:22 (93), 14:53 (91), 16:26 (93), 17:57 (91), 18:40.80

extras: lifting, 10-12's, although I was a little cold by the time I got to them and they weren't very clean

comments: slept in and ran after my first class again. Took yesterday off; I like the 13 out of 14 days pattern, plus I was at video lab all day.

2x800, 3x400, 4x200, rest 200, 8:00, 100, 8:00, 100. Goals 2:46-2:52, 79-82, 34-37. Short workout today, it's weird to think we're tapering already. Anyway, the workout felt strong like last week, although again my legs were locking up a bit on the fast intervals: 2:45, 2:48, 77, 79, 79, 34, 35, 34, 36 My hip flexor tied up a bit on the last 200, and I backed off just before the finish, but I got up to my waist in the ice bath and it's feeling better now.
a little stiff, sore from yesterday, but otherwise uneventful
no morning run, I'm down to one a week. I probably would have done it tuesday if I knew ahead of time, but no matter. also no lifting today

perfect weather out today, I hope it's like this tomorrow. An easy BU loop then a good job on strides, plus some very light hurdle stretching. I'm fired up about tomorrow, it'll be a competitive race with fast times

I knew the race was going to be won with a fast time, but I was hoping it would be me doing it. I also knew that the freshman from Smith had only ever run one steeplechase before, and that your second one is usually significantly faster. I wasn't quite prepared, though, for the race to go out in 80 for the first quarter (even with two less barriers), and I was quite surprised to hear my first split. I had been trying to stay on the Smith girl up until that point, but when went by that split, I let her go and focused on my own pace and holding off the 3rd girl. All in all, even though the race was a PR and a strong effort, I didn't feel like I was 100% mentally, and I even felt like my hurdling technique was pretty sloppy, even though teammates and other people who came to watch told me otherwise. I even smacked my right knee on one of the barriers during a middle lap; I dropped the knee on my trail leg. It hurt pretty badly, but not really until after the race, but I managed to stay on my feet. I think I was compensating a bit the rest of the race, though, as my left calf got extremely tired. My water jumps were some of the cleanest I've ever had, though, and I did eventually end up cutting down Smith's lead, but I think she's just pretty fast overall and didn't die as much as I might have expected from going out that fast. She ended up with an 11:12, so I don't know if I would have won today even with better focus (I never truly made a committed effort to catch her), but next year (or maybe next week?) will be a fun rematch. Anyway, I bought myself another race with today's effort, which qualified me for All-New England's. Splits: 81, 2:50 (89), 4:22 (92), 5:58 (96) 7:31 (93), 9:08 (97), 10:45 (97); 11:28 (43)
started off real slow, I was tired from yesterday, but I got moving a bit. My knee is still a bit sore and the other leg got tired again, but I should be good tomorrow

extras: lifting, skipped the 10-12's to give the left leg a little more break

comments: kind of a worthless day... 6x400 (79-82), 4x200 (34-37) was on the schedule, but my left leg is still more tired than the right, as a result of the hurdle incident on Saturday, and I felt awkward and lopsided and unable to really do anything right. After getting dropped pretty handily on the first four, I took a break while Betsy and Karen did two more 4's, then hopped in after their rest for the 200's, which were also awkward feeling. Today didn't do much for me fitness-wise, I don't think, but I guess it's giving me confidence that I can still run that fast when I'm off my game physically and mentally, and let me know that I'm ready for Saturday. 76, 81, 82, 82; 36, 38, 38, 39
extras: ice bath, 2 mi wu and a really short cool down

comments: Took the day off to give the legs a break, there's no sense in forcing mileage at this point in the season
extras: hurdle drills, which were actually pretty good...

comments: my leg felt great on strides today, and the sun came out, and I'm ready to roll Saturday
drove up to Springfield in the afternoon since there were Friday events, ran a short one with Karen then put in good strides

Well I can't pretend not to be disappointed, not so much with the time, but mostly with the fact that I dropped from my seed of 3rd to 6th, missing out on a shirt a second time and losing to three girls who I've never lost to before. The race pretty much went exactly as I imagined it, with top three at or under 11:20, except I wasn't in there. From the gun I was forced into the lead for the first mile, which I didn't want or think would happen, but I tried to keep it so that I still felt relaxed and wasn't being used. Easter from Colby was up on my shoulder again, just like at Bates, but a little closer to me on the hurdles and I spent the first 4 laps fighting a lot, which in retrospect wasn't a good idea. I wanted to have a good position on the water jumps, but I guess next time that happens I should let someone take the lead and drop back a step until I want to make a move. With three laps to go, Moore from Bates and Marzouk from USM made a move on Easter and I, and I tried to use that to tag along with them. But I just couldn't move today; I'm pretty sure it wasn't physical though, it was just one of those days when my mind is trying to get me to do something and my body won't do it. I can try to pick a few things that may have made it a bad race--a hard start (although I've started faster), the fighting in the first mile, a bad week of schoolwork and not enough sleep--but I don't really know. I'm not super-devasted or anything about the race, it was just kind of a bummer that I came up just short of top 3 again, and that I couldn't grab a few more points for the team, which stepped up big time. But I have one more week to turn things around and take advantage of the fitness i know I have.

Most of the team is done and Betsy couldn't get out before our 5:15 meeting, so I went on my own. Nothing special, just ran through the Fens a bit, thinking about how this weekend might play out. I'll write this one down as 5 in the total, because once in a while I'm sure we come up short.

4x400, 8x200, rest 100, 10:00, 100. Windows were 77-80, 34-36, but it was raining and cold and the wind was blowing pretty hard today (the workout was actually originally 6x4 and 6x2, but since we can run 200's with the wind, Coach changed it). I had Betsy to run with this time, since she became a 10k runner a few weeks ago, and we did pretty well trading off leads and working together. I was soaked through by the second 400 and decided to go without a shirt, even though it was a little cool out, because my clothes weren't keeping me warm. In any case, I think we put down some pretty respectable repeats, getting my confidence back for Saturday. 77, 77, 81, 82; 36, 36, 38, 39, 37, 37, 37, 37

extras: shortened hurdle drills. Looks like the slow heat for this Saturday is going to be a little slower than I thought, and I might be at the front instead of being dragged along like I hoped... oh well.

comments: grass loops, then some solid strides and foam rolling. Really tapering now

Tommorrow is going to be a yucky day. Heavy rain all day along with heavy winds and relatively cool temps. Yay for spring in New England. They actually made all the sprint events finals today, instead of prelims today and finals tomorrow, so that they wouldn't have to run in crap. Truth be told, I'm pretty fired up about tomrrow, especially after watching Leanne and Betsy both PR big time to score some points and earn medals, along with all the other PR's and top 8 finishes that the team busted today.

Well the weather was bad, as they expected. A total of nearly 4" of rain for the day, temperatures around 50 degrees, although not as windy as expected. It really didn't get to me mentally, although it might have limited my warmup a bit. The times were kind of meaningless today, but I ended up second in my heat and I think I managed to get the competitive focus back. Taylor commented afterwards that he thinks it was the best race of the season, and I think I'd have to agree, at least from the perspective if the technical aspects of the barriers, and from a strategy standpoint. The race went out with Easter from Colby jumping to the front, and I tucked in just behind two girls from Quinnipiac. The two of them spent the first mile trying to get in front/around me, just like I did last week, and I just tried my best to stay calm and just let the race play out, pulling ahead whenver a jump came off clean or whateer. It helped that my water jumps were all dead on; I gained a step on whoever I was with on every one. Just after the mile mark (we came through in 6:03), I decided to punch it. I remember thinking that if I sat and waited, I might never kick into the next gear, and it would just turn out like last week. By then a UNH girl was up in front with me, and we both went around Colby together, and traded leads for a bit. I tried my best to stay aggressive for the last three laps; I know there were times that I slowed a bit, but I think overall I stayed after it. At the end one of the Quinnipiac pair was up on me again, but I managed to hang on and just barely stay in front of her.

So I decided over the weekend to run ECAC's on Thursday, for one last shot at a PR and a national provisional mark (11:20). I know that isn't going to be good enough to go, but I'd be happy to get my name on the outdoor performance lists, for this year. I would have been content to stop after Saturday, my season has gone well enough, but since the conditions were so poor and I didn't really get a fiar shot to do what I know I'm capable of, I'd really like to run ECAC's. Anyway, today was a workout since the race is earlier in the week: 4x400, 10:00 rest, 4x200. The times were 75-78, 34-36, but before the workout Coach told me to just go with my rhthym and worry less about the times. I ended up with 78, 80, 79, 80 which wasn't quite what I wanted, but it felt smooth and I didn't want to go nuts with a race so soon. After the long rest I came back with good times for the 200's: 35, 36, 36, 36, although I'll admit they weren't all so easy.
the sun came out today! not until after my run though

extras: ice bath... monday was a little harder than it should have been, i think

comments: Meh, not much to say, I was a week past my peak. Mentally and physically, four important races in a row just wasn't going to happen, I guess. I tried to go for it, hit the first 3 laps ok, 4.5 laps I was still mostly on pace, but after that I just got tired... I mean really tired. I was having trouble being agressive on the barriers, even the water jumps, which were getting sloppy and I was starting to hit the ground hard. Oh well, I didn't get the provisional time, but all in all a good season, PR's in all the events I ran, and a great season for the team. Gonna rest up a bunch and get fired up for XC.
just an easy BU loop with Chi-Fong, who had just finished her last final. Wasn't going to run this week but I am easily swayed
extras: I can still do (one set of) 4 chinups, yay...

comments: another not-actually-training-yet jog, this time with my dad. I got home last night, I'm here all summer, so we headed over late morning to TP state beach for an easy one. North to the 17th st. lifeguard station and back

extras: a haphazard mix of strength work, just to ease into things. pushups, lunges, some core work on the SB

comments: The rec center now costs $5 a trip, so I don't see myself swimming too often this summer, but it felt good to get in the water

I'm going to count my "summer miles" June 1 to Aug 31 again, so I guess this is the official start, although I'm not going to bother with weekly mileage and stuff until next week. TP state beach, south to Guy Fleming, twice around that and back. Gonna try to get some nice hilly runs in this summer, and come back strong.
extras: strides, more random strength work. a little sore from Tuesday

comments: a short run with Deanna down in Mission Bay... she's running the Rock 'n' Roll Marathon this Sunday so we didn't go far

Ran from home, through the school and then over to the High Bluff loop, which I'm pretty sure is 5. Stopped twice to do chinups on some random bars around the neighborhood. Did a set of 4 then a set of 2 both times, although the second time was much easier. Gonna get back on track with these, maybe when I start work and can use the gym there. Today's loop was a little hilly, since it was around Carmel Valley, and took a little more out of me than I expected.
extras: chins, as mentioned. I was going to do more but after passing out for a 2 hour nap in the afternoon, I decided to go with the take-it-easy route

comments: my legs were pretty tired from yesterday so I went to the beach for relative flatness. the fog stayed on for the whole run so it was nice. julie reminded me later in the day how difficult running is for the first week or so when you get to SD, for whatever reason, so I'll just stick it out.
extras: random lifting/core, including lunges and pushups

comments: First real day of work, so I ran from home at about 6pm. It stayed cloudy all day today though, so it was nice and cool. I have two new pairs of shoes now, to last me until winter probably.
extras: Stopped for chinups during the run again. Got up to 5 on the first set (unassisted), then two sets of 2. Also lunges and other core stuff.

comments: park loop, into the canyon a bit, then around the fields at TP for a while. more than 4.5 but yesterday may have been short
extras: chinups, pushups, lunges, hamstring stuff. I got 5, 2.5, 2 on the chinups... I should start a separate chin-up log, considering how obsessed I am with it.

comments: parked at 20th so we could have a spot at the beach after the run, then ran south to Guy Fleming with Julie, then back to the car and then back to Board and Brew for lunch.
ran around Kearney Mesa after work, a relatively flat loop around Montgomery Field, plus some add on.

went out del mar heights rd to carmel valley dr, and followed it until I got back to what used to be black mountain rd. The strawberry stand is still there, although there are no strawberries... thought about trying to reconstruct the Rancho loop from there, but I think that might have been a 10 mile loop, so I just went back the way I came
ran at lunch today, the same loop around Montgomery Field as Monday

Went up Broken Hills from TP state beach, south to UCSD then in through their trails for a bit. maybe not the greatest idea to combine a hilly run with my first long run in over a month, but oh well. I developed a few blisters and whatnot, but overall it was good
dad had to wait and go into work a bit late, so I woke up a bit early and ran before work.
extras: lifting after work at the fitness center

comments: ran at lunch in a bit of a hurry today, didn't get to stretch out much or recover afterwards

extras: lifted during lunch at work, plus abs

comments: Jen was in town for the big day so I took a half-day and we went running at the beach. good times with good friends

Rancho Loop, modified for the new roads and whatnot, plus some detours. it was hot out today, and even though i didn't get out until 4:30 or so, I was still dripping and pretty thirsty by the end. It was also another hilly long run, probably more hills that last week, mostly because I didn't feel like driving somewhere to run. I was trudging a bit up some of the hills and my back's been bugging me, but i got through it ok

Sort of did the equivalent grass loops around here, not on purpose. Started with house loop, plus a detour on a short little dirt path they seem to have just put in. then park loop, and down into the stadium for a couple sets of chins. once around the school after that, another set of chinups, then a repeat of the first two loops. I was actually intending to do high bluff, but this just felt good.
ran at lunch, extending the 5-mile loop to clairemont mesa blvd to make it 6, plus some other additions
lunchtime run again... it was way hot and pretty hilly. not the most pleasant thing ever
Pilates at lunch... thought I was going to a Yoga class but I guess that's on Tuesdays. An exciting new thing nonetheless

After a long, kind of crappy day, I really felt like trying to turn my gut inside out on my run. Drove over to the beach around 6:30 p.m. and headed south for Broken Hills. As soon as I hit the bottom of the trail I decided I'd go for a trail PR, so I gunned it as soon as I was on solid footing. I was working at a moderately hard pace until just under halfway up I saw a group of three runners about two switchbacks ahead of me, and decided to catch them. I caught the first (or last?) one in a pretty short time, but by then the other two had split off and were a little ahead. They were actually going at a pretty hard pace and it took me until South Fork to get them, at which point I was content to just stay at their pace for a moment. (I was being friendly and talking to them once in a while, not just being creepy.) After the trail widened a bit I decided to go for it and pass them, and of course I'd look super lame if they passed me back so I pushed it pretty hard the whole rest of the way. The end result was definitely a new Broken Hills PR by a big chunk, 10:55. I think I had once run 11:20 or 30 something with Dani and Kim, before that. Took the rest of the run at a normal pace but I was feeling much better after the effort. Took a quick break to survey the sunset and the cliffs on Guy Fleming trail.... it was an amazing view. I can't even describe how good it makes me feel to look over the water and the cliffs. I know I'm spoiled rotten but I can't imagine living many other places ever...

fletcher cove north to swami's and back with Julie... was feeling a little tight but we kept moving ok until the very end when it started getting really hot.... nothing a quick dip in the ocean couldn't solve though :D

started at home, ran down to the beach (via the bike path, which took about 40 min for some reason; I guess it's longer) then up north to the little path across from the fairgrounds and back, stopping at Torrey Pines, where my parents had run and had the car. Not too bad of a run overall, I was moving ok even though it was getting a little hot

no work again today and by the time I woke up it was too hot to run, so I lounged around waiting for it to cool off. It didn't really, but I went over to the San Dieguito lagoon around 4 hoping there'd be some shade. It was ok, but really hot on the way back, and I was dragging.
extras: a light set of lifiting. feeling lazy but I didn't want to be sore next time I lifted

comments: quickly becoming my favorite 9 mile run: TP state beach south to guy fleming then back north to dog beach. a little sick of this heat wave... although it's not even really that hot, just super sunny.

park loop plus High Bluff loop, with some detours for chinups. My legs have been getting progressively more beat up, but looking at my runs starting from Friday, it's not surprising. With the long weekend though, I wanted to use the extra time to put in some miles, so I'm not worried. Took today slow, I'm going to keep building the miles this week, then have next week drop down a bit, since I'm traveling the next two weekends anyway. all is still well.
ahh, what sweet relief to have a day off. I felt soo much better today. still hot though and i hopped in the ocean after

put in a few miles with Meri after visiting her up in Hermosa Beach for a wine tasting. we ran casually, probably about 8:30 pace while we were together, then she stopped off early and i added another couple miles

decided to put in some distance since I'm not going to get a long run in with all the traveling. ran up to see if I could piece together green monkey loop, but failed again and turned back after just over 40:00 and came down the same way

Dad had a dr. appt so we were going to leave a bit later for work today, so I decided to wake up a little early (~6) and squeeze in a run in the morning so I could relax when i got home. a little stiff and the shock of hitting the roads so soon after waking up was definitely there, but it was nice and cool
what a crazy run... almost getting attacked by cacti, developing a strange bubble on my skin, and coming within 10 ft of a rattlesnake. anyway, I may stay away from that canyon for a while

went at lunch today since I'm heading to the airport in the evening... crap it was hot out. This was probably my best heat run, though, because I didn't feel like I was dragging or anything as a result of the heat, just uncomfortable.

ran murphy canyon at lunch. starting doubles this week, although today might be the only one. I may or may not also try to stick with 6-day-a-week training, but my goal for the week is around 55-60 so we'll see what I can do Monday-Thursday

extras: yoga at lunch... it was pretty intense and I could feel it on my run later

comments: Nice longer run today, I ran over Del Mar Heights Rd through the east side of TP state park and up north along the cliffs... luckily it was overcast and about 10 degrees cooler today. The cooler temps even brought out some animals; I saw a coyote and two quails on one of the trails. A little bit sore from yoga at the start, but I loosened up.
Considered running twice today to ease up my Friday and Saturday, but I don't have too many miles left and I'm a little tired, so I decided to skip the second run
Surprisingly, despite the heat and my long week with not entirely enough sleep, I was rolling today. Good sign.

Got to the beach before 7:30am today to try to avoid some of the worst of the record-setting temperatures. It helped, especially having the sun low enough to have some shade, but I still definitely had to swing by 2 or 3 water fountains to grab a sip and douse my head and ponytail with water. Started off north from TP state beach for 3 miles for a little warmup before I headed up the hill, then went south via Park Rd. (including Guy Fleming) to UCSD. Ran around the trails there for a bit to be on the dirt and in the shade, although that's actually where I got really hot and starting flagging a bit until I got to some water. After 2 miles around there, I headed straight back down to the parking lot, where I skipped strides and hopped straight into the ocean to cool off. I almost felt like I could have benefitted from those strides, but I couldn't take being out in the hot air anymore. Overall a good run, minus that one stretch.
house loop, park loop, then a couple loops around the school until it added up to 6
A lunchtime run rather than a morning run, but it was cool(er) and even sprinkling when I left at 11:30.
extras: bench, chinups. Yoga tomorrow, so I didn't want to overdo the leg/core stuff

comments: The pace suffered a bit today, it probably wasn't even quite 8.5 miles. I spent a chunk at the beginning sort of running/hiking/scrambling while I was exploring some trails over near the bike path, so that was slow, and then even when I got back on the normal bike path, I just wasn't moving. I don't feel tired during the day and my RHR is normal, so I figure it's just the effects of Sunday's run still, so I'm not concerned.
extras: yoga at lunch... went much better than last time.

comments: ah, legs were feeling a lot better today. Went out Del Mar Heights Rd. to the new hs, the only place I could think of in my neighborhood that was almost entirely flat. Wandered around there for a bit, then looked around the new Catholic high school on the way home... holy crap it's nice there. Incredible football stadium with a 9-lane, all-weather track, with the bleachers (on both sides) backed off a bit so that everyone has a good view. I guess even when you go to a rich kid public school, private schools have a lot more money. I guess I should go check out the Jewish academy next time I'm at the bike path...
put in my second run at lunch (instead of my first, ha). nice to be done with the mileage before I go home for the day
woke up a bit early to get a run in before work, already so hot out
woke up early again today, but this time this is the only run for the day. Nice stuff. I like waking up early; I'm more alert and less hungry all day.

Fletcher Cove to just shy of Swami's, with Julie. Both of us were not intent on moving very fast, which was fine. There was actually a light mist this morning, a nice break from the sunny heat that's been making San Diego feel like Boston. I feel really good about the way this week went, getting my mileage up over 60. I was feeling a little slow on Monday and especially Tuesday, but after an easy, flatter day on Wednesday, the rest of the week was good.

The heat wave has finally broken a little bit here, and I even woke up to hear rain on the roof. Went down to TP state beach with the intent of running north (and flat) after the 3-mile add-on to Guy Fleming. Once I got to the trail, though, I decided that going south would be more exciting, so I continued up the hill and over to UCSD to run in their trails again. On the way back, wandered through a lot of the state park trails to add on distance, enjoying the views. The cloud cover and mist stuck around for the whole run, making it much easier than last week.
extras: lots of core stuff on the SB

comments: pretty uninspired run today... not that I was particularly dragging or tired, but I just had nowhere really that I wanted to go. got home from work, sat around for a while, then just went out wandering.
Lunch run again, same deal as last week.

Ran Green Monkey Loop with Dad, who was finally able to show me how the first part connects back with the end now. Who knows how long that entrance will be there, though, with all the construction. The loop is only like 4.5 though, so I tacked on the rest on the bike path. Timed one of the miles at about 8:10, even though it felt a lot slower.

got dropped off at the Vons shopping center for an errand and ran home. Swung by the track on the way back to put in a 400, just to see where I'm at. I was expecting 80, surprised myself by coming through the 200 in 36, and then hung on ok for a 75. Not bad, considering that I've just been running and running.

Took yesterday off, since I'd gone 2.5 weeks without a day off, and I was going to a Padre game after work anyway. They lost, boo, first time I've ever been to a losing game of theirs. But it was fun anyway, although I got in pretty late last night and only squeezed in about 6 hours of sleep. The rest day, though, outweighed the tiredness, and I took advantage of a foggy, cool day to put in my bigger run at lunch.

Oh man, what a good run... I ran in the afternoon to give myself a little time for some last-minute GRE studying, then stopped by San Eligo Lagoon on the way home from the test and did my run there. I felt really good today, just moving comfortably. I may have still had a little test adrenaline in me (is it nerdy that I get that? yes, oh well) because I was really expecting to be more tired on the run. But I'm not complaining, today's run puts me at just below 60 on a 6-day week, which is great, because that means that next week shouldn't be a big problem to hit 65, which is my peak summer mileage goal. I'm amazed at how well this summer has been going in that regard--without pushing extraordinarily hard, I've done bigger weeks earlier than ever before. Let's hope that works out for the better.

Wow, something caught up to me and I was super-tired last night, and woke up at 9 today even though I went to bed before 11. Since I was doing a long run today, I had to eat before heading out, which means I didn't get running until almost 10, and by then it was pretty hot. I ran to TP state beach from the house and headed north from there, because I figured it would be pretty boring to drive there and run all the way to Cardiff or however far it would have to be. It wasn't a bad run, I went there via the bike path and came back up El Camino and Townsgate. I started getting a little warm just over halfway through and doused my head in the beach showers before heading back up. Most of the run was ok and at a decent pace, but heading back up from Carmel Valley Rd back to my house was just one big, long uphill for like 2 or more miles, and the pace sagged a lot there. I don't think I've ever been out that long, time wise--I've done 14 once as part of a half-marathon road race, but that obviously went a lot faster since it was flat and a race. I got home and grabber some food and water, then basically just layed on the floor for a good 20-30 minutes, too tired to move. A good start to hopefull a good week, though.

messed up my watch but it was about 7 miles at just over 8 minute pace. Biked down to the bike path (to run? a bit ironic, I guess) and ran east through Palacio, then back and out along the beginning of Green Monkey Loop. After strides I took the bike out along the bike path, not too far though, because I was getting hungry. The bike home uphill wasn't totally fun, but oh well.
put in the now-standard murphy canyon loop around 11:20. last week of work!

Just wandered around today, trying not to look at my watch for as long as possible. Ended up exploring a little trail over near Laura's old house (by High Bluff), although I didn't go very far because I was a little spooked to go somewhere so isolated all by myself. Managed to not look at the time until about 55 minutes, which was just perfect for me to be wandering back home.

oh man, for whatever reason, I really felt good this afternoon. My plans to run at the beach got delayed a little bit, but I ended up getting there around 6:45, and it was nice and cool. Just moving really smoothly, not pushing or anything, but not tired either, just really comfortable.

morning run at lunch again. took a body fat test at the fitness center, and came in at 19.4%. I guess that's kind of high for a distance runner, but it's lower than I can ever remember, although I don't do it with any regularity. I know those things are super-inaccurate, but it's cool to know that it's down like 2% from the beginning of the summer (we had to take a fitness test to use the place at work).
another really good feeling run, don't know what made me come around like this, but I'm really glad about it. maybe it was the epsom salt bath on monday, I don't know. but i'm not complaining.

This one was probably more like 2.5, but I've gone over my posted mileage on some other runs, and so I figure it's close enough to put it as 3 and make it to my summer goal of 65 mpw, rather than a wimpy 64.5. I normally would have just done 8 all at once on a Saturday, but I left at 8:00 this morning for Runners' Workshop camp up in Idyllwild, where I will be a counselor for the Torrey Pines girls, after being a camper for 4 years in high school. So today through Thursday will be interesting as far as squeezing mileage in; I'm sort of at the mercy of the camp schedule, but it should be a productive week nonetheless up at 5500 ft. elevation.

headed out with another counselor for a little extra mileage today... it is hilly absolutely everywhere here. It's fun though, to be around so many runners and a bunch of other very good collegiate runners who are also into mileage

Led the C group on intervals, which consisted of a warmup lap along the 1000m loop, 3 intervals, and a cooldown lap. The pace for me, obviously, was not interval pace, as we came through in about 5:45 for most of them. There was one pretty fatty three-staged hill along the loop though, and with the altitude I was sucking air a little bit.

ok, PM is really a misnomer here, because I headed out with Becky, Katie and Olga (some of the other counselors) right after breakfast, around 10:15. Runs here at camp are at around 6:45, so really this was like... I don't know, part of the first run? Anyway, for lack of better options, we headed out Ernie Mac again, but I got a little farther this time since I was running with the other counselors. Felt good to get moving at a normal pace, although I'm still having a bit of difficulty with the hills and the altitude... there is nowhere flat to run here whatsoever.

another one with the C group, out along the Ernie Maxwell trail. It's all uphill out, all downhill back, which is better than the reverse, I guess. We went for about 35 minutes, but I don't want to call it much more than 4 miles, even with the downhill

I switched up groups today, running a point-to-point with the B group, out to some park down the hill, which both A and B did today. This was a good run for many reasons... one, it was really pretty, two, I ran long enough and at a decent enough pace to just run once today, and three, it was cumulatively downhill. We even found a flat, grassy field to do strides on, even though I got smoked by the high schoolers. But hey, I'm never gonna run a 2:13 or a 4:56 (unless it's a 1500, hah) so I won't expect too much.

Kelsey (a counselor) needed an afternoon buddy, and I could use the mileage, so I obliged, even though this morning was a pretty solid effort. I'm going to have a lot of unecessary doubles this week, but I don't think it matters, I feel like I'm getting in shape up here.

Got the B group again today, this time up in front helping lead a fartlek. We were back to Ernie Mac, which made the first three (of 5) pickups pretty intense. We were doing about 3 minutes on, 2 minutes off, and I let two girls in front of me to set whatever pace was best for them. It was pretty managable for me, not a full tempo effort, but nice to be working a little harder. Coming back we were at about 45 minutes, so I added on some loops around camp with Becky...I know we're here for the kids this week, but I obviously still have my own running on my mind

got home at 12:30 and after sitting around, quasi-unpacking, I went for my real run around 2 pm. I started off feeling amazing, since I can actually breathe here, and headed toward the bike path for some relative flatness. Timed the first mile on the path at 7:45, and got to the end, which is just over two miles, in just under 16. Nothing spectacular, but probably faster than I've run all summer... I just really had trouble getting going after (or sometimes during) work. I kept the rest of the run at almost the same pace, although with a little more effort over the last half, adding on through Palacio and other parts of the neighborhood. Anyway, with all the squeezing in runs, I managed to put in 44 miles through today, so looks like I should be able to make it to 65 again this week. Back to campus on Sunday, wow.

ok, so the morning run really wasn't, but hey two miles is two miles. Today was the last day of camp and everyone was leaving right after breakfast, so there wasn't any real running, just a little wrap-up thing.

I wanted to go to the beach to run but didn't have a car this morning, so I just started running there without really a plan for route or distance. Went over Del Mar Heights Rd, down to the state beach through the Torrey Pines extension by Del Mar Hills elementary. Ran north up along the cliffs for a while, up to like 8th or 9th st., then turned around and came back the same way. On the way back, I detoured over to the track and ran 800m at 6 minute pace. I was thinking I'd do a hard mile at first but I was still sleepy and not in the mood to actually push myself.

Ran Broken Hills all the way south to UCSD, hitting Guy Fleming on the way down. Logic would have normally told me to run north this morning and let my legs recover on the flat a bit, but I actually like Broken Hills, and I figure that it wouldn't kill me to run one more hill before I leave tomorrow and run essentially flat runs for the last nine months of my running career. I really can't believe that I'm coming up on my senior season, and that practice starts Monday. I'm pumped to see the team and everyone again though. Today wraps up my second 65 mile week, and I'm feeling good, although this week was a little weird as far as doubles and squeezing miles in at camp. Well worth it though.

We have new instructions for pace this year, which is to average 1:15 per mile slower than race pace, but the average can include a slightly slow 1/2 mile or so in the beginning, and should also inlude a 1-2 mile harder effort at the end of every run (excluding, of course, days of extreme fatigue, etc.). So today, of course, the typical "omigoodness we're all back together" effect was compounded by this new instruction, and we ended up going a little ridiculous. No real harm done, I suppose, as long as things settle down.
First day back on campus. I took yesterday off for travel and general recovery. This morning was just an easy Longfellow loop with half a dozen girls or so.
The groups split up today, so we weren't running as hard. Wandered around the Downtown Crossing/Aquarium area, showing the freshmen around a bit.

Grass loops. Pretty solid pacing, at least according to the new instructions. Set off with a sizeable group that slowly dwindles to Adrieene, Jessie and I, then just Jessie and I for all 9 loops. 7:06, 6:56, 6:57, 6:48, 6:44, 6:45, 6:45, 6:36, 6:08
The team drove out to Franklin Park this afternoon, so we ran the course and then wandered around the golf-course side for a while.

12:03 for the 3200, with a 6:05 first mile, I think, and must be 5:58 for the second. I was 5th on the team again, like last year, but about 30 seconds faster and with way more people within 10 seconds either way of me. This bodes well for the season, I think. In any case, I'm happy that with today, not only with the pacing and the time, but the effort level that it required was much more comfortable than I remember last year being. I'm definitely pumped about today both for myself and everyone else. Cross our fingers for minimal injuries.
Drove out to Newton by myself just because I've got the time now to avoid bridge loops, and got in some miles on the dirt along the Comm Ave. median.

Jamaica Pond, the grass way. My group now is Rachel, Adrienne, Jacqui and Karen, but Karen was in the pool. We started off easy, but got moving a bit once we got past Longwood, trying to settle into the pace window. Adrienne and Jacqui dropped back at that point, and Rachel and I just kept lightly pushing the whole way. Everything was pretty smooth, we actually got moving pretty fast around the pond (10:30, about 7:30 pace), but coming back we both started falling apart a bit just before the BU bridge.
extras: lifting... gosh I was beat today. Maybe the 6am wakeup...

comments: For whatever reason I woke up at 6:30 this morning, no alarm or anything, and after not being able to fall back asleep, I went out for a run around 7:15, since we have an early afternoon practice anyway. Nothing special, but I did see a Colby alum out along the river.

took a nap in the late afternoon and woke up in my running clothes around 5:15, then headed out for a casual, somewhat groggy 4 miles. Nothing special except that over by the Royal Sonesta, I ran by a dead fish just sort of chillin in the grass. It wasn't rotting or anything, and it looked more like a fish tank display fish than any food fish, so it was really random.

we're on a weird schedule to accomodate orientation, so the official practice was in the morning. Ran with the whole group today, feeling pretty tight/tired at the start but I guess we got moving. The run was probably just under 6.5, which puts us right in the target 7:35 pace.

grass loops. Another morning practice and I was really tired today... more sleepy than anything though, but I just didn't really have the energy to focus on staying with my group. About halfway through, though, I woke up a bit and I guess in the end pace and everything was pretty much ideal, starting off with a 7:02, dropping to low 6:40s for a bit, then down to 6:30, 6:20 and two 6:11s to finish off.

we went out to the arboretum today, had the whole group together. We kept it a little more relaxed today, just casual pace until the end where we picked it up slightly. Today wraps up what I officially consider my summer miles, and I am pleased with the total. At the beginning of the summer I was thinking 650 miles, but I topped that pretty easily while feeling really strong, and had a good time trial last week. Just gotta stay on top of things and hope this semester doesn't get to me too much, and I think this will be a pretty good last season.

A decent day overall; definitely another excellent day, teamwise. The group plan was to have Rachel lead the first mile then stay together coming down Bearcage , hitting 6:15 and 12:50, finishing up around 19:45. We did ok for the first bit, came through as a group in 6:19, passed up Finn and Hana just before the hill, and managed to keep together up through Bearcage, but we separated going up instead of coming down. Mile two was Maria, then Jacqui, then me in 12:52, and feeling pretty strong the whole way. I had the two of them in sight most of the way, losing them once in a while in the wilderness, but pretty soon I was losing focus and getting tired, and I lost them. Just before getting onto the field, though, Rachel came by me and I couldn't manage to go with her, ending up 4th.

started off on my own, wandering toward a bridge loop until I decided to turn around and run Fresh Pond. On my way, though, I crossed paths with Maria, so we went together, which probably turned out to be a good thing because I was really dogging it until we got together. We went out to Fresh Pond and came back through Harvard Square, then I added on a bit at the end. It was probably close to 10.5, but I'll write it as 10.
Fresh Pond again, this time back via Concord for variety. Felt pretty good, I got a nap in between runs today. Small bathroom break at Harvard Sq, but other than that we were moving ok

arboretum. The group for now is Finn, Maria, Adrienne, Rachel, Jackie and myself, and we were joined today by Martha, who drove one of the vans. Time went by quickly today, we even figured out a different way to cross between the two halves, and the pace felt good all the way.
Franklin Park. Wandered around a bit then finished up with the course backwards. Finished up strong.
grass loops... started off slow (7:00), then dropped gradually down to finish up in 6:07 for 8 loops.

easy run, i'm feeling a little rundown. I also got a tape job from the trainers before the run, for my heels (I've been having a little pain related to plantar fasciitis for the last couple of weeks), which was way too tight and ripped a whole bunch of blisters in my feet.
cooled off a lot from the weekend, a nice, steady paced run.

extras: lifted in the morning since I have a 7pm meeting after practice today

comments: Great run today. I took the morning off because I woke up with a HR of 46, which is high for me, although I think it was more due to the fact that the heat in the dorm seemed to have been on. I'll run tomorrow instead. I also had tight IT bands and a sore outer knee throughout the day, reminiscent of the troubles I was having over IAP. I took some Motrin, then heated and foamrolled right before practice, and didn't have any pain during the actual run though. Anyway, this afternoon was cool and perfect for running, especially a hard workout. Fartlek at the arboretum with Jacqui, Rachel, Adrienne and Finn. Warmed up 2 mi then started out to do what ended up being abotu 4.5 mi of 600-800m pickups. I took the first one and then after that there was a lot of good rotating and different people leading. I was able to hang with pretty much all of them, dropping back a bit on a long uphill one that Rachel led, although I did fine on the last few after that. Good, productive run overall.
franklin park
did yesterday's run today
grass loops, 8 loops, mostly around the 6:50 range, dropping to 6:12 to finish off

Pleased with the race overall. In retrospect, after talking to Rachel, I probably could have and should have stuck with the group a little longer. I got distracted by one of the front spikes on my right shoe, which for some weird reason I could feel digging into my big toe. It didn't hurt or anything, but I fell back just about the time I started feeling it, so I think I lost focus. Anyway, on the upside, I think this was the best third mile of any of my races so far. I spent most of it just ahead of a runner from Bates and while there were brief moments of "what if she passes me?" for the most part I stayed aggressive and confident and wound up with a decent kick to maintain my place. I also did pretty well on the hills, which in past years have really gotten the best of me. Teamwise is was a great day, everything went according to plan as we sat just behind Keene State for the entire first half of the race, only to pick them (and Bates) off in the woods for a 25-49-61 win.
race_name: vs. Bates & Keene State, Twin Brook Park

comments: Walden Pond with Finn and Adrienne

extras: new lifting... mostly the same but higher weight, less reps

comments: Went to see the podiatrist today and got some arch supports, which I'm supposed to wear all the time. I am supposed to go back if I don't feel better in two weeks, so we'll see.

Flew out to Ohio to run Pre-Nationals tomorrow, so after a long 10 hours of travel (which started at 5am), we drove over to the course to check it out. It's mostly flat but with long grass, and do to it not being marked yet, we had to backtrack a bit and went a little long. I'm super tired and stiff from both flying and not sleeping much the last two nights, but I don't think it'll matter for tomorrow.

Whoa... incredible day teamwise! Over the course of the next day this has all slowly hit me... 4th place behind three nationally ranked teams and 7 points out of third is quite an accomplishment, and it's certainly realistic to plan on being back in November... So overnight rain wet down the course a bit but it didn't feel very slow or muddy, and I was feeling good on the warmup, much better than yesterday coming off of the plane. We got out well as a team with plenty of space across the first stretch, then settled in well over the first 800. At the mile mark, Hana, Rachel, Finn, Jen, Jacqui and I were all still rolling together, and someone called out 6:35 or so. On that flat course, though, there was no way that was right, and I also heard another coach a few meters back yelling something like "6:15 for the full mile," or something like that, so I'm pretty sure splits are off. Anyway, we sped up in response to hearing that, but about half a mile past that point, I couldn't stay with the pack as they moved up. I'm not sure what it was, if it was just the lack of sleep over the week or if everyone else was just running amazingly. From there on I was on my own; there were points when I could see our runners but I never caught back up. All in all though, even though I fell back, I think it was a good race--I stayed in it mentally and I didn't let anyone pass me without a significant effort. In the end, I had a solid kick from about 800m out (partially inspired by the thought that I might not earn dinner, hah), and even managed to get back up on a few women who passed me, including three in the final stretch, and I'm happy with the way it all went.
I am so beat... not the biggest fan of waking up at 5am every other day, with a race in between.

Since the top 7 aren't racing this weekend I felt like going out for a little longer today, and since I haven't been past Elliot since at least last track season, I figured a bridge loop wouldn't be too boring. Did Science Museum-Arsenal at a pretty relaxed pace about 8:10. I was (surprisingly) feeling fine physically, except that my left quad was all sore, and some little things (arches, knees) were a little achy. I guess I'm just getting old.

Arboretum. My quad is still sore although better after the AM run, and my arches/ankles are still hurting a bit on the twisty trails, I think from the arch supports. Hopefully that will go away as I get used to them though, the downhills just sucked a lot today.
After going to bed at 8:30 last night, I woke up almost exactly 12 hours later and was feeling good on today's first run, although a little achy

Today was a "make your own workout day." The rules were that we had to run 6000m of total intervals in 22:35, with 33:25 to finish the entire workout. The interval length, pace and rest were all up to us. Rachel and I (the groups are shifting a bit) decided on 1200, 2x1600, 2x800 in 4:30 (2:30 rest), 6:05 (3:15), 6:05 (3:15), 2:55 (1:50), 2:55. I was feeling a little tight in the lower leg from my arch supports, but we ended up hitting every time either dead on or under, which was awesome. I definitely feel like I am at least in as good of shape as the end of last track season, if not in better shape than ever. Certainly one of my best workouts: 4:27 (2:21), 6:02 (3:12), 6:01 (3:25), 2:54 (1:55), 2:55.
franklin park, mostly by myself since Rachel has class until 5
grass loops
a decently paced run through Comm Ave and Beacon. Went a little longer than we intended, but it was ok since most of the group isn't racing tomorrow

Top 7 had this week off, but we went out to the meet with everyone to do a fartlek on the Mt. Holyoke course, which is going to be the NEWMAC course. The course is basically a 1.5 mile loop that we do twice, mostly flat but with two short, steep uphills and one short, really steep downhill. There are also a lot of twists and turns and unsure footing... should be a good championship meet. Anyway, Rachel and I ended up running just over 4 loops, with 6x600m fartleks thrown in. I lost Rachel on the last two a bit, my legs were just a little heavy today.

was going to take today off but I'm taking tomorrow off for Yom Kippur, and I was actually feeling pretty energetic in the late afternoon. Took off around 1pm in the rain, rain around the Fens for a while before heading back down Comm Ave to the Longfellow bridge and around that way
extras: lifting- did legs today and I'll squeeze in upper body tomorrow after I eat something

comments: Workout at Franklin Park: 2xmile (first mile, then the second) with 3:30 rest following each, then 3xwilderness with 2:00 rest. Target times were 6:05-6:08, 6:25-6:28, 3:05-3:08. My group today was Rachel and Adrienne, and starting out we tried to key off of Hana, Jen and Finn, staying 5 second back since their time was 6:00. They went out way too fast, though, and in the end we came through under 6, me being third at 5:58 after easing off a bunch at the end. I was relieved to hear such a fast time, though, because otherwise I would have been wondering why it felt so hard. The second mile was an attempt to find a middle ground between not dropping the pace too much and not going too far under the window, which was accomplished well in 6:21. By the time we got to the wilderness loops, I was feeling pretty flat, which is maybe a result of the Yom Kippur fast (I've never done a hard workout the day after, so I don't know), but I managed to keep it together mostly, running 3:08, 3:09, 3:09. All in all, it was a decent workout and a very encouraging one considering that I was able to run those times without feeling particularly zippy.

aw... I was feeling dead today. Coach mentioned before we left that yesterday was pretty hard so we should go easy today, but I really didn't realize how hard yesterday was until we started moving today. If I could even call it moving. I was struggling just to hang with the others
felt like aquajogging to loosen up a bit... it felt good but it definitely got my heart rate up, which was surprising
grass loops, nothing exciting, mostly casual pacing, until we dropped to 6-flat for the final lap
extras: lifting during the day, to get to my 7pm presentation on time

comments: Long story short, we got an outside box and got pushed to nearly the back of the race coming across the field, which meant we had to fight pretty hard to get up to position by the first mile. From there, things pretty much went according to plan and I was working as hard as I could, coming through the mile in 5:57 and the two-mile in 12:33. After that, though, the extra effort at the start really took its toll, as I faded and struggled to maintain my composure. Even though cumulatively I passed people in every mile, there were definitely a lot of stretches (mostly the uphill ones) where 5, 6, 7 people passed me at a time. I got most of them back on the downhills, which were I think my strongest part of the race, but once it came down to the final 600 I was just getting sucked up and I was struggling to hang on. I mustered up one last burst in the final straightaway, passing two people and finishing with a just barely PR for the course (5 seconds). I think the amount of effort I put in, though was worth closer to 19:30, though, in a cleaner race. More specifically, though, the real issue was that I was pushing hard and being very aggressive, but I was not specfic enough in my commitments. I made it through the second mile of the race by reminding myself that I was obligated to help out my group even when I didn't feel good, and that gave me the motivation enough to try to keep Adrienne in sight, planning for a moment when I would feel good enough to move up to her. That moment never made itself perfectly clear, though, and I spent a good chunk of the race just 5-10m behind her, finishing only 6 seconds back. I think next time, I need to say "ok, I will catch her by this point," and make it concrete. Not a bad day, though, but I've got more.

We have no official practice this week, except for tomorrow's workout and meeting up for lifting Monday and Thursday. I decided that I wanted to do the longer workout in the morning, so I ran out toward the BC reservoir. Thought I was moving pretty slow, but I got out to Chestnut Hill Ave. in 30 min, which I'm pretty sure is just shy of 4 mi. Kept going to the reservoir and went about 1/3 of the way around before turning back. Going around would have made it 9.5, not that the half mile would have killed me, but I just didn't want to go around. Ended up going out in 36 coming back in about 33, of course the mostly downhill helps.

Workout at FP. first mile, 2xwilderness, guys' fifth mile (essentially mile 2, but a little shorter), in 5:58-6:02, 3:06-3:08, then 5:46-5:49, with 3:00 after the mile and 2:00 after each wilderness. The last mile seemed ridiculously fast, but I guess since the guys run 8k, it's actually short of a mile, maybe by 15 seconds? Anyway, I didn't hit the last one at all, I was really just feeling sleepy more than anything. I don't know how to explain it, I'm not physically fatigued, but more than anything I'm just tired and I feel like I'd rather close my eyes than try to keep my head in a workout. I dunno, I'm going to try a new plan-- do less schoolwork. We'll see how long that lasts before I feel guilty or panicked, but whatever. 5:55, 3:06, 3:07, 6:02

Went on my own today, and made today's run a little longer since I didn't do a second morning run this week. felt good to go for a longer run, although I'm probably going to take tomorrow off just to give myself a reasonable shot this Saturday

Don't know if it was the day in the pool Thursday or just a general coming around, but I felt good and fresh on the warmup today and was excited about the race. The course was awesome as well, a really beautiful run through wooded trails, with some decent sized hills but ones that made you feel good for working them, rather than sucking the life out of you. Anyway, got out well, for the first time in any workout or race this season I didn't feel like I was dying to hang on at the start (I'm not the type to warm up quickly). After a bit of jostling about I settled in with Adrienne and Erika in about 25th place, and the three of us came through a quick first mile (they read us 5:36, but the markers were off, still definitely a 5:50-5:55). About a mile and a half in, I moved right up next to Adrienne and Erika, then sort of unintentionally got ahead of them... I was feeling good though and with it being a short race, I decided to keep pressing. The second mile was the hilly one and all through the woods, and I spent most of the time going back and forth with a bunch of Williams girls-- I'd get a good gap on the downhills and they'd come back on the uphills. In the end, though, I came out ahead of about half of them, and just sort of hung on as much as I could have in the last half mile. Solid race overall, I held my own on a hilly course and even earned a candy bar by winning the "smallest deviation and most people passed from mile 1 to finish" contest.

Betsy was in town visiting, so we met up and ran to Fresh Pond. We were moving at a comfortable but decent pace, probably faster than Betsy intended, but it felt good. Got back to campus and I added on around a grass loop plus some to make it 11

Footbridge/Sci museum with my new group, Adrienne, Erika and Jessie. Started off easy, then got moving a bit toward Western on the way out. Somewhere near the BU bridge, though, coming back (so only halfway through), we started really moving fast... I don't know exactly how or why it happened but we definitely had to be dropping something in the 7:15 range. I fell back a bit but was able to catch up at lights and stops and such, but I definitely would not have gone that fast on my own.

2x1600, 2x800, with 300 rest after the 1600's and 200 between the 8's. Window: 5:55-5:59, 2:50-2:53. I was probably not properly intimidated by this workout, as the times were pretty fast and the rest was certainly short. I managed it ok regardless, though, just in the window on all of them. It wasn't super smooth or easy, but I got the job done. The difficulties might have come from the erratic sleep I'm getting right now (trying to make next week better) and the late lunch, but I'm happy with it anyway. Coach certainly was... we got a glowing email tonight. 5:56, 5:59, 2:50, 2:53

missed the vans to the arboretum since I've been (and I'm going to be) in lab all the time this week, so I ran with Coach H, to Fresh Pond and back through Harvard Sq. Never really picked up the pace, but I wasn't feeling as crappy as I thought I might after yesterday
grass loops. Mostly a bunch of high 6:40's, finishing with a 6:38, 6:17

Tempo at FP. Warmed up 2 miles then alternated between 2:00 and 3:00 tempos. It was pouring rain by the 3rd pickup, which was ok, but I was exhausted from the whole week, schoolwise, and things didn't really go amazingly, but it was fine
no meet this weekend so I just went along the river, watching the Head of the Charles. It's cooling off now, about mid-forties when I went this morning, but nice.
just wandered around today, science museum to boston common, then back over the BU bridge to watch the boats again. another cool day

the Jamaica Pond and back, not all the way around though. Solid run today, started off with Jessie, Erika, Adrienne and Coach H, at a decent pace but Jessie and Erika turned back earlier. The rest of us turned at 32 minutes, and got moving nicely on the way back (maybe because it was downhill?). I felt really great though, coming back across Beacon and then Comm Ave. When we got over the BU bridge, though, Adrienne pulled away for an agressive end-of-run pickup, but I didn't feel like racing so I just kept it fast, but not strenuous. I'll be pretty pumped if I feel this way come Saturday.
extras: lifting. A good day in the weight room too.

comments: Wilderness Pizza Relays. We were paired off with partners of equal ability, and each pair was handicapped so the fastest teams started last. We had to do four wilderness loops apiece for the race, then the top 8 added on two more. After some shuffling due to absentees, I ended up being paired with Adrienne, 58 seconds back. We were pretty much right in the middle, with Maria and Finn starting something like 30 seconds behind us. Adrienne ran first and tagged off to me with no changes in position. I ran a comfortable first loop, still all alone, in 3:01. We were gaining ground the whole time and by the third loop Adrienne had passed Gwen and was moving up on Jessie. Gwen was paired with Labuz and Jessie with Erika, and it was the three of us together on that loop. I moved away from Liz pretty quickly, then got Erika on the beginning of the grassy, downhill stretch. I ran scared the rest of the interval but managed to put a good gap on her, which Adrienne lengthened. On the last interval it was me and Erika, although I got off the line first, and I figured if I got a good enough gap before she got going, it would look more discouraging. So I started off hard and just tried to hang on, and managed to come in first. After the race, we had two more to do, which sucked more than anything... we were all tired and with no motivation, and everyone dropped a good 15-20 seconds per interval. 3:01, 3:00, 2:58, 3:04, 3:20, 3:21 ¡Ganamos una pizza!
last trip to the arboretum... just ran around pretty easily, recovering from yesterday. the hills were a little rough today
mm... chilly today. still feeling tuesday a little
extras: lifting, kept it a little lighter to not be sore for saturday.

comments: drove out to Mt. Holyoke and ran the course, same as the one I did a fartlek on a few weeks back. seems ok, but tomorrow is supposed to be rainy and ugly

A dissapointing repeat of a 4th place finish, this year behind Coast Guard, Wellesley and Smith. The whole thing, really, came down to one mistake of letting the race get out across the field and having our whole team funneled back to about 40th place before the first turn, after which point there was nowhere to pass anyone until after the first mile or so. We (at least I) realized the mistake pretty much immediately, when we watched runners from teams that weren't even in contention flooding in in front of us, but even then it was practically too late to do anything. Not to mention that the course was flooded with at least 2 inches of rain and/or 4 inches of mud , limiting the places where we could run. I don't know how everyone else felt, but I basically just had the sensation that I truly was pushing myself as hard as I could the whole time, but with no result; I just simply wasn't passing anyone. Maybe I took pizza relays too seriously, maybe I am getting a little sick or maybe today just wasn't going to be my day. It's a really frustrating thing, to think that all of our talent couldn't come to fruitition on account of a silly strategical mistake--I don't know how we messed that up; if it was everyone's fault for not knowing, my fault for not passing on my knowledge of crowded starts from high school, or Coach Taylor's fault for not explicitly telling us that. I think, really, the responsibility lies a little bit in every body, not in a blame sense but just in the sense that everyone should be responsible for knowing and sharing always. Anyway, I am sad that Jessie and I didn't get to walk up and accept the trophy on behalf of the team and sad that this was the only opportunity and it's over. I know there's still regionals and the chance for 7 of us to represent MIT and prove how good we are, hopefully to the nation. But it's still too bad for everyone who finished their season today, and I feel badly that they don't get to feel like they raced in the race where we proved we were the best in our conference.
Took yesterday off because I wasn't feeling well, and I was still groggy and out of it today, so I ran on my own, shorter and slower.
another easy day, although I'm feeling a bit better. missed the fartlek, but I'm not heartbroken

feeling a lot better today, and fresh as well from all the rest. I'm still congested but I don't have the drowsy/heart racing feeling, so I'm pretty sure I'm over it. Ran to the Commons via Science Museum with the group, although they were running a little slower from yesterday's workout and I was feelign a little antsy. We got moving ok toward the end, then when we got back, I added on a grass loop at a little faster pace just to feel a little better.
7 grass loops... they felt fast since it was pretty cool and dark today. I ran with a longsleeve but probably didn't need it. Ended with 6:46, 6:29, so nothing specatular

Pizza Relays 2, this time it was just 2 teams of 4xmile, on account of a lot of people being either done with their season or forcibly done with their season (the injury bug has hit hard). The race was pretty much over after the second leg. I got the baton for the third leg about 20 seconds up, and pretty much just pushed hard enough so that I was running what felt even, finishing in 5:47. After the relay, we ran 2x800 and 4x400, windows of 2:49-2:51, 81-83, with 200 rest after the 800's and 100 between 4's. The 800's were manageable; I led the first in 2:49, then followed for the next in 2:51. The 400's were a little tougher for me, but I managed to hit every one, 82, 82, 83, 82. Just like all of my workouts lately (and races too, I guess) nothing was smooth, easy, or amazing feeling, but I have the focus to stick with it, so I guess that's all I need for next weekend.

ran out Mass Ave, turned on Columbus and followed the Southwest Corridor out until I hit 28 minutes. Wasn't moving very fast, definitely feeling yesterday a little. Ran exactly 8 min pace the whole way, based on the route I measured on GMaps.

Footbridge-Harvard with Jessie, Liz, and Adrienne. Started off really easy but picked it up nicely on the way back, then picked it up again a little more from the far end of the bridge to bring the average down to about 7:35, right where it should be. Today felt easy and nice, which is a great way to start off this peak.

3x800, 3x400, with the same goal times and rest as last week. The first 800 felt a little junky, but I got in a decent rhythm by the third one (which I led) and things were going pretty smoothly. Switching to the 400's didn't present too much of a shock, although I fell off a bit on the 2nd. Coach gave us extra rest before the last one, to make sure our legs felt ok, which helped a whole bunch. I finished off leading the final one in what was the easiest interval of the night, even though it was the fastest. Looking back on last year's workouts I'm amazed at how much faster this is going, and I plan on translating this into Saturday's race. 2:51, 2:50, 2:50, 81, 84, 79

The day off before the Williams meet seemed to do me good, so I opted to pool run today. Tomorrow is a pre-meet on the Springfield course, and then I'm ready to kick ass at my last cross country meet ever. I'm feeling primed and ready to go.

A long 6, but yesterday was short. Lots of rain and puddles today, and we didn't go until 5, so it was dark. other than that, a pretty non-eventful river-longfellow loop, even though I was hoping to go somewhere more creative
Jogged the course. It's hillier than I remember, but otherwise the weather looks great for tomorrow

Well I gave it all I had, but for whatever reason, the legs weren't there. Maybe it was the type of course; the short, steep hills always wear me out, or maybe we should have had a more conservative first mile, I don't know.
