All I know is that I can honestly say that I kept pushing it in my mind--there were moments of indecision and relaxation but every time I snapped out of it and kept pressing, but by the last half of the race I couldn't stop myself from slowing. I went out well with Adrienne for the first 2000 meters or so, coming through the mile in 6:00 and about 50th place, exactly where I wanted to be. After that... I don't know why things didn't continue along as planned. I finished only 3 seconds faster than last year, despite being in much better shape, which is a little frustrating (although maybe last year's course was faster? I was about 15 places higher). Anyway, I don't know. I gave it everything I had that day, and for whatever reason that wasn't my absolute best. I'm waxing poetic, here, but really, I don't know what else to do. I'm not upset about the race, because there's nothing that I can do from here, but I can't honestly say that I'm pleased with the way it went. I can wish, but on the most basic level, I guess I'm satisfied.
race_name: NCAA D3 New England Regional , Springfield , MA

comments: Ugh, last week was a mess. A good week to not have practice, since I spent most of my life in the Pappalardo Lab, but a diet of primarily coffee and donuts didn't do wonders for having a week off. Woke up this morning not intending to run, but I really felt gross and just had to go for a short one to shake things out. I hate to say this, but if we had qualified for Nationals yesterday, I'm not really sure if I could have afforded to leave Thursday, considering I've spent a good 30 hrs in lab since Wednesday and I've only been sleeping about 4-5 hours a night. Maybe everything happens for a reason... after all, I'm a student first, right?

yesterday was another long lab day, so I took off, but I'm not really concerned with getting in any particular mileage this week. Just want to make next week not suck. Ran in the morning around 9 so I could have the day free, since I have a few meetings and then we're leaving for aB's house in Jersey for Thanksgiving! It's getting a little cooler, about 35 today, but I was ok in a long sleeve and gloves, no tights yet.

WHheee Thanksgiving! Yesterday was off since we slept until 11 and then where catching a bus to NYC soon after, but whatever. Woke up at 11 again but this time got in the obligatory "earn your dinner" Thanksgiving run in North Brunswick, NJ with aB, although it was pretty gross and rainy out the whole time. Still feeling a little uncoordinated from the week off

ugh, today was a much nicer day (50's, sunny) but for some reason both aB and I were feeling like crap. Maybe it was because we had to set an alarm and wake up at 10, in order to start the drive back at a reasonable time. I dunno. In any case, my legs felt junky and my breathing was labored, so we took it a little easy and cut the run short. Still got to explore the little wooded lake area near aB's house, and we built in some strides at the end. I love Thanksgiving.

Met up with Jessie to run, now that I'm back to campus. After a little snafu over where we were meeting which involved me crossing the Mass Ave bridge twice, we meet up and ran a JFK loop together. Today was a nice feeling run, I'm not sure how fast we were going but at least I wasn't gasping for air. Parted ways at the Boston side of Mass Ave and I came back and added on a Longfellow warmup loop and a grass loop, and finished up with some strides.
the schedule said off, so...

Today felt a lot better and more coordinated, but I timed what I thought was a pretty quick grass loop at the end and it was only 6:40 or so. Granted, I was alone, and I guess the dark just makes you feel faster.
extras: Timed 400 as a part of "testing", 72 high. Nothing spectacular but a good bit faster than last year, and a tie for my fastest 400 since high school.

comments: forgot a watch but ran Fresh Pond, coming back through Harvard with Labuz

2 mi warmup, 4 mi fartlek (600-800m accelerations), 1mi to finish. Started with Hana and Lauren, but got dropped immediately when we started the pickups. Managed to work myself pretty hard though regardless, with a few setbacks with a lack of focus in the dark. Overall a good one that left me feeling worked.

I took today off because I've really just been working all the time and not sleeping, and by the time practice rolled around I was really zoned out and even dizzy. I felt weird asking for the day off just because I was tired, but Coach didn't even protest. cool, I gues.

Feeling a lot better today. I ended up running on my own, miscommunication, so I just wandered over to Downtown Crossing, back through the Commons. It was nice to run by myself actually, because we were having our cross country banquet later that evening, and I appreciated the time to just reflect to myself about the season and make sure everything was ready.

Today wasn't on the schedule, but it was sunny and this seemed more fun than starting work. Ran over to Bunker Hill. Even though it was 48 minutes, there's no way I could call it 8:00 pace with good conscience, so I'll round down.

Went by myself, since the groups are so thin. In the future though, I think I can go with the 1500 runners, and just do more. Ran out Beacon and turned around at the Reservior. A bunch of my tempos were uphill and I even went up Summit on the way back. Decent day
Reorganized a bit with the paces, so I ran with Dre and Labuz. We went to the Commons, wandered around, headed back and I added on around the BU bridge.
extras: lifting, although I skipped the upper body because I was on a time crunch. 2.009 is getting crazy

comments: Got to practice late and was in a time crunch again to get back to lab, plus I was pretty wired from a midafternoon coffee, so when we got going it was Jessie and I moving pretty quickly. Felt good but I'm really starting to get worn out

Another long day and not enough sleep. I showed up to practice late and exhausted. I was on my own anyway for the fartlek so I just let everyone leave as I sat and just breathed for a while. Finally decided to get changed and head out 20 minutes later, thinking I was going to at least try the fartlek. Instead, I got over to the other side of the river and realized I was a little dizzy and just generally not focused, and I just started walking. It wasn't a concious decision of laziness or anything, I just couldn't keep running. Made my way straight back from there, walking and running, then went up to the track and sat some more. Talked to Coach and he told me to take the next three days off, which I think is a great plan. I don't even want to think about running until I go home... we'll see about the intrasquad.

Finally started up again. 2.009 presentations were last night and I have a life again. The presentation actually went decently well, even though we were working on our product all the way until 5pm, with the presentation at 7:30. Anyhoo, it's over, I'm not getting too much more sleep this week because of grad school apps and stuff, but at least I'm relaxed and life is good. One 15 minute presentation to go and I'm a second semester senior!

Jessie and I both opted out of the fartlek today, me because of my 5 days off and her because she's not feeling well. We both slogged through the run pretty painfully, although the pace ended up being ok
Jamaica Pond on my own. Classes ended yesterday but we still have practice. At least I got to run in the daylight.
To the Fens, then twice around with Labuz. I don't like feeling out of shape, this is lame.

Wasn't expecting much and nothing much happened. I'm glad that as a captain, I got to choose my own events, because otherwise would have been in the 3k, which I think would have been painful. Hana and Jessie were also in the mile and also fried by school, so I was able to hang onto them for the first half, which I think helped a lot. After that I was just tired and not able to focus. The DMR was much of the same, except I got the baton pretty far back, so I didn't have anyone the whole way. Oh well, going home tomorrow morning!

Wasn't expecting much and nothing much happened. I'm glad that as a captain, I got to choose my own events, because otherwise would have been in the 3k, which I think would have been painful. Got home from the airport around 3:15 this afternoon and the only thing I wanted to do was to go to the beach and run. And even though I feel gross and out of shape, I really wanted to run Broken Hills. I figured if I felt really bad at the top I could always just turn back straight away and make it a short one. I felt ok though and decided to run to Scripps hospital. Turned there and hit Guy Fleming on the way down. I felt great after that, took a nice bath and had a good dinner. This is going to be a great winter break.

Ran down to the bike path and started what was supposed to be 4x5:00 tempo, although I really don't feel like I picked it up much. I was beat and couldn't get moving, even though the focus was there (thanks in part to a solid 9pm-9am sleep session last night, in my very own bed). At least the "tempo" served to keep my focus, otherwise I may have dropped to something ridiculous like 9:00 miles. Came back and fell asleep while trying to tan outside.

ugh. being out of shape is no fun, especially at home where every run is hilly. After racing saturday, running up TP sunday, then trying to do a workout yesterday, my legs were fried. Oh, and also, I decided to lift in the morning before today's run, for some unknown reason. Yeah, I don't do my prime thinking on vacation. anyway, slogged through a run today... it was ok and probably decent pace except on the hills. I ran down to the canyon via Biology Trail, though, so there was a pretty significant amount of uphill coming back. oh well, my legs will come around soon.
extras: lifting. wow, what a difference sleep makes. got right back into 5 chins on the first set, after only manage 4 during making and 3 last week. followed up with a strong set of 3, then 2. everything else went as usual

comments: finally, a flat run and a decent feeling run--the two might be correlated. TP state beach north to Lomas Santa Fe. started off easy with my dad. He dropped back and then passed me again when I stopped at the 17th street bathrooms, but then he turned back early so I never caught him. Just kept things easy today, I finally had enough energy in me that I could have pushed a little, but I couldn't convince myself to. Took about 35 heading out, so I guess the downhill plus just a little warming up brought me back at a decent pace. I can't wait until I get back into some mileage, I can't believe I can barely survive 8 miles.

All right, things are coming around. I was able to get moving ok on today's fartlek, although I was fading by the last one. Drove over and parked by San Dieguito Park and warmed up in there, following most of the 1st mile of our home course. Then I headed down to the lagoon trail, starting my fartlek just after getting on the actual path, and did about 4 miles of 800m pickups (5 and a half pickups). Today was hot and I wished I was wearing a sweattie, because my eyes were burning toward the end. Otherwise it was generally a success, especially relative to my other runs. The run was a bit longer than 8, but I've rounded up on a couple so this will even things out.
Today was a bit hectic and I ended up running just an hour after lunch, which made for a kind of sluggish run. things were ok overall, though
extras: only had time for the upper body and ab lifts. I'll try to get legs in tomorrow

comments: Late morning run, followed by brunch at Pacifica, a Saturday tradition at home. Hailey came along with us this time, and despite my groggy, tired, allergic state, we managed to run this at 8:00 pace, a feat that I'm not sure I've managed for a while. I guess I woke up part way in, but the nice thing about it was that it didn't feel like we were pushing or straining. A good run overall, and nice to catch up with old teammates. Also, a good end to a week that I struggled through a bit. I didn't realize until adding up the miles after the run today, but I did manage to get over 50, which is a start. I figure I'll still be building base through the first couple weeks of January, but I really don't think it's a big deal since indoor is a long season.
extras: legs from yesterday, "ice bath" in the ocean

comments: Drove up to Mammoth today. Was intending to take the day off but we got here around 4:30 and I couldn't think of anything else to do until dinner. Ran from the Seasons4, up to Canyon Lodge, then back down around to the Village. Extended the run a bit out Minaret Rd., then came back. The going was a bit slow on the uphills since I was huffing and puffing in the mountain air (my HR shot up to 215 at some point, apparently), but it felt good to get out on the roads. So excited for tomorrow!

Ok, I really can't believe that I can run in shorts, in the dark, in the mountains, in the winter, but it was that warm out. We got back from the slopes around 4:30 and I headed out shortly after that. A little bit of soreness in my left calf when I first took off my boots, but as I got running I felt no effects of the boarding. Ran down toward Meridian Ave and then through the neighboorhoods. A few random pickups just to get moving but I'm going to miss the tempo run. I got two honks of approval tonight, the first accompanied with some (drunken?) cheering and the other with a "You're diehard!" I have to admit, it does make me feel sort of hardcore. :D

I guess I got a little too cocky about the weather... we woke up this morning to 60 mph winds and a bunch of closed lifts (115 mph gusts at the top). It was pretty bad considering we've never been here when they had to close lifts before. After waiting around about 45 min at the lodge, we decided to come home and just buy a half-day ticket, hoping conditions would be better in the afternoon. We got back aroud 10:00, and I contemplating going for a run since we had a few free hours, but I was pretty sleepy and I think I'm catching a cold, so I decided (with much personal angst) to take the day off. I know that I'm not going to get any fitter while I'm up here, but I'm trying, at the least, to stay in the same shape, and tomorrow is probably going to be a day off since we have evening plans. Wow, I don't know if I've ever written that much about NOT running.
extras: got through one set of pushups and one set up abs before I realized that I hurt in a lot of new places and this wasn't going to happen

comments: Well, it was both lucky and unlucky that we woke up to high winds again today. Unlucky because most of the lifts were closed, but lucky because I woke up at 7:15 and was told to go back to bed, and because when I woke up again an hour later, there still wasn't much open and I was able to go for a run. I ran a loop down towards the Village and up to Canyon Lodge, adding on a bunch of side streets. The going was slow since it was snowing and the ground was a little slippery, not to mention the hills. I busted out the YakTraks and had a pretty fun run; I do miss running in the snow afterall, and running counterclockwise I was able to avoid most of the wind. Now hopefully these two days of snow will make for good mountain conditions as well.

Got home from Mammoth this evening, around 7pm. After a long drive I was eager to get out the door, even though it was dark and getting a bit late for a run. Spent the first 15 minutes of the run deciding whether or not to do the scheduled fartlek or push it until tomorrow, mostly because I was beat from the trip and my left leg was also particularly tired from yesterday's AMAZING powder day on the slopes. I was still thinking I wasn't going to do it until 16:00, when I was at the bottom of Glencliff and I just went for it, starting things off with an uphill fartlek. Overall, though, I felt good and I think was running pretty hard; probably still a lot of adrenaline from yesterday and not enough time to really get sore, although on the last (5th) one, I definitely felt really fatigued and cut it at 2:00. 28:05 of effort in total.

TP state beach, south to guy fleming then north to 22nd. Thought about running Broken Hills but I figured I should go easy on my legs a bit. A beautiful day out, warm, sunny and happy. Capped the run off with an "ice bath" in the ocean.

Parked at Fletcher Cove with the 'rents, but as usual ran on my own. Went North along the 101 to G Street in Encinitas. Nothing special, although I do love running by the campgrounds and smelling BBQ, surfwax and sunscreen. I also saw two twins, running, dressed in the same running outfit. It was trippy. I can't believe break is almost over

Finished 2006 on an up note. Got a hold of Deanna yesterday and was able to convince her to run long with me today. I think this is the longest run I've done since probably late October, given all the tapering and then the setbacks I had. Met up with her in the early afternoon and we ran PQ canyon, which was nice because I'd been wanting to do it all break. She's not really training for anything and I'm just getting back in shape so we started off pretty easy. Things felt smooth the whole way and we got to 40 minutes pretty quickly. Added on a little more past the 5 mi mark, then turned and headed back. We were running at just around 8:00 pace the whole way, which I don't think I would have done without a partner. Good day and now I can enjoy tonight.

Woke up late today then laid out in the back yard for a bit before finally having some tea and heading out around 2pm. Ran down to the bike path were I started my 4x5:00. Three of them took me to the end and back, so for the fourth I ran back again to the half-mile mark and then turned around. Today felt decent and I was moving, I had 6:20 pace or so for the last half-mile of the last pickup. Happy 2007

Got up early to drop my mom off at work so I could have the car, then stopped at the beach on the way home for my run. Was going to do Broken Hills but the tide was way too high, so I ran up Park Rd, down Beach, then finally up BH. It meant climbed 420 ft. twice, but whatever, there's no hills in Boston. Last run in San Diego as a college student... back tonight on a red-eye. Sigh.

Got back to campus around noon and sat around unpacking and doing nothing for a while until practice. I was a little tired, even though I slept for all of both legs of the flight, and I didn't quite eat right, but today's workout went really well. The sessions got shorter this week, 1:30-2:15 for the pickups, so it was fast and we squeezed in 8 of them. I ran with Karen today, and the two of us took turns leading the fartleks, until I started dropping back on the 6th. I put it together a little bit on the 7th, but Karen got away again on the final one. In any case, we were doing well, and we kept the pace up between intervals, making today pretty successful overall.

Did most of today's run with Jessie and Labuz, starting off with a JFK loop. When we got back to the Mass. Ave bridge I kept going to Longfellow to make it more of a mileage day. Good pace and good conversation... it's so nice to run with people again.

Not bad, not great. I think the fitness is there (or getting there) but today was more of an issue of focus. Karen and I were trading leads every three laps, which worked out really well and made things easy, but when we caught up to Labuz she fought us passing her and it kind of frustrated me and threw me off mentally, and I lost Karen. Went through a bunch of what were probably pretty slow laps (I'll check splits later) until latching back onto Labuz with about a mile to go, and finally passing her for good with five left. I had a decent kick from 800m out.

slept in, had brunch with some of the alums and other track girls, then waited a while for the food to digest before heading out around 2:30. It cooled off a bunch today, back down to about 47, after yesterday's sweltering, record-breaking 69 degree day. I was still in a t-shirt, though, and since I was going to be able to run in all daylight, I headed to the arboretum, going a little bit along the dirt path near the entrance before turning around. I think I'll run this week really similar to last, with the addition of a couple morning runs to put me over 60mpw, and hopefully keep that up through january.

Did a regular run today instead of a workout, as per coach's recommendation since i'm still building mileage. labuz was doing the same so she and I went early. we had to swing by APhi to get her shoes, and after that we just kept going down Mass Ave and turned on Columbus to get on that Southwest Corridor.

Jamaica Pond and around with Labuz and Karen. I don't know why it took us so long because it felt like we were moving, but I've always written this run down as 8.5 miles, so if nothing else I'll just have a little leeway in rounding down.
starting these up again, hopefully it will help

Ugh, horrible workout today. The temperature dropped to the low 30's and the wind picked up, in typical Boston fashion. We had 4 mi of 600m accelerations, but I basically got dropped by Lauren and Karen on the warmup, so I was on my own the whole way. We also picked the wrong direction to go, windwise, so the whole thing was way more effort than it should have been. I was totally not into the workout for the first half, starting from Science Museum to about the BU bridge. Just past there, though, I decided that I really needed to get going if I wanted to get anywhere this season, so I did the best I could focus together and I think I put in a few decent efforts from there to just past Footbridge. 3 minutes slower on this same loop from last week, but with the wind and cold I suppose it's not so bad.
fresh pond, back through harvard square. Moving along pretty well, although Karen was a few steps ahead of me the whole time.
not much happening, although it's definitely getting cold. the plantar is starting to hurt again now that my mileage is up, but I'll try to keep it under control

aB and I lifted in the morning, since we have class until 5 and there was going to be team picture today. my legs were a little wobbly on the run in the evening, probably having something to do with finally doing abs right, but no big deal. just an easy bridge loop with adrienne. pumped for tomorrow's 3k, I want to put some fast ones in to prepare for the steeple

Fell off of Karen's pace again, although I was a bit more focused this week. We were both shooting for 11 flat and came through the mile right on, 5:55. At that point, though, Karen and a bunch of Colby girls moved up and despite truly trying to hang on this time, I couldn't do it and got swallowed by 5 girls, eventually. I kept hearing splits from the Colby coach of 46, 47, when I was going for 44's, but it didn't quite occur to me that on a 200m track a drop of 2-3 seconds was actually pretty bad. Hopefully with a few workouts on the track I'll get the feel for things and snap back into shape quickly.
I was a little sore still from the race (and some wrestling matches) since I took yesterday off, so taking it slow today felt good. nothing else exciting

Coach has us on the track this week since we weren't performing quite up to his expectations, which I was ok with today, considering the 20-degree weather. I know I'm getting spoiled with this January, but still, I wasn't in the mood for a fartlek outside. Plus, I wanted to be on the track to help get my focus back under control and to test if I really was in shape or not. We had a brutal 4x800 (2:00 rest), followed by 5:00, then 8x400. Karen was late and with the ability spread on the team we ended up with 4 people running the same workout by themselves. My goal times were 2:55, then "a pace a couple seconds faster" for the 4's. I had no idea how this would go, based on my last few races, but I decided to just suck it up and give it a shot. Amazingly, enough, things turned out ok. I don't want to oversell the workout, but it was certainly better than any of my races so far and somewhat comprable to an early season outdoor workout last year. We did the workout at BU, to take advantage of their nice wide turns and banked track. I basically spent most of the workout telling myself to keep not only my eyes but my whole head and body looking up, trying to basically "get tall" and keep good form, which I think helped a lot. The 800's went by with relatively little strain, (key word: relatively), and I spent the 5:00 rest wondering why I couldn't do that a few days ago. The 400's were a little rougher by the time I got to the 3rd one, and #4 fell a part slightly. On the 5th one I tried to get back in it, comparing it to the 3rd quarter of a race, but it was the 6th one that really came around. I don't know what it was, I was just thining about the form thing, and suddenly I was moving and Coach Taylor even commented on it as I came around the first lap. After coming through in 84 for that one, I didn't want to slow down and really put my mind to bringing in the last two well. On the very last one, Coach had Lauren wait for me, and I got a little antsy at the start with someone else to run with, but kept it together to still finish well. 2:55, 2:54, 2:54, 2:56; 85, 86, 86, 87, 86, 84, 83, 81
extras: lifting! the workout was supposed to be tomorrow, but we couldn't get to BU then, so we moved the workout and kept the lift today. I'm exhausted

comments: karen and I wandered over through charlestown to the Constitution, then back over to downtown boston and home through the commons and beacon. a little warmer than this morning but still pretty cool
oof cold outside today! something like 8 degrees in the morning...
ended up on my own, couldn't quite get moving enough to run with karen today. still not running too slow, just about 8:00 pace the whole way
fresh pond, around.
out beacon with becky; we turned a little before the resevior so I added a grass loop to make it 8.

3x800, 6x400, 6x200, with rests of 2:00, 1:00 and :45 and 5:00 between sets. The windows were a little vague since they went from Maria to Becky, but I decided to just see how close I could hang with Karen and Lauren, instead of doing the workout on my own like last week. Turned out to be a decent choice and I ended up sticking with them the whole workout, even strong enough to rotate leads. Today was definitely a better workout and somehow actually easier mentally than last week, although I'm definitely beat now and wish I could go to sleep instead of working on my thesis... phew hopefully working hard now will let me ease up spring term and really have a good shot at outdoor 2:52, 2:48, 2:52; 80, 81, 81, 81, 82, 83; 37, 38, 38, 38, 37, 38

not as slow, but still hurting. Went Harvard-Elliot with Lauren and Karen and I was sort of struggling to keep up, then just before Elliot on the way out, my mind wandered for a moment and when I looked up, they had 10 yards on me. I tried to latch back on for a while but it wasn't happening and by Footbridge coming back I had lost sight of them. no big deal, though, just a recovery run
mmm... slow...

extras: steeple drills! just stretching ones... we're starting up with these for a little while

comments: Karen, jen doyle and I all got trapped at the start but 300m in things had settled down a bit and I moved into 3rd. We came through the first couple laps a little behind pace, then Karen moved up ahead of me. I tried my best to run my own race, trying to stay on the planned pace and take things 3 laps at a time. Came through the mile in 5:50 and 5th place, with the next pack ahead of me by about 50 meters. I didn't know how far behind me the next group was but I didn't feel anyone directly on me so I just kept pushing to try to keep it that way. With 300 to go, someone from Bowdoin came up on me but I fought her off and stayed ahead for another lap. On the same spot on the backstretch, though, she pulled ahead again and this time I couldn't go. Then, right at the line, Colby came up on me, sort of a surprise, and my reaction was a bit too slow and I got nipped at the line by half a second. I lost the scoring place which was a bummer, but we won the meet anyway which was awesome.
beautiful day out... ran in shorts today to the arboretum, up to the bonsai hut (although all the trees are inside). relaxed but moving

slept through the morning run but I have plenty of mornings to make it up. surprisingly colder than I thought today, with a bit of wind. Becky and I ran Fresh Pond back through Harvard Sq, then I added on around campus for a bit

5x2:00 at race pace with anywhere between 3:30 and 5:00 in between. Obviously a lot shorter than our last tempo, but it was a lot faster and I was definitely feeling worked. Went with Lauren today, who usually runs faster than I'm comfortable with on normal runs, and the warmup was a little fast. Since it was just the two of us, though, I wanted someone to at least start with, so I went with it. Hung on (barely) for the first 3 pickups, but by the 3rd rest I couldn't stay with her anymore and finished it out alone. Still good work overall.

Ran around 8:00 this morning to get things out of the way so I could go to my robot competition tonight (yup, that's what the cool kids do). Wasn't really awake until the very end of the run and the whole thing was pretty lame and shuffle-y, a very slow run.

A tennis match at BU this afternoon forced us to do our workout at 10AM, which was nice but a little early (not the 10:00 part, but the waking up with enough time to be awake by the workout). We're moving to shorter, faster and more rest: 4x600 (2:00), 4x400 (1:30), 4x200 (1:00) with 5:00 in between. I was a little groggy, but my legs were responding, so the workout went well overall. The 200's were a little off; I just felt like I couldn't pick up my legs at all, so maybe I just didn't eat enough beforehand. After the workout I went back, ate lunch, then passed out for an hour or so, which was much needed. Still feel like I could have a great run on Monday. 2:11, 2:08, 2:06, 2:06; 82, 83, 80, 80, 38, 38, 39, 40

I'll call it a long 5, just to have some rounding room later. Slept in, added up my mileage and realized I was already near 60, so I just went short and pretty easy. It cooled off a lot and the Esplanade was a bit icy, so I just ran to Bunker Hill on my own, although a little slower because of the conditions.
Getting down to the really short pre-meet runs... yay. Jay came with me for an easy out-and-back along the river. Not too cold, but windy

I was in the slow heat, of two heats, which was still going to be fast, considering the teams. Looking at the seeds I thought I had a shot of winning the heat, so I tried to put myself in position early on, waiting in about 4th place. The race went out a little hard, considering my goal of 10:50, but I didn't want to lose contact. I felt strong through the whole thing and basically spent most of my time worrying about staying on position and listening less to splits. I did a decent job, and even though the pace as a whole slowed toward the end, I still managed the PR and to basically reach my goal. I also am confident, though, that I could have run faster in a better-paced race, although I don't know if I'll run anymore flat 3ks in my life. 81, 85, 85, 86 (5:38), 89, 91, 92, 40
race_name: BU, BC, Providence College, UNH, BU

comments: Started out with Karen, Lauren and Hana as my group, with Adrienne and Rachel tagging on for the beginning. I got dropped pratically before we got off campus, though, and just wandered around for my run. Turned out to be a good decision as I bumped into Kevin later who mentioned that the guys cross paths with them and even they had trouble hanging on...

today was supposed to be "quality" run since we have no workout this week. I fell back again (although this time from the milers) because I definitely take a little longer to warm up, especially with the heel, but managed to scrounge a decent paced run out of it.
ran through Central Sq. so I could stop at walgreens on the way back. my plantar is acting up but it felt better after the stop
ran with Rachel for the first part of her run. one of the more decently paced morning runs I've had, since I'm usually asleep and without any motivation to run fast at all

This was really just a tune up meet, although a much more competitive field than ever before. Labuz and I were in the slow heat, although no one was seeded faster than 5:40. Labuz took the lead off the line and I settled into 3rd/4th. There was a good bit of jostling for position for the first 3 laps, I forgot what those shorter races were like. The pace felt slow at that point but I was wary about taking the lead and giving someone time to respond. At the 800 mark, though, I reverted back to my high school style of only caring about the 3rd quarter, and opened up a 5m gap on the field just as we crossed the line. We were a bit off of the target pace for a 5:30, which was what I was shooting for, but tried to push it. My legs were feeling quite unresponsive and I was just hoping that no one would catch back up. Coming around the last 150 I felt someone catching back up to me and put in some semblance of a kick to just barely hang on for the heat win.
extras: 18 wu/12 cd, another mile and a half running to the Coop and back to buy a textbook, hah

comments: on my own today since Karen is on a trip, Lauren has a class, Becky is done, and Hana is too fast for me. hah. ran to Jamaica Pond, around, plus some extra coming back. My legs were a little tired/sore but hopefully that will ease off. Can't decide if I'm going to drop the morning runs or not this week, although I got picked for a jury this morning which big time sucks and cuts into my morning lazy time

2 sets of 4x400: 81, 81, 80, 81, 82, 82, 83, 82. It's a big weird squeezing a workout between an "I just finished crying" warmup and an "I'm about to start crying again" cooldown, but that's what today was. I learned today that this jury is going to last through the middle/end of next week and I'm pretty much flipping out about how I'm going to pass my classes. Maybe I'm overreacting, maybe not, but that's where I'm at. All things considered, though, I'm surprised I hit the workout mostly in the window (78-82), also considering that we were on MIT's track instead of BU. Anyway, tomorrow is a snow day for the trial so on Thursday I'm going to beg the judge to be let off. We'll see.

Today was all icy and snowy and we were required to run inside. Put in 45 minutes on a treadmill set to about 7:30-7:40 pace most of the time, based on this conversion chart . In normal conditions I definitely could have/would have gone longer, but even with the FoodNetwork on at the Zcenter I was getting bored out of my mind. Strides felt a little funny but by the end my legs felt good and it was nice to move fast
pretty much a duplicate of yesterday. I did get excused from the jury though, so now I can start thinking about the race. My season goal was to match my outdoor PR of 18:40, so we'll see what happens
drove up to USM just after lunch, put in some laps around the indoor track with Karen

Decent race today. It was an indoor PR, although my goal time was a little faster, and I was hoping to at least break 19:00. I was right on through just under 2 miles, at which point I started hearing 46's sand 47's for my splits (45's would have been 18:45). I couldn't get back on it though, despite some honest efforts. I was also a little thrown off by the fact that there was no cumulative time clock, and so I could never tell myself "Oh, man, I'm falling to XX pace." Anyway, I was much more disappointed immediately following the race than I am now, and I was even surprised to hear that Coach Taylor thought it was a reasonable race. I suppose considering where I started the season... Plus we took 5th overall, highest finish ever!

Just shaking out the restless feeling... today was the second warm day in a row (45 ish) and I went in shorts and a long sleeve. Today was an excellent run. I didn't have a specific distance in mind, but I did want to go by water, so I ran over Longfellow toward the aquariam. I ended up chasing the wharf around and heading out toward the new ICA. It was such a nice day, and so nice to be by the water, and I stopped just to chill for a little while and take it in, before heading back through South Station and the Commons. :D
beacon, to the reservoir and back with karen and lauren.
Not the smoothest feeling run, but we got through it.

Probably a slightly long 7... ran past Bunker Hill to the USS Consitution (where we may have arranged a tour for tomorrow), then into the North End, back over to the Esplande, and home over the Harvard Bridge. Ran with a bigger group today, Hana, Rachel, aB, and Jennifer

Well, Joshua didn't cop out on us, and we arrive at the Constitution at 6:00 to find that we actually did have a tour waiting. It was actually pretty funny and pretty sweet--we got a private tour, just the 5 of us and 3 of the Navy guys, and we got to go onto all the restricted areas of the boat (like, we literally covered every square inch of the boat, including the surgeon's room and the original keel). After nearly an hour on the boat, we were getting cold and it was nearly 7:00, and the guys were starting to regress into Boot Camp stories, so we decided it would be a good idea to head back. It was definitely quite worth it, even though it took 2 hours to run just over 5 miles, hah.

ugh, messiest run in a while. I ran alone earlier in the day so I could catch a flight to berkeley tonight, and I must have run in the absolute rainiest part of the day. It was a nice run until my foot got soaked in cold water, at which point I probably went progressivly slower and slower while my feet started to feel like rocks, which probably wasn't good for the heel. Cut the run short at that point and headed upstairs to thaw and do my drills

Woke up late and gave Tye a call, who came over and took me on a run. Brenna lives right by the southwest end of campus, and we went from there up the fire road and down some other trail I don't remember the name of. It was a pretty brutal run--it was pretty much all uphill for the first half, until we hit a set of steep rollercoaster hills, followed by an intense downhill, that probably hurt my quads more than any part of the run. It was a pretty sweet run though, because at the top of the fire roads there's an amazing view of Oakland, the Bay, and San Fran, and today was a really clear day. Plus it was warm enough to go in a sportsbra. My plantar was hurting pretty badly the rest of the day though, maybe from the pounding.

The run was a little short, constrained by available time, fullness, soreness and pain. Left about 30 min after a veggie burrito, just because it was the only free hour I'd have. My plantar was hurting a lot toward the last half of the run, and my quads were definitely still sore from yesterday, so things didn't go so well, but hopefully I loosened up a little.
6x3:00 tempo in some ass cold conditions. I don't know how I was so cold.
Hana showed us were "Castle" is, it's actually not far at all, just a little turret (sp?) up in Somerville. After getting there, we just wandered around through Somerville for a while
extras: steeple drills, half lifting... these steeple/lifting days get really long

comments: It's a little late in the week to be building mileage through morning runs, but I just wanted to get back in the habit. Didn't feel like getting all bundled for a short run, so I went to the treadmills down in BC. The good treadmill was taken, though, and about 20 minutes into my run, the other treadmill just kind of copped out, like it just stopped. I hopped on the elliptical for 5 more minutes, but I'll just call it 2.5

Yay, it's warmer again. Ran in shorts and a longsleeve, although I probably could have done without the longsleeve. Nice to get out for a steady run today, and a good finish to the week. I got the mileage up a bit, although with a short Sunday and Monday off (since I was doing grad school visit stuff), the total came out low.

Craig Wildman was offering rides to the Middlesex Fells today, and I had never been, so I took him up on it. Turned out to be only me, him and Sean Nolan, which was kind of funny. We headed out and I told them that they didn't need to wait for me, seeing as how they were a bit faster than me, but Craig said he didn't have anything to train for so he didn't mind. Had a fun time just following them around all the different paths and trails, not looking at my watch ever. It was warm again today, but since it had been so cold for so long, there was still ice all over the trails, which made for some interesting downhills (we definitely all took a spill or two). We did detour out to the road a bit (at which point I was glad we didn't split off, because I would have been so lost), and get some clear running in, but a good chunk of the run was just picking our way through things. We got back to the car around 72 minutes, which was a little shorter than planned but none of us really wanted to deal with the slop anymore, so we ended there. On the way home, I decided that I'd just put in any easy 4 in the afternoon, so get some clear running in and since the morning was not particularly long. When we got back to campus, though, I decided that I didn't want to shower twice, so I just went out for a second run then... kind of a halfway second run and halfway long run. Whatever

the workout was 4 miles of 600-800m pickups, which was pretty close to the milers' 4 miles of 600m pickups, so we combined forces and headed out to the Eliot bridge together. Today felt great... maybe it was the morning run, I dunno. We warmed up just over 1.5 mi and Labuz led the first one, closer to the 600m end. I took the second, another short one, after which it was just me, Jessie and Adrienne running together. Every 2nd or 3rd pickup we'd throw in a long one then swing back to grab Jessie, who would stop at 600m. Today was a really solid workout with Jessie and I pushing each other a lot-- it was one of those days that wasn't necessarily easy, but not so hard that it was out of reach either. We'd run, and one of us would fall off then catch our focus, and so on. I got a total of 7 pickups in with two longer ones, so I'd call it a productive day
just an easy run, trying to do these at a reasonable (aka not asleep) pace so that my afternoon run feels better
Jamaica with Labuz, at an easy but steady pace. I love daylight saving's time!
Another pretty casual run. Me, Labuz, Hana, Adrienne and Jen... Adrienne turned back early and Hana and Jen were feeling pretty beat so we went easy.
Woke up early for a phone interview with a company in Spain, and since it was only 8:45 when I was done and I don't have class until 11, I figured I'd go for a run

The workout was scheduled for tomorrow, but it's supposed to be cold and snowy and since we're outside now, Coach moved the workout up to today. It was raining lightly, but as always, the wind was the biggest factor. Even so, it wasn't that bad. We had 8x800 with 2:30 rest, window of 2:55-3:05. Today went amazingly, even though I've been a little more run-down than usual this week. The first 5 went by no problem, the 6th started to hurt a little but we compensated pretty well, then on the last two I just started getting tired, but nothing that I'm really worried about. Plus, I'm about a week ahead of last week, so that's pretty sweet. 2:53, 2:58, 2:57, 2:57, 2:55, 2:54, 2:57, 3:00
snowy
out beacon past the reservoir (not around, because of the snow)
fartlek... things cooled off and it was a little windy, so today wasn't quite as successful as last week, but I still did pretty well.
took yesterday off to go boarding, last day of the season. we took it easy though, so I'm not really sore today
an easy run to Jamaica Pond (the short way, since the other way is snowy/muddy), nice and sunny, t-shirt and gloves
seems to be warming up a bit, shorts and a longsleeve this morning

I had an all-day lab scheduled in my physiology class today, 8am-7pm, so I decided that waking up at 6 would be practically the same as waking up at 7am and opted to do my run in the morning. It was actually a good choice, because the run was fine (moving a little slow at some points, but not really dragging), and after standing up in a surgery room all day, I was pretty pumped to just come home and take a nap and *not* go for a run.

same as last week, 8x800 with 2:30 rest, but faster windows: 2:53-3:00. It was super windy today and I was beat from standing all day yesterday, so I wasn't expecting much improvement from last week, but Adrienne and I ended up pretty much crushing the workout. I faded a little at the end, like last week, but it was truly a physical thing and not a giving up thing, so I can deal with that. Pretty intense day overall, I'm just kind of sitting around now looking for food, falling asleep... 2:55, 2:51, 2:55, 2:55, 2:58, 2:54, 2:55, 3:00
just a chill run, to the commons with Liz and wandered around a bit. bumped into a Colby alum (I can't remember her name) who we've been seeing around a bit.

I usually go with 2 AM runs a week, but I threw in a third since I took sunday off. Got to sleep in today, my only class was cancelled so I just took things easy, went for a run, then took it easy some more. yay spring break!
took it really slow and easy, intense week coming up.... yay spring break! wrapped up a solid week, 56 miles in six days, sweet

after a long day at the airport and driving around for lunch, we finally made it back to the resort where we're staying around 5pm, then Hana, Maria and I headed out for a run. With the traveling and heat and everything, I kind of dragged ass a bit, but it's no big deal

another hot and miserable run; I had trouble keeping up with anyone today. We ran the NTC bike path I went the whole way out by myself, but coming back I turned a little early so I could latch onto Adrienne who I was able to stick with until the last uphill in. Added on a bit before heading back into the track since we had two hours to kill before we were allowed to go in and lift. Laid out in the sun for a while then went in for lifting. Did some abs too. Considered an afternoon run but no one else was going, the pool was calling (and lazy river) and we have a workout tomorrow.

felt a little better this afternoon, after eating well and resting up, so I put in a decent afternoon run through the trees across from the resort. I think it was good for me to get in a run where I actually felt like I was moving.

Well all the hot and sucking on runs manifested itself on today's workout, too. We had 5x800 and 5x400, with 2:30 rest after the 8's, 1:30 after the 4's, 5:00 between sets. Windows were 2:48-2:54, 78-83, fast but not unreasonable. The first one felt about the same as first one's always do, but I degraded quickly. After a bad second one where I fell off from Adrienne, I led the 3rd back on pace, but fell off again on 4 and 5. I tried to get the 400's going again after some rest but 200m into the first interval I hit a wall again and came through in 89, big time off pace, and quit the workout after that, since it clearly was not a mental thing. It sucks to not finish a workout but I think if I rest up, eat well, and stay out of the sun, I should bounce back ok. 2:51, 2:55, 2:52, 3:08, 3:00; 89--

we got dropped off at N. Bradshaw Rd., which is a dirt road that runs through a large orange grove. We ran at 7:30 instead of 8:30 this morning, which was pleasant at least from a sun/temperature perspective. The run was a little hilly and sandy but still way better than the bike path.
orange groves again, 7:30 again, although we went a slightly different way that was a little sandier
extras: 10-12's, getting a lot more confident with the left lead leg on the barriers, lifting, 20 minutes of really low effort swimming, aka floating in the NTC pool

comments: easy run, then spent a lot of time on stretching, strides

The time doesn't mean much today, since it was ridiculously hot out and all of the distance events were really slow. I'm disappointed, though, in getting beat by one of the Colby girls who was running right off of my shoulder the whole time, and the fact that just in general, I wasn't really racing between the hurdles. Technically, though, my form was pretty solid; I had great water jumps (minus the first one) and decent hurdles, although I wish I had used my left leg more early on because I was getting tired by the end. Given everything, though, it wasn't a horrible race, and there's a lot I know I can fix pretty easily. Today's race hurt a lot afterwards, though, my back was aching pretty badly. It used to be that it would only hurt when I jumped poorly, but that wasn't the case today; I guess I'm getting old. I'm going to ask about not steepling as much until postseason meets.
race_name: Spring Training Trip Meet, NTC, Clermont, FL

comments: felt pretty good today, just rolling along nicely
grass loops

4x800, 4x400, 2:30 rest on the 8's, 1:30 on the 4's, 5:00 between sets. Basically last week's workout but shorter, I guess to keep us fresher. The windows were the same, 2:48-2:54, 78-83, but I actually hit them this week and finished the workout, heh. The 400's were slower than I had hoped but it was really windy on the backstretch, so no big deal 2:52, 2:54, 2:53, 2:53; 83, 82, 83, 85

Mem. Dr. down to Fresh Pond, but we cut it off at Brattle St. The run is like 6.3 according to the gmap pedometer, but I'll round down today. It started snowing fat snowflakes around 4 this afternoon, and the weather wasn't any more pleasant by the time practice started at 5. The most miserable part, though, was the mile over hurdles; I was just way to stiff to do anything cleanly. oh well
BU loop with Rachel then over the Next House to grab my bike

another run that was probably a little longer than I'm writing down, but no big deal. Started off with a big group of Hana, aB, labuz, jen, and adrienne, moving pretty well. about 3-4 miles in, though, labuz and I dropped back a bit, although I still think we were rolling around 7:30 pace.

well, looks like the pre-meet was kind of unecessary, since our meet is cancelled due to weather. we're entered to run at Coast Guard, but the forecast is high of 38 and snow, much like Wednesday, and there's no cover. It went to a vote whether or not we were going, and turns out most of the team has no desire to sit around and get cold for 5 hours, just to run bad times. I could have gone either way, since I was entered in the 5k and not the steeple, so I abstained. Tomorrow's just an on-your-own time trial instead, 1500 over barriers, since the weather should be decent here
wouldn't normally do a morning run on a friday, but I want to make sure I'm ready for the end of the season, and the week's total was looking low. plus I got to sleep in anyway

Meh, may as well write this down as a race review. Solid day but I definitely could have gone faster; I'm not really used to a 1500m steeple. Even split the whole thing, which is nice and pretty much reflected my comfort with the pace. Don't know if I could have held it for another 1500m but I was not too phased when I finished and could have at least run another 800 at that pace. A good confidence booster, and helpful as far as pacing, since this is pretty much my goal pace for later this season. I'm pretty ok with not having gone to Coast Guard today, although apparently it didn't snow. Today was fun, a chunk of people showed up at the same time and we just hung out and timed each other and stretched, sort of like a mini meet. Splits: 91, 3:00, 4:31, 5:39
Fresh Pond plus Sketchy Loop. Left my watch in the locker room but I'm pretty sure it was 11.5 or just over
jamaica (not around) with jessie, rachel, becky, jen and hana.
still pretty beat from yesterday. the run started ok but I got pretty tired at the end.

Crappy day out, about 38 degrees and raining on and off. A big group went with all the prefrosh...we went over to the Fens (via the BU bridge, the Harvard bridge was apparently really windy) . Moving at a pretty decent pace to start, but I slowed at the end to make sure everyone got back ok.
grass loops, pretty slow. it was a late night
BU loop with more prefrosh

Well, the dominating event of this race was certainly the fact that I managed to break my hand (5th metacarpal on the pinky of my left hand) falling over a barrier. Yup, that's right... the irony of hurting myself running is not lost on me. I was running a pretty solid race, came through the first mile a little fast (90sec pace) but that's where the race was going (I was trying to reel in the same Colby runner that beat me on spring break). I fell off a little after there, 95/96s for two laps, but I was still feeling decently strong and tried to give it a decent kick toward the end. Form overall was a little sloppy, though, and I ended up hurdling a lot of the barriers with my left leg forward. Coming up on the last lap, I was getting more tired and more sloppy but trying to pick it up at the same time. The combination of these, I suppose, led to disaster on the first barrier of the last lap, where I clipped a leg (I thought lead, Coach Brooks thought trail), and went down pretty hard... on my face. At that point it was instinct to jump up and try to get back into it, although I have to admit that the next four barriers were pretty much the least pleasant of my life. Anyway, I managed to finish without losing any places or anything, and faster than spring break. Based on my pace and the girl in front of me, I'm guessing I would have been at 11:35 or so, even with the poor hurdling. Don't know why I was so off today, the fitness was fine, I was just a little un-alert. Splits: 89, 2:59 (90), 4:31 (92), 6:04 (93), 7:41 (97 yuck), 9:17 (96), 11:03 (1:46, this is where I fell), 11:52
extras: 2 wu, 1 cd (didn't really want to cool down because I didn't want to go that ling without ice on my hand, just did a grass loop and strides)

comments: Marathon Day... went to watch in the morning then ran a bit along the course in the afternoon. Labuz, Becky, Karen and I basically just ended up running down Beacon anyway, not quite to reservoir. Checked in with the trainers this afternoon and they made me a nifty little exercise splint, which is much smaller and lighter and will prevent me from stinking up my normal one that I got at MGH. I think running and racing will both be totally fine at this point.
extras: lifting, legs and abs. I can't hold anything, so I just did unweighted lunges, hamstring curls, and abs. boo

comments: took yesterday off just to reduce bloodflow to the hand and give my body a rest. It was a crappy day out anyway and I ended up sleeping until 11:30, so I'm fine with that. Ran 3mi on the treadmill this morning, just to gauge the pain (there was virtually none) and make sure I didn't have any problems before going out on the roads.

charlestown with Liz, just us; we went early because i was supposed to have a dr. appt at 6. I didn't end up going because i saw the orthepedic guy earlier in the day, turns out I have two fractures, but both in the finger and both will heal fine.
extras: mile over hurdles, hurdle drills. gotta admit I was a little less agressive on the first lap of mile over hurdles. drills ok

comments: treadmill again, it ws gross out and getting dressed in warm clothes takes me too long
JFK-Harvard
extras: 10-12s, lifting (legs+abs). again, a little nervous on the first couple attempts

comments: Was going to take today off but it was soooo nice out. Got to run without a shirt on, yay. Just a really easy bridge loop, and strides
waaarm day out, hooray!
extras: lifting, legs only, 2 sets. we're tapering. crazy

comments: 3 sets of 4x400, 2:00 between intervals, 6:00 between sets, window 78-83. There was no grace or fluidity to this workout, but I did everything I was supposed to, so that's all I can ask: 81, 82, 81, 81; 79, 80, 79, 80; 81, 82, 81, 79
extras: lifting, legs only, 2 sets. we're tapering. crazy

comments: ran on my own since I had a lab at 4:30. just went along the river and turned back at 20. came back for 800m over hurdles, which was a bit of a mess, then hurdle drills and 10-12s, which got better. I still jump too high to clear the hurdles, and too late on my 10-12s, but there's still some time

NEWMAC Champs, woooohoo! Damn, seriously, it's surreal... Coach kept telling us that "we look good," and apparently Coach Taylor was pretty confident on us winning too, but really, with Wheaton having won all of the last nine years that the conference existed... I just wasn't going to take anything for granted, so I volunteered myself for the double "if necessary." Coach took me up on it, also doubling a lot of people in the 1500/5k, so that we could fill up the 10k. All of it worked out well, as we went 1-2-3-5 in the 10k, and 1-3-4-8 in the 5k. Steeple was first; I was going for the win. And really, in retrospect, I should have had it. The winning time was 11:29, just around my PR, and I'm so much faster this year. The plan was to sit and wait through 800m, since everyone always goes out too hard in a steeple, then get within striking distance by the mile. With 3 laps to go, I was gonna lay it down and try for the win. I made it through the plan through about the mile, at which point I just plain wussed out... argh. I dunno, I started catching Nora Youngs of Smith, and just sort of sat behind her as she slowed down (thus I slowed down too), until I finally managed to pass her with about a lap and half to go. As I started laying down my kick, though (or attempt at one), I started to see the leader in sight and at that point I realized I had so much left in me... if only I'd have gone a lap earlier! Argh! Yeah, I was kicking myself for a long time (still am). The 5k was kind of a "just try to get a few points" thing, and I was significantly less nervous for it. Went out calmly in the back of the first pack, in about 9th. Came through the mile in 6:00. A couple of laps later though, Coach Brooks radioed Taylor and turns out we already had the meet wrapped up, and Coach yelled to me that I could drop out if I wanted, to save my legs. Haha... I think even he knew I wouldn't do that. Next lap, though, he told me to at least relax, which was ok with me, so I stopped trying to gain position and just hold my (barely) scoring place (I was 8th at that point). I think it would have taken a good chunk out of me to try to move up, although I think the next place (and possibly the next two) were within reach. Still came through with a pretty solid time though... Steeple splits: 86, 2:56, 4:27, 6:02, 7:40, 9:20, 10:53, 11:38 5K: 6:00, 12:10, 18:40
race_name: NEWMAC Championships, Coast Guard Academy, CT

comments: just loosening up

2 sets of 4x400, 2:00 rest, 6:00 between sets. basically two-thirds of last week, windows of 78-82. This week though, was much easier (from the get-go, not just because it was shorter), and I felt great despite the dead legs from saturday. I mean, really, I was moving fast, even though I didn't feel fresh and wasn't trying that hard (in a relative sense). 78, 81, 79, 80; 78, 80, 79, 78

Just a chill run, science museum plus a grass loop. back for 800m over hurdles... so turns out keeping your head up is really helpful, hah. Yeah, gotta remember that on Saturday. The drills were a lot cleaner today, though. I finally got the hang of my trail leg (swing the knee all the way through!) and even my left lead leg is improving.
Only 3-4 on the schedule today! Ran via Longfellow to the Public Gardens, then back on the Esplanade. Beautiful day out. Legs were a little tired from all the drills yesterday.

A team second place at D3's!!! AHHH! Ok, well my own race could have been a little better. I was hoping to get top 3, improving on my 5th place seed, but instead I fell (almost literally) to 6th. Started off in the lead, by accident, but just took us through slowly, until people got impatient and passed me on the first water jump. I was doing ok for the first mile, a little back from the pack but still in the game. On the 6th lap, I started picking it up, passing Nora Youngs from Smith, trying to right my mistake from last week. We were both moving at this point, she wasn't going to let me go and I wasn't going to play the waiting game. Then a Williams girl came around both of us, and passed me right before the water barrier. I freaked out a bit when I thought she might be in my landing spot on the jump, and ended jumping way too close from my "bad" foot. Lost a bit of momentum there, at the least, and ended up finishin behind both of them. I wonder if I'm going out too fast...
felt like running... we'll see what my race schedule is like the next two weeks. Jamaica, around the pond.
moving along ok, although i'm still a bit sore/tired. mostly my quads, which I suppose is expected from a steeple
same as last week. a little winder. Ran by myself during my 12-2 lunch so that I could avoid lacrosse practice in the afternoon. It's getting waaarm. 79, 80, 79, 80, 80, 80, 80, 80
ran with Coach H through the Fens, just wandering. Legs a little tight from yesterday; I didn't have time to stretch and I'm not running in the mornings.
yay.... tapering! Haha too bad I can't sleep because I'm barely exercising. oh, yeah, and my thesis...
just trying to freshen up

Well, came through in 6:04, pretty much the same as every race this season, then finished up in 11:39, pretty much the same as every race this season. Not much to say. Came through feeling strong at the start, as always, pushing the front of the unseeded heat with someone from Amherst. Just like every race this season, though, when she made her move, I copped out. There was even a lap in there where I thought to myself "well, I'm happy with the race and the season, I just need to finish," and truly stopped pushing. Tried to bring it back for the last lap of my life but didn't end up with anything spectacular, getting outkicked for 3rd place in the end. Oh well
extras: A cool down that involved a 2 minutes jog, laying down in the grass, and some strides. Can't believe it's over for good.
